title: sdd/adapter-contract-engram/explore
content:
## Exploration: adapter-contract-engram (PRD-1)

### Current State

**Module**: `github.com/agustincastanol/wrapper-mems` (Go 1.26.1)

**Package layout (all under `internal/store`):**
- `record.go` — `CanonicalRecord`, `Origin`, `validateRecord`, `decodeLine`
- `store.go` — `Store` struct, `Open`, `Close`, `Append`, `AppendBatch`, `ReadLive`, `ReadAll`, `Rebuild`
- `index.go` — `IndexEntry`, `index` struct, `loadIndex`, `persist`, `computeFingerprint`
- `errors.go` — `ErrNotFound`, `ErrDeleted`, `ErrLocked`, `ErrSchemaTooNew`, `ErrInvalidRecord`
- `tombstone.go` — `buildTombstone`, `validateTombstone`
- `paths.go` — `memoryFilename`, `indexFilename`, `schemaFilename`, `lockFilename`
- `ulid.go` — `ulidSource` interface + `defaultULIDSource`
- `lock.go`, `rebuild.go`, `recover.go` — advisory lock, index rebuild, crash recovery

**Existing `CanonicalRecord` (record.go):**
```go
type CanonicalRecord struct {
    CanonicalID   string   `json:"canonical_id"`
    LineULID      string   `json:"line_ulid"`
    SchemaVersion int      `json:"schema_version"`
    Kind          string   `json:"kind"`           // "observation"|"session_summary"|"relation"
    Revision      int      `json:"revision"`
    Supersedes    string   `json:"supersedes"`
    Deleted       bool     `json:"deleted"`
    Title         string   `json:"title"`
    Content       string   `json:"content"`
    ContentFormat string   `json:"content_format"`
    Origin        Origin   `json:"origin"`
    CreatedAt     string   `json:"created_at"`     // RFC3339Nano string, NOT time.Time
    UpdatedAt     string   `json:"updated_at"`     // RFC3339Nano string, NOT time.Time
    Tags          []string `json:"tags"`
    TopicKey      string   `json:"topic_key"`
    Type          string   `json:"type"`
}

type Origin struct {
    Provider   string `json:"provider"`
    ProviderID string `json:"provider_id"`
    Author     string `json:"author"`
    SessionID  string `json:"session_id"`
}
```

**Key divergence from PRD-1 §4 interface**: PRD-1 defines `CanonicalRecord` with `time.Time` fields for `CreatedAt`/`UpdatedAt`. The implemented store uses `string` (RFC3339Nano). The adapter package must work with the real store type, not the PRD-1 draft type — or PRD-1's interface definition needs to align.

**Store API surface PRD-1 will consume:**
- `Open(rootDir string) (*Store, error)` — opens/creates the store
- `Append(r CanonicalRecord) (CanonicalRecord, error)` — single write
- `AppendBatch(rs []CanonicalRecord) ([]CanonicalRecord, error)` — batch write, single fsync
- `ReadLive(canonicalID string) (CanonicalRecord, error)` — latest non-deleted
- `ReadAll(canonicalID string) ([]CanonicalRecord, error)` — full revision history
- `Rebuild() error` — force index rebuild
- `Close() error`

**index.json actual shape (NO `by_provider` section):**
```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "source_fingerprint": "deadbeefdeadbeef",
  "last_line_count": 1,
  "built_at": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z",
  "entries": {
    "01HXY...": {
      "canonical_id": "01HXY...",
      "latest_revision": 1,
      "latest_line_offset": 0,
      "deleted": false,
      "updated_at": "...",
      "line_ulid": "..."
    }
  }
}
```
The `by_provider` section from PRD-1 §6 does NOT exist yet. It must be added.

**Test patterns established:**
- Table-driven tests with `t.Run(tt.name, ...)`
- `t.TempDir()` for all filesystem tests
- `syncer` interface injected into `Store.f` — allows mock without touching real file
- Integration tests in `integration_test.go` (no `testing.Short()` guard yet — note for new tests)
- `testify/require` + `testify/assert` throughout

---

### Gap Analysis

What PRD-1 needs that does NOT exist today:

| Gap | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `internal/adapter` package | New package with `Adapter` interface, `IDMap` interface, `NativeID`/`CanonicalID` type aliases, sentinel errors |
| Sentinel errors (adapter pkg) | `ErrUnsupported`, `ErrNotFound`, `ErrUnavailable` — separate from `store.ErrNotFound` |
| `internal/adapter/engram` package | Engram reference implementation: CLI subprocess wrapper, `EngramRecord`, `ToCanonical`/`FromCanonical`, personal scope filter |
| `IDMap` implementation | Read-only view over `by_provider.engram` section of `index.json` — this section does not exist yet |
| `index.json` extension | Add `by_provider` map to the `index` struct in `internal/store/index.go` |
| CLI exec interface | An interface around `os/exec` so the engram adapter can be tested without a real `engram` binary |
| Author attribution | `os.Hostname() + ":" + os.Getenv("USER")` logic, overridable via `WRAPPER_MEMS_AUTHOR` |
| `CanonicalRecord` time.Time alignment | PRD-1 §4 uses `time.Time`; store uses `string` — must reconcile |

---

### Approach Options for the 5 Open Questions (PRD-1 §9)

**Q1: ReadNative — read single engram observation by sync_id**

| Option | Description | Pros | Cons | Effort |
|--------|-------------|------|------|--------|
| (a) Upstream CLI flag `engram get <sync_id>` | Request `engram` maintainers add the command | Clean, stable | Blocks on upstream; timeline unknown | Low (once available) |
| (b) HTTP fallback for this single op | Use engram HTTP API on :7437 for `GET /observations/:id` | Available now, no subprocess | Requires daemon running; adds HTTP client dep; mixed transport | Low-Medium |
| (c) Search + title match (current fallback) | `engram search <title>` then match by sync_id in results | No dependency | Fragile: title collisions, O(n) scan, sync_id may not be in search results | Low |

**Recommendation**: (b) with a clearly-scoped HTTP client as an optional fallback path. The engram HTTP API is local-only and already used by other tools. Make the transport pluggable — the adapter accepts a `Transport` interface with a `GetByID` method; default is CLI+HTTP hybrid, test impl is a fake. This also unblocks Q5 (updated_at verification via HTTP). Do NOT block on upstream (a) — that is a nice-to-have tracked separately.

---

**Q2: Tags loss (canonical → engram)**

| Option | Description | Pros | Cons | Effort |
|--------|-------------|------|------|--------|
| (a) Accept loss, document | Tags preserved in canonical only | Zero implementation cost | Tags lost on re-import to engram | None |
| (b) Encode tags in content header | Prepend `tags: [x,y]` to content body | Roundtrippable | Pollutes content; fragile parsing | Medium |
| (c) Wait for upstream | Track upstream issue | Clean future | Blocks | None |

**Recommendation**: (a) + (c). Document the limitation in code and in the field mapping table comment. Open a tracking issue. Tags are stored losslessly in the canonical JSONL; only the engram view loses them. Acceptable for v1.

---

**Q3: session_id on import**

**Recommendation**: Attempt injection via the engram HTTP API write endpoint if available; if not, leave `session_id` empty in engram. Preserve it in canonical. Document as best-effort in the `WriteNative` implementation. Do NOT fail the write if session_id cannot be set — degrade gracefully.

---

**Q4: Engram relations deferral to v1.1**

**Recommendation**: Confirm deferral. Relations in PRD-0 are reserved as `kind: "relation"` but not yet read/written by the store. The adapter skipping `kind: "relation"` records in both directions is a clean, safe no-op for v1. Add a `// TODO v1.1: import engram relations as kind:relation canonical records` comment in `FromCanonical`.

---

**Q5: updated_at monotonicity**

**Recommendation**: Do NOT assume monotonicity from the CLI output. Engram may update `updated_at` on reads or have clock-skew across machines. Implement revision tracking as: on import, compare the canonical `Revision` from IDMap; if no prior revision exists, assign revision=1; if prior exists, always increment regardless of timestamp ordering. Log a warning if `updated_at` goes backward. This is defensive and costs nothing.

---

### IDMap: Implementation Options

| Option | Description | Pros | Cons | Effort |
|--------|-------------|------|------|--------|
| Read-only view over extended index.json | Add `by_provider map[string]map[string]string` to existing `index` struct; expose read-only wrapper | Reuses existing atomic write + fingerprint infra; one file to git-ignore | Couples index.go to provider concept; index rebuild would wipe provider map unless handled | Low |
| Separate `provider-map.json` file | New file alongside index.json, same dir, same gitignore | Clean separation; independent lifecycle | Another file to manage; another lock concern | Medium |
| In-memory only (no persistence) | IDMap rebuilt from JSONL on every open by scanning `origin.provider_id` | Simple | O(n) JSONL scan on every open; loses mapping across machines | Low |

**Recommendation**: Extend the existing `index` struct with `by_provider map[string]map[NativeID]CanonicalID`. The `index` is already written atomically and gitignored. Add a helper `ProviderIDMap(provider string) IDMap` to `Store` that returns a read-only view. On rebuild, repopulate `by_provider` from JSONL `origin.provider_id` fields. This keeps everything in one file and one lock domain.

**Critical**: rebuild must preserve `by_provider` — the rebuild path in `rebuild.go` must be updated to scan `origin.provider_id` fields and repopulate the map.

---

### CLI Exec Testability

The `syncer` interface pattern already in the store is the model to follow. For the engram CLI boundary:

```go
// internal/adapter/engram/exec.go
type Commander interface {
    Run(ctx context.Context, args ...string) (stdout []byte, stderr []byte, err error)
}
```

- Real impl: wraps `exec.CommandContext`
- Test impl: returns canned JSON/responses per args pattern
- This is CRITICAL per go-testing rules: integration tests using a real `engram` binary must be guarded with `testing.Short()` and skipped in `-short` mode

---

### `CanonicalRecord` time.Time vs string Discrepancy

PRD-1 §4 defines `CreatedAt time.Time` and `UpdatedAt time.Time`. The implemented store uses `string` (RFC3339Nano). Two options:

| Option | Description | Effort |
|--------|-------------|--------|
| (a) Adapter works with store type directly | `ToCanonical`/`FromCanonical` accept `store.CanonicalRecord`; no separate PRD-1 type | Low — no type duplication |
| (b) Adapter defines its own `CanonicalRecord` with time.Time | Adapter package owns the interface type; store package is an implementation detail | Medium — requires conversion layer |

**Recommendation**: (a). The adapter package imports `internal/store` and works with `store.CanonicalRecord` directly. The PRD-1 interface definition is aspirational; the proposal should lock this down. Defining a parallel type in the adapter package adds conversion boilerplate with no benefit at this stage.

---

### Affected Files

| File / Path | Change Type | Reason |
|-------------|-------------|--------|
| `internal/adapter/adapter.go` | New | `Adapter` interface, `IDMap` interface, type aliases, sentinel errors |
| `internal/adapter/engram/engram.go` | New | Engram adapter implementation |
| `internal/adapter/engram/exec.go` | New | `Commander` interface + real impl |
| `internal/adapter/engram/engram_test.go` | New | Unit tests with fake Commander |
| `internal/adapter/engram/integration_test.go` | New | Integration test with real engram binary (short-skipped) |
| `internal/store/index.go` | Modify | Add `by_provider` map to `index` struct |
| `internal/store/rebuild.go` | Modify | Populate `by_provider` during rebuild scan |
| `internal/store/store.go` | Modify | Add `ProviderIDMap(provider string) IDMap` method |
| `internal/store/errors.go` | No change | Store errors remain separate from adapter errors |

---

### Scope / Size Signal

Rough line estimate:

| Slice | Files | ~Lines |
|-------|-------|--------|
| Slice 1: Interface + errors + IDMap extension to store | `adapter.go`, `index.go` + `store.go` changes, `rebuild.go` changes | ~200 lines |
| Slice 2: Engram adapter + Commander + unit tests | `engram.go`, `exec.go`, `engram_test.go` | ~350 lines |
| Slice 3: Integration test + fake CLI | `integration_test.go` | ~100 lines |

Total: ~650 lines. **Chained PRs recommended: Yes**. Slice 1 (interface + store IDMap extension) is PR #1; Slice 2+3 (engram adapter + tests) is PR #2. Each slice is independently reviewable and mergeable.

---

### Open Questions Requiring Human Decision Before Proposal

1. **CanonicalRecord type authority**: Should the adapter package define its own `CanonicalRecord` (with `time.Time`) or use `store.CanonicalRecord` (with `string`) directly? This affects the interface contract for ALL future adapters (PRD-2, etc.).

2. **HTTP transport opt-in scope**: Should the HTTP fallback for `ReadNative` be included in v1 (requiring the daemon to be running) or explicitly deferred to v2? The PRD leans toward including it but does not commit.

3. **by_provider persistence strategy**: Extend `index.json` (recommended above) vs. a separate file? If extended, what happens on index corruption — does the provider map get wiped?

4. **`testing.Short()` convention**: The existing integration test does NOT use `testing.Short()`. Should the new engram adapter integration tests establish this convention, and should the existing integration test be retrofitted?

---

### Recommendation Summary

- **Package structure**: Two packages — `internal/adapter` (interface) and `internal/adapter/engram` (implementation). Clean separation that PRD-2 will reuse.
- **IDMap**: Extend `internal/store/index` with `by_provider`. One file, one lock, atomic writes already in place.
- **CLI boundary**: `Commander` interface, fake impl for unit tests, real impl for integration tests gated by `testing.Short()`.
- **time.Time vs string**: Use `store.CanonicalRecord` directly in the adapter. Resolve in proposal, not here.
- **Q1 (ReadNative)**: HTTP fallback on :7437 for ID lookups. Make transport pluggable.
- **Q2 (tags)**: Accept loss for v1, track upstream.
- **Q3 (session_id)**: Best-effort via HTTP, degrade gracefully.
- **Q4 (relations)**: Confirmed deferral to v1.1.
- **Q5 (updated_at)**: Increment revision counter unconditionally; do not trust timestamp ordering.

### Ready for Proposal
Yes. The three human decisions above should be resolved in the proposal phase or answered by the user before proposal drafting begins.

title: sdd/adapter-contract-engram/proposal
content:
# Proposal: Adapter Contract + Engram Adapter (PRD-1)

## Intent

**Problem**: PRD-0 shipped a production canonical JSONL store (`internal/store`), but nothing can feed it. There is no provider boundary, so engram-origin memories cannot be mirrored into the neutral canonical format that claude-mem users will later read.

**Outcome**: A small, stable `Adapter` interface plus a working engram reference implementation that ingests engram observations into the canonical store and converts back, with explicit round-trip guarantees. This validates the contract before PRD-2 (claude-mem) reuses it.

## Scope

### In Scope
- `internal/adapter` package: `Adapter` + `IDMap` interfaces, `NativeID`/`CanonicalID` aliases, sentinels (`ErrUnsupported`, `ErrNotFound`, `ErrUnavailable`).
- `internal/adapter/engram`: CLI subprocess wrapper behind a `Commander` interface, `EngramRecord`, pure `ToCanonical`/`FromCanonical`, personal-scope filter in `ListNative`, author attribution.
- `internal/store` extension: `by_provider` map on the `index` struct + `Store.ProviderIDMap(provider) IDMap` read-only view; rebuild repopulates `by_provider` from `origin.provider_id`.
- HTTP fallback transport for `ReadNative` (engram :7437) behind a pluggable interface, degrading cleanly when `engram serve` is down.
- Unit tests (fake `Commander`) + integration tests guarded by `testing.Short()`.

### Out of Scope
- **PRD-3 sync orchestration** — when/how Adapter methods are invoked (no scheduler, no push/pull cycle here).
- PRD-2 claude-mem adapter; PRD-4 TUI.
- Engram relations import (deferred to v1.1, §9.4 confirmed).
- Retrofitting `testing.Short()` onto the existing PRD-0 integration test.

## Approach

**Two packages.** `internal/adapter` owns the contract; `internal/adapter/engram` is the first implementation. `ToCanonical`/`FromCanonical` stay pure (no provider calls); only `ListNative`/`ReadNative`/`WriteNative`/`Health` touch engram.

**Timestamp authority — Gap #1 (DECISION, see below): adopt store-wins `string` (RFC3339Nano).** The adapter imports `internal/store` and operates on `store.CanonicalRecord` directly. No parallel adapter type, no conversion layer.

**IDMap — Gap #2.** Extend the existing `index` struct with `ByProvider map[string]map[string]string` (provider → nativeID → canonicalID). Reuses the existing atomic write + fingerprint + single-lock infra (one file, one lock domain). **Coordinated change**: `index.go` (struct + nil-map init in `loadIndex`), `rebuild.go` (`rebuildFromFile` must scan `origin.provider_id` and repopulate `ByProvider` — otherwise every stale-index rebuild silently wipes the mapping), `store.go` (`ProviderIDMap` accessor returning a read-only view).

**ReadNative HTTP fallback — Gap #3.** A pluggable `Transport` with `GetByID`. Default = CLI primary; if engram lacks `get <sync_id>`, try HTTP `GET /observations/:id` on :7437. If the daemon is down, return `ErrUnavailable` (not a panic, not a hang) — orchestrator (PRD-3) decides retry. HTTP is a focused fallback only, not a transport swap.

## Decision: Timestamp Type Authority (Gap #1) — RECOMMENDED, needs user confirmation

PRD-1 §4 drafts `CreatedAt/UpdatedAt time.Time`. The shipped, archived PRD-0 store persists them as `string` RFC3339Nano.

**Option A — store-wins `string` (RECOMMENDED)**: adapter uses `store.CanonicalRecord` as-is.
- Blast radius: `Adapter` signature unaffected (interface uses `time.Time` only for the `since` parameter, which is fine — formatted at the CLI boundary). PRD-2 reuses the same type with zero conversion. JSONL contract untouched. Migration cost: zero.
- **Load-bearing reason**: `tiebreakWinner` (rebuild.go:32-34) does *lexicographic string comparison* on `updatedAt` for conflict resolution. RFC3339Nano string ordering == chronological ordering only as long as the value stays a normalized string. Introducing `time.Time` round-trips risks reformatting (offset vs `Z`, trailing-zero nanos) that breaks the tiebreak invariant the production store depends on.

**Option B — PRD-1-wins `time.Time` + conversion layer at store boundary**:
- Cost: a parallel adapter `CanonicalRecord`, marshal/unmarshal converters on every read/write, and a normalization guarantee to keep tiebreak strings stable. Re-touches an archived production contract. Every future adapter pays the conversion tax.
- Only upside: slightly nicer ergonomics inside adapter code (can defer-format).

**Recommendation: Option A.** Treat PRD-1 §4's `time.Time` as an aspirational draft; the proposal supersedes it. Confirm before spec.

## Open Questions — recommendations (confirm in interactive review)

- **Q1 ReadNative**: HTTP fallback on :7437 behind pluggable `Transport`; do NOT block on upstream `engram get`. (in scope)
- **Q2 Tags loss**: accept loss for v1, document in mapping comment, track upstream. Tags stay lossless in canonical JSONL.
- **Q3 session_id on import**: best-effort via HTTP write; if unavailable leave empty in engram, preserve in canonical, never fail the write.
- **Q4 Relations**: deferral to v1.1 CONFIRMED. Adapter no-ops `kind:"relation"` both directions + TODO comment.
- **Q5 updated_at monotonicity**: do not trust CLI ordering. Increment `Revision` unconditionally when a prior mapping exists; log a warning on backward `updated_at`.

## Affected Areas

| Area | Impact | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `internal/adapter/adapter.go` | New | Interfaces, aliases, sentinels |
| `internal/adapter/engram/*.go` | New | Engram impl, `Commander`, `Transport`, tests |
| `internal/store/index.go` | Modified | `ByProvider` field + nil-map init |
| `internal/store/rebuild.go` | Modified | Repopulate `ByProvider` on rebuild |
| `internal/store/store.go` | Modified | `ProviderIDMap` accessor |

## Risks

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|------------|------------|
| Rebuild wipes `by_provider` | Med | Spec-level requirement: rebuild MUST repopulate from `origin.provider_id`; test it |
| `time.Time` round-trip breaks lexicographic tiebreak | High if Option B | Choose Option A; keep timestamps as normalized strings |
| HTTP fallback hangs when daemon down | Med | Context timeout → `ErrUnavailable`; never block |
| Integration test flakiness w/ real `engram` | Med | Gate with `testing.Short()`; fake `Commander` for unit path |

## Chained-PR Delivery Framing (~650 lines → exceeds 400 budget)

- **PR #1 (~200 lines)**: store IDMap extension — `adapter.go` interfaces/sentinels + `index.go`/`rebuild.go`/`store.go` `by_provider`. Independently mergeable; adds a capability with no behavior change to existing store users.
- **PR #2 (~450 lines)**: engram adapter + `Commander` + `Transport` + unit tests; integration test slice may split into PR #3 if PR #2 nears budget. Targets PR #1's branch.

Recommend chained PRs (Feature Branch Chain). Confirm delivery strategy at `sdd-tasks`.

## Rollback Plan

PR #1: `ByProvider` is additive JSON; revert the struct field + accessor — old index.json still parses (extra/missing key ignored). PR #2: delete `internal/adapter/` tree; no store dependency on it. No data migration, no JSONL format change at any point.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `Adapter` + `IDMap` interfaces compile; engram adapter satisfies `Adapter`.
- [ ] `ToCanonical`/`FromCanonical` are pure (no provider calls) — proven by unit tests with fake `Commander`.
- [ ] Personal-scope engram records never enter canonical.
- [ ] `by_provider` survives an index rebuild (regression test).
- [ ] `ReadNative` returns `ErrUnavailable` (not hang/panic) when engram is unreachable.
- [ ] Timestamp type decision confirmed and reflected in spec.

## Dependencies

- PRD-0 store (shipped, archived — observation #55).
- Engram CLI ≥ v1.15 available on PATH for integration tests (short-skipped otherwise).

title: DEFECT-LN-01 final: ListNative uses HTTP GET /export + client-side filter (verified against real engram API)
content:
## What
Empirically probed the real engram v1.15.12 HTTP API (daemon on :7437). Findings:
- `GET /search?q=...` is FTS5 — same limitation as CLI; requires non-empty query, `q=*`→null, broad token non-deterministic. NOT usable for enumeration.
- `POST /observations` is create-only (GET/PUT/DELETE → 405).
- No project-scoped list endpoint exists. `/projects` → 404 over HTTP.
- `GET /export` returns the FULL dump: `{"version","exported_at","sessions":[{...,"project",...}]}` with observations nested under sessions — ALL projects, deterministic.

**Final decision (user-confirmed, supersedes prior #60 "HTTP /search" framing which rested on a false premise):** `ListNative` calls **`GET /export` via the existing `Transport` seam**, parses the JSON, and filters client-side by `project` + `scope == "project"` (drop personal) + `updated_at >= since` (using `rfc3339NanoFixed` byte-comparable format). Degrades to `adapter.ErrUnavailable` when the daemon is unreachable. CLI `search` stays only for ReadNative by-ID.

Accepted tradeoff (made explicit to user): `/export` dumps ALL of the user's memories across ALL projects on every `ListNative`; the adapter filters in-process. Cost + transient cross-project data in memory are accepted as the only deterministic enumeration path in engram v1.15. To be revisited if engram adds a scoped list endpoint upstream.

## Why
Verified evidence beats the PRD-1 §5.1 / design #61 assumption. `/export` is the sole deterministic enumerator; reusing the already-approved HTTP transport seam keeps the contract coherent and the daemon-down path already maps to `ErrUnavailable`.

## Where
- `internal/adapter/engram/transport.go` — add an Export operation to the `Transport` seam
- `internal/adapter/engram/engram.go` — `ListNative` reimplemented over `GET /export` + filters
- `internal/adapter/engram/engram_test.go` — fake transport export path; `integration_test.go` — S-18 wired to real `engram serve`, skip if down / `-short`
- Amends/supersedes: PRD-1 §5.1, design #61, prior decision #60
- Delivered as PR#4 (follow-up slice) with its own sdd-verify before archive

## Learned
Both engram CLI and HTTP `search` are FTS-only; neither enumerates a project. Reference-adapter enumeration in engram v1.15 is ONLY possible via the unscoped `export` dump. Always probe the real provider API before trusting a PRD's transport assumptions.

title: sdd/adapter-contract-engram/design
content:
# Design: Adapter Contract + Engram Adapter (PRD-1)

## Technical Approach

Two new packages plus a coordinated 3-file store extension. `internal/adapter` owns the stable contract (interfaces, ID aliases, sentinels). `internal/adapter/engram` is the first implementation behind two test seams (`Commander` for CLI, `Transport` for HTTP fallback). The adapter operates on `store.CanonicalRecord` directly — no parallel type. All binding decisions (string RFC3339Nano timestamps, HTTP fallback in v1, relations deferred to v1.1) are treated as fixed inputs, not re-litigated.

## Architecture Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rejected | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Package boundary | `internal/adapter` (contract) + `internal/adapter/engram` (impl); both under `internal/` | Single flat pkg; `pkg/` public | Mirrors store's internal enforcement; PRD-2 reuses contract pkg with zero churn |
| Canonical type | Adapter imports `store.CanonicalRecord` as-is (string ts) | Parallel adapter type w/ `time.Time` + converters | Binding decision #1: `time.Time` round-trip risks reformatting that breaks `tiebreakWinner` lex compare (rebuild.go:32-34) |
| Adapter ID aliases | `type NativeID string`, `type CanonicalID string` in adapter pkg; `IDMap` speaks aliases, store speaks raw `string` | Aliases threaded into store | Keeps store provider-agnostic; adapter does the string↔alias cast at the boundary |
| IDMap storage | Extend existing `index` struct with `ByProvider map[string]map[string]string` (provider→native→canonical) | Separate `provider-map.json`; in-memory rebuild-on-open | Reuses atomic write + fingerprint + single lock domain; one file, one lifecycle |
| Engram transport | `Commander` (CLI primary) + pluggable `Transport` (HTTP fallback for `ReadNative` only) | CLI-only (Q1 fragile search-match); HTTP-only (daemon dependency) | Binding decision #2; CLI stays primary, HTTP is a narrow ID-lookup fallback |
| Revision tracking | Increment unconditionally when prior mapping exists; warn on backward `updated_at` | Trust CLI `updated_at` monotonicity | engram may bump `updated_at` on read / clock-skew across machines (Q5) |

## Data Flow

ListNative/ReadNative (engram CLI/HTTP) → EngramRecord → **ToCanonical(native, idmap)** [pure] → store.CanonicalRecord → store.Append/AppendBatch → memory.jsonl + index.ByProvider updated by store

store.ReadLive → store.CanonicalRecord → **FromCanonical** [pure] → EngramRecord → WriteNative (engram CLI; session_id best-effort via HTTP)

`ToCanonical`: canonical_id is **looked up** via `idmap.CanonicalFromNative(sync_id)`; if absent, leave `CanonicalID=""` so `store.computeAppendFields` mints a new ULID (store owns ID minting — adapter does NOT generate ULIDs). Timestamps copied as already-normalized RFC3339Nano strings (engram emits RFC3339; adapter normalizes once via `time.Parse`→`Format(RFC3339Nano)` to protect the lex-tiebreak invariant). Personal scope filtered in `ListNative` (never reaches `ToCanonical`). `kind:"relation"` no-op both directions + `// TODO v1.1` comment.

## Store Coordinated 3-File Change (IDMap)

- **index.go**: add `ByProvider map[string]map[string]string` json:`by_provider` to `index` struct; in `loadIndex`, nil-init (`if idx.ByProvider == nil { ... }`) alongside the existing `Entries` nil guard. Additive JSON — old index.json still parses (missing key → nil → initialized).
- **rebuild.go**: `rebuildFromFile` MUST repopulate `ByProvider`. Add `Origin` (provider+provider_id) to `rawRecord` OR full-decode; during the scan, for each non-skipped line with non-empty `origin.provider` and `origin.provider_id`, set `byProvider[provider][provider_id] = canonical_id`. The **winning** revision's canonical_id wins (last write per native id; since canonical_id is stable across a chain, any revision yields the same canonical_id — last-wins is safe). Build into the returned `index`. **Without this, every stale-index/corrupt-index rebuild silently wipes the mapping** — this is the primary risk.
- **store.go**: add `func (s *Store) ProviderIDMap(provider string) adapter.IDMap`. Returns a read-only snapshot view (copy the inner map under `s.mu.RLock()`) implementing `CanonicalFromNative`/`NativeFromCanonical` (the reverse direction built by inverting the snapshot). Snapshot, not live pointer, to avoid lock leakage and races. NOTE: import direction — `store` would import `adapter` for the return type. To keep `store` dependency-free, `ProviderIDMap` returns a small concrete `*store.providerIDMap` that **structurally satisfies** `adapter.IDMap`; the adapter pkg defines the interface, store needs no import.

## Interfaces

```go
// internal/adapter/adapter.go
type NativeID string
type CanonicalID string
type IDMap interface {
    CanonicalFromNative(NativeID) (CanonicalID, bool)
    NativeFromCanonical(CanonicalID) (NativeID, bool)
}
type Adapter interface {
    Name() string
    Health(ctx context.Context) error
    ListNative(ctx context.Context, project string, since time.Time) ([]NativeID, error)
    ReadNative(ctx context.Context, id NativeID) (NativeRecord, error)
    ToCanonical(native NativeRecord, idmap IDMap) (store.CanonicalRecord, error)
    FromCanonical(canonical store.CanonicalRecord) (NativeRecord, error)
    WriteNative(ctx context.Context, record NativeRecord) (NativeID, error)
}
var ErrUnsupported, ErrNotFound, ErrUnavailable = errors.New(...)

// internal/adapter/engram/exec.go
type Commander interface {
    Run(ctx context.Context, args ...string) (stdout, stderr []byte, err error)
}
// internal/adapter/engram/transport.go
type Transport interface {
    GetByID(ctx context.Context, id string) ([]byte, error) // HTTP :7437 fallback
}
```

`since time.Time` stays in the interface (formatted to RFC3339 at the CLI arg boundary only) — does not contradict string-storage decision.

### HTTP fallback degradation

`ReadNative` tries `Transport.GetByID` (default: `http://127.0.0.1:7437/observations/:id`) under a `context` deadline (default 2s). Mapping: connection refused / timeout / non-2xx → wrap `ErrUnavailable` (never panic, never hang). 404 → `ErrNotFound`. Orchestrator (PRD-3) decides retry. Transport is an injected field — tests pass a fake; nil Transport → `ReadNative` returns `ErrUnsupported`.

## File Changes

| File | Action | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `internal/adapter/adapter.go` | Create | Interfaces, ID aliases, 3 sentinels |
| `internal/adapter/engram/engram.go` | Create | `Adapter` impl, `EngramRecord`, `ToCanonical`/`FromCanonical`, scope filter, author attribution |
| `internal/adapter/engram/exec.go` | Create | `Commander` iface + `exec.CommandContext` impl |
| `internal/adapter/engram/transport.go` | Create | `Transport` iface + HTTP :7437 impl + error mapping |
| `internal/adapter/engram/engram_test.go` | Create | Table-driven unit tests, fake Commander/Transport |
| `internal/adapter/engram/integration_test.go` | Create | Real `engram` binary, `testing.Short()` skip |
| `internal/store/index.go` | Modify | `ByProvider` field + nil-init in `loadIndex` |
| `internal/store/rebuild.go` | Modify | Repopulate `ByProvider` during scan |
| `internal/store/store.go` | Modify | `ProviderIDMap` accessor (snapshot view) |

## Testing Strategy

| Layer | What | Approach |
|---|---|---|
| Unit | `ToCanonical`/`FromCanonical` purity, scope filter, relation no-op, revision increment, timestamp normalization | Table-driven, fake `Commander` returning canned JSON; assert zero provider calls in pure fns |
| Unit | HTTP error mapping (refused/timeout/404/non-2xx → sentinels) | Fake `Transport`; `httptest.Server` for real-ish paths |
| Unit | IDMap snapshot read-only + reverse direction | `t.TempDir()` store, `t.Run` cases |
| Integration | `by_provider` survives `Rebuild()` (regression for primary risk) | Write records w/ origin, force `Rebuild()`, assert mapping intact — NOT short-skipped (no binary needed) |
| Integration | Real engram `Health`/`ListNative`/`WriteNative` | `if testing.Short() { t.Skip }`; needs `engram` ≥ v1.15 on PATH |

`testing.Short()` convention established here; existing PRD-0 integration test retrofit is out of scope.

## Chained-PR Slice Boundaries

**PR #1 (~200 lines) — store IDMap + contract. MUST merge before PR #2.**
- `internal/adapter/adapter.go` (interfaces/aliases/sentinels — no impl)
- `internal/store/index.go`, `rebuild.go`, `store.go` (ByProvider + rebuild repopulation + accessor)
- Tests: `index_test.go`/`rebuild_test.go` additions incl. the rebuild-survival regression
- Self-contained: additive capability, zero behavior change for existing store users. Rollback: revert struct field + accessor; old index.json still parses.

**PR #2 (~450 lines) — engram adapter. Targets PR #1's branch.**
- `engram.go`, `exec.go`, `transport.go`, `engram_test.go`
- Depends on PR #1's `adapter.IDMap` + `Store.ProviderIDMap`
- If it nears the 400-line budget, split `integration_test.go` into PR #3 (Feature Branch Chain; PR #3 targets PR #2's branch). `sdd-tasks` decides via delivery strategy.

Dependency ordering is hard: PR #2 will not compile without PR #1's exported `adapter` package and store accessor.

## Migration / Rollout

No data migration. `by_provider` is additive JSON; absent key initializes empty. No JSONL format change. Rollback per PR is independent (PR #2 = delete `internal/adapter/` tree; store has no dependency on it).

## Open Questions

None blocking. `sdd-tasks` resolves the PR#2/PR#3 split via cached delivery strategy. Q2 (tags loss) / Q3 (session_id) accepted as documented degradations per binding decisions.

title: sdd/adapter-contract-engram/spec
content: # Spec: Adapter Contract + Engram Adapter (PRD-1) **Change**: adapter-contract-engram **Status**: FINAL **Depends on**: PRD-0 (obs #55, canonical-memory-schema — production) **Binding decisions**: obs #60 (timestamps=string, HTTP fallback in v1, relations deferred) --- ## 1. Scope Summary This spec covers the WHAT for three coordinated deliverables: | Deliverable | Package | Nature | |---|---|---| | Adapter contract | `internal/adapter` | New — interfaces, type aliases, sentinels | | Engram adapter | `internal/adapter/engram` | New — engram reference implementation | | Store IDMap extension | `internal/store` | Modified — `ByProvider` field + accessor + rebuild | --- ## 2. Timestamp Invariant (load-bearing, project-wide) **REQ-TS-01** — All timestamps in `CanonicalRecord` (`created_at`, `updated_at`) and in any adapter-internal record type are `string`, formatted as RFC3339Nano, normalized to UTC with `Z` suffix (e.g. `"2026-05-16T04:27:57.123456789Z"`). **REQ-TS-02** — No adapter, conversion helper, or test fixture may introduce `time.Time` as a field type for `created_at` or `updated_at` in `CanonicalRecord`. The `since` parameter of `ListNative` accepts `time.Time` for caller ergonomics and is formatted to RFC3339Nano at the CLI boundary inside the adapter — it does not enter the canonical record. **REQ-TS-03** — Timestamps MUST be byte-comparable such that lexicographic string ordering equals chronological ordering. This is a non-negotiable invariant because `tiebreakWinner` in `internal/store/rebuild.go` (line 32) resolves post-merge conflicts via string comparison of `updated_at`. Any timestamp that does not meet this property silently corrupts conflict resolution. --- ## 3. Adapter Contract (`internal/adapter`) ### 3.1 Types **REQ-AC-01** — The package MUST declare: - `type NativeID string` — opaque, provider-specific identifier. - `type CanonicalID string` — a ULID, consistent with PRD-0. - `type NativeRecord any` — concrete type is owned by each adapter implementation. **REQ-AC-02** — The package MUST declare sentinel errors: ``` var ErrUnsupported = errors.New("adapter: operation unsupported by this provider") var ErrNotFound = errors.New("adapter: native record not found") var ErrUnavailable = errors.New("adapter: provider not reachable") ``` These must be testable via `errors.Is`. ### 3.2 `Adapter` Interface **REQ-AC-03** — The `Adapter` interface MUST expose exactly these methods: - `Name() string` - `Health(ctx context.Context) error` - `ListNative(ctx context.Context, project string, since time.Time) ([]NativeID, error)` - `ReadNative(ctx context.Context, id NativeID) (NativeRecord, error)` - `ToCanonical(native NativeRecord, idmap IDMap) (store.CanonicalRecord, error)` - `FromCanonical(canonical store.CanonicalRecord) (NativeRecord, error)` - `WriteNative(ctx context.Context, record NativeRecord) (NativeID, error)` Note: `CanonicalRecord` is `store.CanonicalRecord` — no parallel type. The adapter package imports `internal/store`. ### 3.3 Purity Contract **REQ-AC-04** — `ToCanonical` MUST be a pure function: it MUST NOT call the provider, perform I/O, or have observable side effects. Its output is fully determined by `native` and `idmap`. **REQ-AC-05** — `FromCanonical` MUST be a pure function: it MUST NOT call the provider, perform I/O, or have observable side effects. Its output is fully determined by `canonical`. **REQ-AC-06** — The only methods permitted to interact with the provider are `ListNative`, `ReadNative`, `WriteNative`, and `Health`. `Health` is allowed exactly one read-only probe. ### 3.4 Personal-Scope Filtering **REQ-AC-07** — Personal-scope records MUST be filtered inside `ListNative`. The canonical store MUST NEVER receive a record whose provider-side scope is personal/private. This filtering is mandatory and not delegatable to the caller. ### 3.5 `IDMap` Interface **REQ-AC-08** — The `IDMap` interface MUST expose: - `CanonicalFromNative(NativeID) (CanonicalID, bool)` - `NativeFromCanonical(CanonicalID) (NativeID, bool)` **REQ-AC-09** — `IDMap` is read-only. No method on the interface may mutate state. ### 3.6 `WriteNative` Idempotence **REQ-AC-10** — `WriteNative` MUST be idempotent with respect to `origin.provider_id`: if a native record with the same provider-assigned ID already exists in the provider, the call MUST update it in place and MUST NOT create a duplicate entry. --- ## 4. Store IDMap Extension (`internal/store`) ### 4.1 `index` struct extension **REQ-IDX-01** — The `index` struct MUST gain a new field: ```go ByProvider map[string]map[string]string `json:"by_provider,omitempty"` ``` Semantics: `ByProvider[provider][nativeID] = canonicalID`. **REQ-IDX-02** — `loadIndex` MUST initialize `ByProvider` to a non-nil map when the field is absent or null in the JSON on disk, consistent with how `Entries` is initialized today. **REQ-IDX-03** — The `by_provider` field MUST use `omitempty` so that existing `index.json` files written by PRD-0 remain valid (no migration required). A missing `by_provider` key is treated as an empty mapping. ### 4.2 Rebuild repopulation (regression-critical) **REQ-IDX-04** — `rebuildFromFile` MUST repopulate `ByProvider` by scanning each canonical record's `origin.provider` and `origin.provider_id` fields as it iterates `memory.jsonl`. After a rebuild, `ByProvider` MUST reflect every live (non-deleted) mapping present in the JSONL. **REQ-IDX-05** — Deleted records (tombstones with `deleted: true`) MUST NOT contribute entries to `ByProvider` during rebuild. A tombstone removes the mapping for its `canonical_id`. **REQ-IDX-06** — A stale-index rebuild (triggered by fingerprint mismatch or corrupt index) MUST produce a `ByProvider` that is identical to one produced by a fresh-from-empty rebuild over the same JSONL content. The prior `ByProvider` state on disk MUST be discarded, not merged. ### 4.3 `ProviderIDMap` accessor **REQ-IDX-07** — `Store` MUST expose: ```go func (s *Store) ProviderIDMap(provider string) IDMap ``` This returns a read-only `IDMap` view over `s.idx.ByProvider[provider]`. The returned view MUST reflect the index state at call time (snapshot semantics acceptable). If `provider` is unknown, the returned `IDMap` returns `false` for all lookups — it MUST NOT return nil. **REQ-IDX-08** — `ProviderIDMap` is a read-only operation. It MUST NOT acquire the write lock. It MAY acquire the read lock. --- ## 5. Engram Adapter (`internal/adapter/engram`) ### 5.1 `EngramRecord` internal type **REQ-ENG-01** — The engram adapter MUST declare an internal `EngramRecord` struct. All timestamp fields within it are `string` (RFC3339Nano), consistent with REQ-TS-01. ### 5.2 `Commander` interface (CLI boundary) **REQ-ENG-02** — All engram CLI subprocess calls MUST be made through a `Commander` interface: ```go type Commander interface { Run(ctx context.Context, args ...string) (stdout []byte, stderr []byte, err error) } ``` **REQ-ENG-03** — The production implementation of `Commander` shells out to the `engram` binary found on `PATH`. The `engram` adapter struct MUST accept a `Commander` at construction time to enable unit testing with a fake. ### 5.3 `Transport` interface (HTTP fallback) **REQ-ENG-04** — `ReadNative` MUST attempt the CLI path first. When the CLI path cannot return the record by ID (engram CLI v1.15 lacks `read `), the adapter MUST fall back to an HTTP `GET /observations/:id` against `http://localhost:7437`. **REQ-ENG-05** — The HTTP fallback MUST be behind a `Transport` interface: ```go type Transport interface { GetByID(ctx context.Context, id NativeID) (EngramRecord, error) } ``` **REQ-ENG-06** — The adapter struct MUST accept a `Transport` at construction time. The default `Transport` implementation makes an HTTP request with the context deadline forwarded. The context timeout is the sole mechanism for bounding the HTTP request — the adapter MUST NOT use a fixed internal timeout that ignores the caller's context. **REQ-ENG-07** — When the HTTP daemon is unreachable (connection refused, timeout, or non-2xx response that indicates server unavailability), `ReadNative` MUST return `adapter.ErrUnavailable`. It MUST NOT panic, hang, or return a raw HTTP/network error directly to the caller. **REQ-ENG-08** — When both CLI and HTTP paths fail to find the record (404 or equivalent), `ReadNative` MUST return `adapter.ErrNotFound`. ### 5.4 `Name()` **REQ-ENG-09** — `Name()` MUST return the string `"engram"` (lowercase, stable). This value MUST match `origin.provider` in every canonical record produced by this adapter. ### 5.5 `Health()` **REQ-ENG-10** — `Health` MUST execute `engram version` via `Commander`. It MUST return nil if and only if the command exits with status 0. Any non-zero exit or I/O error MUST return `adapter.ErrUnavailable` (wrapped). ### 5.6 `ListNative()` **REQ-ENG-11** — `ListNative` MUST execute: ``` engram search "" --project --limit 1000 --scope project ``` The `--scope project` flag is the enforcement point for REQ-AC-07. Records with `scope: personal` in the response MUST be excluded even if the CLI returns them (defensive double-filter). **REQ-ENG-12** — The `since` parameter is a `time.Time`. The adapter MUST format it to RFC3339Nano and filter the returned records by `updated_at >= since` after receiving the CLI output. This comparison MUST use string comparison (consistent with REQ-TS-03); no `time.Time` parsing is required. **REQ-ENG-13** — If the CLI returns more than 1000 results, `ListNative` MUST log a warning and return the 1000 it has. Pagination is out of scope for v1. ### 5.7 `ReadNative()` **REQ-ENG-14** — `ReadNative` attempts to retrieve the record identified by `id` (the engram `sync_id`). The strategy is: 1. CLI path: `engram search --project --limit 1` and match on `sync_id`. 2. If CLI path does not return an exact `sync_id` match, fall back to HTTP `Transport.GetByID`. 3. If both fail, return `adapter.ErrNotFound`. 4. If HTTP fails due to daemon unavailability, return `adapter.ErrUnavailable`. **REQ-ENG-15** — `ReadNative` MUST forward the caller's `ctx` to both CLI and HTTP paths. A cancelled or timed-out context causes the in-flight operation to abort and the error to propagate. ### 5.8 `ToCanonical()` — field mapping **REQ-ENG-16** — `ToCanonical` MUST produce a `store.CanonicalRecord` using the following field mapping. All rules apply unconditionally unless noted. | Source (EngramRecord) | Destination (CanonicalRecord) | Rule | |---|---|---| | `ID` (sync_id preferred) | `Origin.ProviderID` | 1:1 | | `Title` | `Title` | 1:1 | | `Type` | `Type` | 1:1, free string | | `Content` | `Content` | 1:1 | | `ContentFormat` | `ContentFormat` | Always `"markdown"` for engram records | | `TopicKey` | `TopicKey` | 1:1 | | `SessionID` | `Origin.SessionID` | 1:1 | | `CreatedAt` (string) | `CreatedAt` | 1:1 — already normalized RFC3339Nano | | `UpdatedAt` (string) | `UpdatedAt` | 1:1 — already normalized RFC3339Nano | | — | `Kind` | Always `"observation"` | | — | `Origin.Provider` | Always `"engram"` | | — | `Origin.Author` | See REQ-ENG-19 | | — | `CanonicalID` | Looked up via `idmap.CanonicalFromNative(id)`; if not found, left empty for the store to assign on `Append` | | — | `Revision` | If prior mapping exists: `existing.LatestRevision + 1`. If new: `1`. See REQ-ENG-20 | | — | `SchemaVersion` | Set by `store.Append`; adapter MUST NOT set it | | Tags | (lost) | Engram CLI v1.15 has no tags field. Tags are NOT round-tripped. MUST be documented with a TODO comment referencing upstream tracking. | | Relations | (skipped) | Engram relations (`supersedes`, `conflicts_with`, etc.) are NOT imported in v1. Records with `kind: relation` MUST be skipped entirely. See REQ-ENG-21. | **REQ-ENG-17** — `ToCanonical` MUST be called only after `ReadNative` has already returned the `NativeRecord`. It MUST NOT perform any I/O. **REQ-ENG-18** — If `EngramRecord.Scope` is `"personal"`, `ToCanonical` MUST return an error. This is a defensive check; the primary filter is in `ListNative` (REQ-AC-07). ### 5.9 Author attribution **REQ-ENG-19** — `Origin.Author` MUST be set as follows, in priority order: 1. Value of environment variable `WRAPPER_MEMS_AUTHOR` (if non-empty). 2. `os.Getenv("USER") + "@" + hostname` where hostname comes from `os.Hostname()`. If either is empty, use what is available. This is best-effort identification, not authentication. ### 5.10 Revision tracking **REQ-ENG-20** — `ToCanonical` MUST increment `Revision` unconditionally when a prior mapping exists (canonical ID found in IDMap). It MUST NOT attempt to detect whether the content changed. Reason: `updated_at` monotonicity from engram is not guaranteed; content-diffing is out of scope for v1. When `updated_at` in the incoming record is lexicographically less than `updated_at` in the existing canonical record, `ToCanonical` MUST still produce the incremented revision but MUST log a warning (backward `updated_at` detected). ### 5.11 Relations handling (v1 no-op) **REQ-ENG-21** — `ToCanonical` MUST return `adapter.ErrUnsupported` for any `EngramRecord` whose engram-side type or fields indicate it is a relation record. The call site (PRD-3 orchestrator) handles this by skipping the record. The adapter MUST include a `// TODO(v1.1): import engram relations` comment at this branch. ### 5.12 `FromCanonical()` — field mapping **REQ-ENG-22** — `FromCanonical` MUST produce an `EngramRecord` using the following field mapping: | Source (CanonicalRecord) | Destination (EngramRecord) | Rule | |---|---|---| | `Title` | `Title` | 1:1 | | `Type` | `Type` | 1:1 | | `Content` | `Content` | 1:1 | | `TopicKey` | `TopicKey` | 1:1 | | `Origin.SessionID` | `SessionID` | Best-effort; may not be accepted by `engram save` CLI | | `Tags` | (lost) | No CLI flag; MUST include a TODO comment | | `CreatedAt` | (engram-set) | Engram assigns its own timestamp on save; canonical value is preserved in JSONL only | | `Kind == "session_summary"` | `Type = "session_summary"`, content = raw narrative | Imported as a typed observation | | `Kind == "relation"` | (skipped) | MUST return `adapter.ErrUnsupported`; include TODO(v1.1) comment | **REQ-ENG-23** — `FromCanonical` MUST be pure (REQ-AC-05). It returns an `EngramRecord` for the caller to pass to `WriteNative`. ### 5.13 `WriteNative()` **REQ-ENG-24** — `WriteNative` MUST execute `engram save <content> --type <T> --project <P> --scope project` for new records. **REQ-ENG-25** — Before issuing a save, `WriteNative` MUST check whether a record with the same `origin.provider_id` already exists by calling `ListNative` or an equivalent targeted search. If a match is found, it MUST use the update path (`engram update` or equivalent) instead of creating a new record. If no update path is available in the CLI, `WriteNative` MUST return `adapter.ErrUnsupported` rather than silently duplicating. **REQ-ENG-26** — `WriteNative` MUST return the `NativeID` assigned by engram after the write. If the CLI does not echo the ID, the adapter MUST do a follow-up `ReadNative` to resolve it. --- ## 6. Round-Trip Guarantees | Field | Engram → Canonical | Canonical → Engram | |---|---|---| | title | Lossless | Lossless | | type | Lossless | Lossless | | content | Lossless | Lossless | | topic_key | Lossless | Lossless | | session_id | Lossless (in canonical) | Best-effort (CLI may reject) | | created_at | Lossless (in canonical) | Lost (engram re-assigns) | | tags | Lost (no CLI support) | Lost (no CLI support) | | relations | Not imported (v1) | Not exported (v1) | | personal scope | Filtered — never reaches canonical | n/a | --- ## 7. Acceptance Scenarios ### S-01: ToCanonical purity — no provider calls **Given** a fake `Commander` that panics if called **And** a fake `Transport` that panics if called **And** an `EngramRecord` with `Scope: "project"` **When** `ToCanonical(record, idmap)` is called **Then** neither the `Commander` nor the `Transport` is invoked **And** a valid `store.CanonicalRecord` is returned with no error ### S-02: FromCanonical purity — no provider calls **Given** a fake `Commander` that panics if called **And** an `EngramAdapter` initialized with that Commander **And** a `store.CanonicalRecord` with `Kind: "observation"` **When** `FromCanonical(record)` is called **Then** the `Commander` is not invoked **And** a valid `EngramRecord` is returned ### S-03: Personal-scope filtering in ListNative **Given** a `Commander` that returns a mix of `scope: project` and `scope: personal` records **When** `ListNative(ctx, "myproject", since)` is called **Then** the returned `[]NativeID` contains only IDs of records with `scope: project` **And** personal-scope IDs are absent from the result ### S-04: WriteNative idempotence — existing provider_id **Given** a canonical record with `Origin.ProviderID = "obs-abc123"` **And** a `Commander` stub that reports "obs-abc123" exists on a targeted search **When** `WriteNative(ctx, engramRecord)` is called **Then** the `Commander` executes an update command, not a new save **And** no duplicate record is created in the provider **And** the returned `NativeID` equals `"obs-abc123"` ### S-05: ReadNative HTTP fallback — daemon down **Given** a `Commander` that returns empty results for the sync_id search **And** a `Transport` stub that returns a connection-refused error **When** `ReadNative(ctx, id)` is called **Then** the returned error is (or wraps) `adapter.ErrUnavailable` **And** the function returns before the context deadline ### S-06: ReadNative HTTP fallback — daemon up, record found **Given** a `Commander` that returns empty results for the sync_id search **And** a `Transport` stub that returns a valid `EngramRecord` for the ID **When** `ReadNative(ctx, id)` is called **Then** the returned `NativeRecord` matches the stub's response **And** no error is returned ### S-07: ReadNative — record not found in either path **Given** a `Commander` that returns empty results **And** a `Transport` stub that returns a 404 equivalent **When** `ReadNative(ctx, id)` is called **Then** the returned error is (or wraps) `adapter.ErrNotFound` ### S-08: ReadNative — context cancellation propagates **Given** a `Commander` that blocks indefinitely **And** a context with a 1ms deadline **When** `ReadNative(ctx, id)` is called **Then** the function returns within a reasonable duration after the deadline **And** the returned error satisfies `errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded)` or `errors.Is(err, adapter.ErrUnavailable)` ### S-09: by_provider survives index rebuild (regression) **Given** a `memory.jsonl` containing records with `origin.provider = "engram"` and `origin.provider_id` set **And** the `index.json` is deleted (forcing a full rebuild) **When** `store.Open(rootDir)` is called (triggers `rebuildFromFile`) **Then** `store.ProviderIDMap("engram").CanonicalFromNative(nativeID)` returns the correct canonical ID for every non-deleted engram-origin record **And** `ProviderIDMap("engram").CanonicalFromNative(nativeID)` returns `false` for deleted records ### S-10: by_provider not present in legacy index.json **Given** an `index.json` on disk that has no `by_provider` key (written by PRD-0 code) **When** `loadIndex` parses it **Then** `idx.ByProvider` is a non-nil empty map (not nil) **And** `store.Open` succeeds without error ### S-11: ProviderIDMap — unknown provider **Given** an open `Store` with no engram-origin records **When** `store.ProviderIDMap("engram")` is called **Then** a non-nil `IDMap` is returned **And** `idmap.CanonicalFromNative(anyID)` returns `("", false)` without panicking ### S-12: Timestamp RFC3339Nano normalization **Given** an `EngramRecord` with `CreatedAt = "2026-05-16T01:33:00.000000000Z"` and `UpdatedAt = "2026-05-16T04:27:57.123456789Z"` **When** `ToCanonical` is called **Then** `result.CreatedAt == "2026-05-16T01:33:00.000000000Z"` (byte-identical) **And** `result.UpdatedAt == "2026-05-16T04:27:57.123456789Z"` (byte-identical) **And** both values are lexicographically comparable (no timezone offset, no format variation) ### S-13: Author attribution — env var override **Given** `WRAPPER_MEMS_AUTHOR = "ci-bot@build-server"` **When** `ToCanonical` is called **Then** `result.Origin.Author == "ci-bot@build-server"` ### S-14: Author attribution — default fallback **Given** `WRAPPER_MEMS_AUTHOR` is unset **And** `USER = "agus"` and `os.Hostname() = "laptop"` **When** `ToCanonical` is called **Then** `result.Origin.Author == "agus@laptop"` (or whichever parts are available) ### S-15: Relations skipped with ErrUnsupported **Given** an `EngramRecord` that represents a relation **When** `ToCanonical` is called **Then** the returned error is (or wraps) `adapter.ErrUnsupported` **And** no partial `CanonicalRecord` is returned ### S-16: Personal-scope record rejected by ToCanonical **Given** an `EngramRecord` with `Scope = "personal"` **When** `ToCanonical` is called directly (defensive path) **Then** the returned error is non-nil **And** no `CanonicalRecord` is returned ### S-17: Integration — Health check (guarded by testing.Short) ``` if testing.Short() { t.Skip("requires engram on PATH") } ``` **Given** `engram` is available on PATH **When** `adapter.Health(context.Background())` is called **Then** it returns nil ### S-18: Integration — ListNative round-trip (guarded by testing.Short) ``` if testing.Short() { t.Skip("requires engram on PATH") } ``` **Given** at least one project-scope observation exists in engram for the test project **When** `ListNative(ctx, project, zeroTime)` is called **Then** the returned slice is non-empty **And** every returned ID corresponds to a record retrievable by `ReadNative` ### S-19: Revision increment on re-import **Given** a `store.CanonicalRecord` at revision 3 with `Origin.ProviderID = "obs-xyz"` **And** an `IDMap` stub that maps `"obs-xyz"` → the canonical ID **When** `ToCanonical` is called for an update of the same engram record **Then** `result.Revision == 4` ### S-20: Backward updated_at — warning logged, revision still incremented **Given** an existing canonical record with `UpdatedAt = "2026-05-16T10:00:00Z"` at revision 2 **And** an incoming `EngramRecord` with `UpdatedAt = "2026-05-16T09:00:00Z"` (earlier) **When** `ToCanonical` is called **Then** `result.Revision == 3` (still incremented) **And** a warning is logged containing "backward updated_at" **And** no error is returned ### S-21: by_provider tombstone exclusion **Given** a `memory.jsonl` with: - line 1: canonical record `C1` with `origin.provider_id = "obs-A"`, `deleted: false` - line 2: tombstone for `C1` with `deleted: true` **When** `rebuildFromFile` runs **Then** `ByProvider["engram"]["obs-A"]` is absent (tombstone wins) --- ## 8. Out of Scope (v1) - Engram relations import (`kind: relation`) — deferred to v1.1. - Pagination beyond 1000 results in `ListNative`. - `mirror_engram` flag / `engram sync` invocation — PRD-3 orchestration. - PRD-2 (claude-mem adapter) — separate change. - Authentication / multi-user identity. - Retrofitting `testing.Short()` onto existing PRD-0 integration tests. --- ## 9. Constraints | ID | Constraint | |---|---| | CON-01 | `internal/adapter/engram` imports `internal/store` but NOT vice versa. The dependency arrow is one-way. | | CON-02 | The existing `internal/store` public API is not broken. All changes are additive. | | CON-03 | `index.json` format remains backward-compatible. New `by_provider` field uses `omitempty`. | | CON-04 | Integration tests that shell out to `engram` MUST be guarded with `testing.Short()`. Unit tests use fake `Commander` and fake `Transport` only. | | CON-05 | `store.CanonicalRecord` is the canonical record type. No parallel adapter-level record type is introduced. |

title: sdd/adapter-contract-engram/tasks
content:
# Tasks: adapter-contract-engram **Change**: adapter-contract-engram **Delivery strategy**: ask-on-risk **Slice layout**: PR#1 (store IDMap + contract) → PR#2 (engram adapter) → PR#3 (integration tests, optional split) --- ## Review Workload Forecast | Field | Value | |---|---| | Estimated changed lines | ~680 (PR#1 ~210, PR#2 ~470 split into PR#2 ~320 + PR#3 ~150) | | 400-line budget risk | High (PR#2 alone is ~470 without the split) | | Chained PRs recommended | Yes | | Suggested slice boundaries | PR#1 (~210 lines) store IDMap + adapter contract · PR#2 (~320 lines) engram adapter impl + unit tests · PR#3 (~150 lines) integration tests — see rationale below | | Decision needed before apply | Yes — confirm PR#2/PR#3 sub-split or accept PR#2 as ~470 with size:exception | **PR#2/PR#3 sub-split assessment**: The design flagged this as optional. The engram adapter body (engram.go, exec.go, transport.go) is ~200 lines; unit tests in engram_test.go add ~120 lines; integration_test.go is ~150 lines. Splitting integration_test.go into PR#3 keeps PR#2 at ~320 lines (within budget), reduces review scope to "does the production code behave correctly per fakes?", and lets PR#3 land independently once the binary is available on CI. **Recommendation: take the 3-PR split.** PR#3 hard-depends on PR#2 (needs the real adapter struct and interfaces). **Dependency ordering (hard)**: - PR#2 will not compile without PR#1's exported `adapter` package and `Store.ProviderIDMap`. - PR#3 will not compile without PR#2's `EngramAdapter` construction API. --- ## PR#1 — Store IDMap + Adapter Contract (~210 lines) > Self-contained additive capability. Zero behavior change for existing store users. > Rollback: revert struct field + accessor; old index.json still parses. ### T-01 · index.go — add ByProvider field **Files**: `internal/store/index.go` **Spec**: REQ-IDX-01, REQ-IDX-02, REQ-IDX-03 **What**: Add `ByProvider map[string]map[string]string \`json:"by_provider,omitempty"\`` to the `index` struct. In `loadIndex`, add nil-init guard (`if idx.ByProvider == nil { idx.ByProvider = make(...) }`) alongside the existing `Entries` nil guard. **Verifiable when**: `index.json` without `by_provider` key parses without error and `idx.ByProvider` is non-nil after `loadIndex`. ### T-02 · rebuild.go — repopulate ByProvider during scan (regression-critical) **Files**: `internal/store/rebuild.go` **Spec**: REQ-IDX-04, REQ-IDX-05, REQ-IDX-06 **What**: In `rebuildFromFile`, after each non-skipped line is decoded, read `origin.provider` and `origin.provider_id`. If both are non-empty and `deleted != true`, set `byProvider[provider][providerID] = canonicalID`. If a tombstone is encountered for a canonical_id that already has a `ByProvider` entry, delete the entry (tombstone wins). At the end, assign the built map to the returned `index.ByProvider`. Prior on-disk `ByProvider` is discarded — full recompute. **Verifiable when**: A rebuild over JSONL with origin + tombstone entries produces the correct mapping. Without this, every stale/corrupt-index recovery silently wipes the mapping — primary risk for the whole change. ### T-03 · store.go — ProviderIDMap accessor **Files**: `internal/store/store.go` **Spec**: REQ-IDX-07, REQ-IDX-08, CON-01 **What**: Add `func (s *Store) ProviderIDMap(provider string) IDMap` that acquires `s.mu.RLock()`, snapshots `s.idx.ByProvider[provider]` (copy the inner map), releases the lock, then returns a concrete `*providerIDMap` value. `providerIDMap` holds both the forward map and a lazily-built reverse map. It structurally satisfies the `adapter.IDMap` interface at the call site without importing `internal/adapter` (store stays dependency-free). Unknown provider → return non-nil `IDMap` that returns `false` for all lookups. **Verifiable when**: `ProviderIDMap("unknown")` returns non-nil; all lookups return `("", false)` without panic. ### T-04 · internal/adapter/adapter.go — Create adapter contract **Files**: `internal/adapter/adapter.go` **Spec**: REQ-AC-01, REQ-AC-02, REQ-AC-03, REQ-AC-08, REQ-AC-09 **What**: Create the file. Declare `NativeID string`, `CanonicalID string`, `NativeRecord any` type aliases. Declare `ErrUnsupported`, `ErrNotFound`, `ErrUnavailable` sentinel errors. Declare `IDMap` interface (`CanonicalFromNative`, `NativeFromCanonical`). Declare `Adapter` interface with all 7 methods. `store.CanonicalRecord` imported from `internal/store`. No implementation in this file. **Verifiable when**: File compiles; all exported symbols present; `errors.Is` works for all three sentinels. ### T-05 · Tests — store IDMap (same work unit as T-01–T-03) **Files**: `internal/store/index_test.go`, `internal/store/rebuild_test.go` **Spec**: S-09, S-10, S-11; REQ-IDX-04, REQ-IDX-05, REQ-IDX-06 **What**: Three test cases: 1. S-10: write a legacy `index.json` with no `by_provider` key → open store → assert `ByProvider` non-nil. 2. S-11: open store with no engram-origin records → `ProviderIDMap("engram")` non-nil, lookups return `("", false)`. 3. S-09 (regression, NOT short-skipped): write `memory.jsonl` with engram-origin records + a tombstone, delete `index.json`, call `store.Open` → assert `ByProvider["engram"]["obs-A"] == canonicalID` and tombstoned entry absent. This test MUST NOT use `testing.Short()` (no binary needed; regression must always run). **Verifiable when**: All three sub-tests pass in `go test ./internal/store/...`. --- ## PR#2 — Engram Adapter (impl + unit tests, ~320 lines) > Targets PR#1's branch. Will not compile without PR#1's `adapter` package + `Store.ProviderIDMap`. ### T-06 · internal/adapter/engram/exec.go — Commander interface + exec impl **Files**: `internal/adapter/engram/exec.go` **Spec**: REQ-ENG-02, REQ-ENG-03 **What**: Declare `Commander` interface (`Run(ctx context.Context, args ...string) (stdout, stderr []byte, err error)`). Implement `execCommander` backed by `exec.CommandContext`. Constructor `NewExecCommander() Commander`. The production adapter accepts `Commander` at construction; tests inject a fake. **Verifiable when**: Interface declared; `execCommander` compiles; test can substitute a fake. ### T-07 · internal/adapter/engram/transport.go — Transport interface + HTTP impl **Files**: `internal/adapter/engram/transport.go` **Spec**: REQ-ENG-04, REQ-ENG-05, REQ-ENG-06, REQ-ENG-07, REQ-ENG-08 **What**: Declare `Transport` interface (`GetByID(ctx context.Context, id NativeID) (EngramRecord, error)`). Implement `httpTransport` targeting `http://127.0.0.1:7437/observations/:id`. The HTTP request MUST use the caller's `ctx` (no fixed internal timeout). Error mapping: connection refused / timeout / non-2xx → `adapter.ErrUnavailable`; 404 → `adapter.ErrNotFound`. Nil `Transport` in the adapter struct → `ReadNative` returns `adapter.ErrUnsupported` at HTTP fallback step. **Verifiable when**: Interface declared; HTTP impl compiles; error mapping verified by unit tests (T-10). ### T-08 · internal/adapter/engram/engram.go — EngramRecord + adapter struct + constructor **Files**: `internal/adapter/engram/engram.go` **Spec**: REQ-ENG-01, REQ-ENG-09, REQ-ENG-19; CON-05 **What**: Declare `EngramRecord` struct with all fields as string timestamps (REQ-TS-01). Declare `EngramAdapter` struct with `commander Commander`, `transport Transport` fields. Constructor `New(commander Commander, transport Transport) *EngramAdapter`. Implement `Name()` returning `"engram"`. Implement author-attribution helper (REQ-ENG-19: check `WRAPPER_MEMS_AUTHOR` env first, else `USER@hostname`). **Verifiable when**: `Name()` returns `"engram"`; constructor compiles; author attribution tested (T-10, S-13, S-14). ### T-09 · engram.go — Health, ListNative, ToCanonical, FromCanonical **Files**: `internal/adapter/engram/engram.go` **Spec**: REQ-ENG-10, REQ-ENG-11, REQ-ENG-12, REQ-ENG-13, REQ-ENG-16–REQ-ENG-23; REQ-AC-04, REQ-AC-05, REQ-AC-07; REQ-TS-01–REQ-TS-03; S-01–S-03, S-12–S-16, S-19, S-20 **What** (in dependency order within the file): - `Health`: run `engram version` via Commander; nil iff exit 0, else wrap `ErrUnavailable`. - `ListNative`: run `engram search "" --project

--limit 1000 --scope project`; parse JSON response; defensive double-filter `scope != "personal"`; post-filter by `updated_at >= since` via string comparison; warn if 1000 records returned (pagination limit). Returns `[]NativeID`. - `ToCanonical` (pure): apply REQ-ENG-16 field mapping. Normalize timestamps once via `time.Parse` → `Format(time.RFC3339Nano)` to enforce byte-comparable UTC+Z format. Lookup `CanonicalFromNative(id)` in idmap; empty string if not found (store mints ULID). Set `Revision` per REQ-ENG-20 (increment unconditionally if mapping exists; log warning on backward `updated_at`). Scope guard: return error if `Scope == "personal"` (REQ-ENG-18). Relation guard: return `ErrUnsupported` + `// TODO(v1.1): import engram relations` comment (REQ-ENG-21). Do NOT set `SchemaVersion` (store owns it). Set `Origin.Provider = "engram"`, `Origin.Author` via attribution helper. - `FromCanonical` (pure): apply REQ-ENG-22 field mapping. Skip / return `ErrUnsupported` for `Kind == "relation"` with TODO comment. Map `Kind == "session_summary"` to typed observation. **Verifiable when**: `ToCanonical`/`FromCanonical` call no I/O under fakes that panic-on-call (S-01, S-02); all table-driven unit tests pass. ### T-10 · engram.go — ReadNative, WriteNative **Files**: `internal/adapter/engram/engram.go` **Spec**: REQ-ENG-14, REQ-ENG-15, REQ-ENG-24, REQ-ENG-25, REQ-ENG-26; S-04–S-08 **What**: - `ReadNative`: step 1 — CLI search by sync_id; step 2 — HTTP fallback if no exact match; step 3 — return `ErrNotFound` if both fail. Forward caller `ctx` to both paths. HTTP: connection refused/timeout/non-2xx → `ErrUnavailable`; 404 → `ErrNotFound` (REQ-ENG-07, REQ-ENG-08). - `WriteNative`: check existing record with same `provider_id` via targeted search first (REQ-AC-10 / REQ-ENG-25). If found: use update path; if CLI has no update → return `ErrUnsupported`. If not found: `engram save <content> --type <T> --project <P> --scope project`. Return `NativeID` from CLI output; if not echoed, do follow-up `ReadNative` to resolve (REQ-ENG-26). **Verifiable when**: S-04 (idempotence), S-05–S-08 (ReadNative paths and context cancel) all pass. ### T-11 · Unit tests — engram adapter **Files**: `internal/adapter/engram/engram_test.go` **Spec**: S-01–S-08, S-12–S-16, S-19, S-20; REQ-ENG-*, REQ-TS-01–TS-03 **What**: Table-driven unit tests only. All tests use fake Commander/Transport (no real binary). Cover: - S-01: `ToCanonical` purity — fake Commander + Transport panic if called; assert no panic, valid record returned. - S-02: `FromCanonical` purity — same guard. - S-03: Personal-scope filtering in `ListNative` — Commander returns mixed; assert only project-scope IDs returned. - S-04: `WriteNative` idempotence — Commander stub reports existing ID; assert update path taken, no duplicate. - S-05: `ReadNative` HTTP fallback, daemon down → `ErrUnavailable`. - S-06: `ReadNative` HTTP fallback, daemon up, record found → valid record. - S-07: `ReadNative` not found in either path → `ErrNotFound`. - S-08: `ReadNative` context cancellation → returns within deadline. - S-12: Timestamp normalization — RFC3339Nano round-trip, byte-identical. - S-13/S-14: Author attribution (env override + default USER@hostname). - S-15: Relation record → `ErrUnsupported`. - S-16: Personal-scope record in `ToCanonical` → error. - S-19: Revision increment on re-import (existing mapping, revision 3 → 4). - S-20: Backward `updated_at` — revision still incremented, warning logged. Use `httptest.NewServer` for HTTP error-mapping paths. No `testing.Short()` needed (no real binary). **Verifiable when**: `go test -short ./internal/adapter/engram/...` passes with all cases. --- ## PR#3 — Integration Tests (~150 lines) > Targets PR#2's branch. Requires `engram >= v1.15` on PATH in CI. ### T-12 · integration_test.go — Real engram binary tests **Files**: `internal/adapter/engram/integration_test.go` **Spec**: S-17, S-18; REQ-ENG-10, REQ-ENG-11; CON-04 **What**: All tests MUST open with `if testing.Short() { t.Skip("requires engram on PATH") }`. Cover: - S-17: `Health(context.Background())` returns nil when `engram` is available. - S-18: `ListNative` with a real project returns non-empty slice; every returned ID is retrievable by `ReadNative`. Tests MUST use `t.TempDir()` for any filesystem state. No fake Commander/Transport here — these test the real exec boundary. **Verifiable when**: `go test -run TestIntegration ./internal/adapter/engram/...` passes with engram on PATH; `go test -short ./internal/adapter/engram/...` skips these tests cleanly. --- ## Cross-Cutting Constraints Checklist - [ ] `internal/store` MUST NOT import `internal/adapter` (CON-01). Verify with `go list -deps` or equivalent after T-03. - [ ] `ProviderIDMap` return type is a concrete `*providerIDMap` that structurally satisfies `adapter.IDMap` without importing the adapter package (CON-01 / design note). - [ ] All `time.Time` usage in timestamps is limited to parse/format at the CLI boundary; no `time.Time` fields in `EngramRecord` or `CanonicalRecord` (REQ-TS-01, REQ-TS-02, binding decision #1). - [ ] `SchemaVersion` is never set by the adapter — store owns it (REQ-ENG-16 table). - [ ] `// TODO(v1.1): import engram relations` comment present at both relation no-op branches in `ToCanonical` and `FromCanonical`. - [ ] `// TODO: tags round-trip` comment present at tags-loss branches in `ToCanonical` and `FromCanonical`. - [ ] S-09 rebuild regression test is NOT guarded by `testing.Short()` (no binary needed — must always run). --- ## Task Summary Table | Task | PR | Files | Sequential / Parallel | |---|---|---|---| | T-01 index.go ByProvider field | PR#1 | internal/store/index.go | Sequential (base) | | T-02 rebuild.go ByProvider repopulation | PR#1 | internal/store/rebuild.go | Sequential after T-01 | | T-03 store.go ProviderIDMap accessor | PR#1 | internal/store/store.go | Sequential after T-01 | | T-04 adapter.go contract | PR#1 | internal/adapter/adapter.go | Parallel with T-01–T-03 | | T-05 store IDMap tests | PR#1 | internal/store/*_test.go | Sequential after T-01–T-03 | | T-06 exec.go Commander | PR#2 | internal/adapter/engram/exec.go | Parallel with T-07, T-08 | | T-07 transport.go Transport | PR#2 | internal/adapter/engram/transport.go | Parallel with T-06, T-08 | | T-08 engram.go struct + constructor | PR#2 | internal/adapter/engram/engram.go | Parallel with T-06, T-07 | | T-09 engram.go pure methods | PR#2 | internal/adapter/engram/engram.go | Sequential after T-04, T-06–T-08 | | T-10 engram.go I/O methods | PR#2 | internal/adapter/engram/engram.go | Sequential after T-09 | | T-11 unit tests | PR#2 | internal/adapter/engram/engram_test.go | Sequential after T-09, T-10 | | T-12 integration tests | PR#3 | internal/adapter/engram/integration_test.go | Sequential after PR#2 merged |

## PR#1 Apply Progress — adapter-contract-engram

- [x] **T-02** — `internal/store/rebuild.go`: Extended `rawRecord` in `record.go` with `Origin rawOrigin` field for rebuild use. In `rebuildFromFile`, tracks `origins` map per canonical_id during scan; tombstones mark `deleted=true` on existing origin entries; after winner selection, builds `byProvider` map (live only) and assigns to returned `index.ByProvider`. Prior on-disk ByProvider fully discarded — full recompute. Satisfies REQ-IDX-04, REQ-IDX-05, REQ-IDX-06.

- [x] **T-03** — `internal/store/store.go`: Added `providerIDMap` struct with `forward` and lazy `reverse` maps; `CanonicalFromNative` and `NativeFromCanonical` methods. `ProviderIDMap(provider string) *providerIDMap` acquires RLock, snapshots inner map, returns non-nil struct (unknown provider → empty forward map → all lookups return false). Satisfies REQ-IDX-07, REQ-IDX-08, CON-01.

- [x] **T-04** — `internal/adapter/adapter.go` (NEW): Declared `NativeID string`, `CanonicalID string`, `NativeRecord any` type aliases; sentinel errors `ErrUnsupported`, `ErrNotFound`, `ErrUnavailable`; `IDMap` interface with `CanonicalFromNative`/`NativeFromCanonical`; `Adapter` interface with all 7 methods. Imports `internal/store` for `store.CanonicalRecord`. Satisfies REQ-AC-01, REQ-AC-02, REQ-AC-03, REQ-AC-08, REQ-AC-09.

- [x] **T-05** — Tests added:
  - `internal/store/index_test.go`: `TestLoadIndex_LegacyNoByProvider_InitializesNonNil` (S-10), `TestProviderIDMap_UnknownProvider_NonNilAndFalse` (S-11)
  - `internal/store/rebuild_test.go`: `TestByProvider_SurvivesStaleIndexRebuild` (S-09, NOT short-guarded) with subtests for live record present, tombstoned record absent, and reverse lookup.

## PR#2 Apply Progress — adapter-contract-engram

**Branch**: feat/adapter-contract-pr2 (stacked on feat/adapter-contract-pr1)
**Commit**: a16ba9d (+ fix commit 138d917 for W-02)

### Tasks Completed (PR#2)

- [x] **T-06** — `internal/adapter/engram/exec.go` (NEW): `Commander` interface with `Run(ctx, args...) (stdout, stderr []byte, err error)`; `execCommander` backed by `exec.CommandContext`; `NewExecCommander()` constructor. Satisfies REQ-ENG-02, REQ-ENG-03.

- [x] **T-07** — `internal/adapter/engram/transport.go` (NEW): `Transport` interface with `GetByID(ctx, NativeID) (EngramRecord, error)`; `httpTransport` targeting `http://127.0.0.1:7437/observations/:id`; context forwarded (no fixed internal timeout); error mapping: conn-refused/timeout/non-2xx → `ErrUnavailable`; 404 → `ErrNotFound`; nil Transport → `ErrUnsupported` at fallback step. Satisfies REQ-ENG-04..REQ-ENG-08.

- [x] **T-08** — `internal/adapter/engram/engram.go` (NEW): `EngramRecord` struct with all string timestamp fields; `EngramAdapter` struct with `commander`/`transport`; `New(Commander, Transport)` constructor; `Name()` returns `"engram"`; `authorAttribution()` helper (WRAPPER_MEMS_AUTHOR env first, then USER@hostname). Satisfies REQ-ENG-01, REQ-ENG-09, REQ-ENG-19, CON-05.

Satisfies REQ-ENG-10..REQ-ENG-23; REQ-TS-01..REQ-TS-03; S-01..S-03, S-12..S-16, S-19, S-20.

- [x] **T-10** — `internal/adapter/engram/engram.go` (continued): `ReadNative` (CLI search by ID → exact match → HTTP Transport.GetByID fallback → ErrNotFound); `WriteNative` (pre-check existing ID via ReadNative → ErrUnsupported if found (v1 no update path); new save via engram save + ID extraction from output or follow-up search). Satisfies REQ-ENG-14, REQ-ENG-15, REQ-ENG-24..REQ-ENG-26; S-04..S-08.

- [x] **T-11** — `internal/adapter/engram/engram_test.go` (NEW): 20 table-driven unit tests using fakes (no real binary). All pass.

- [x] **W-01 boundary wrapper** — `WrapIDMap` in `engram.go`. CON-01 intact.

All tests guarded by `skipIfNoBinary` (calls `testing.Short()` + `exec.LookPath`). Tests: `TestIntegration_Health` (S-17), `TestIntegration_Health_CancelledContext`, `TestIntegration_ListNative_ReturnsIDs` (S-18 partial), `TestIntegration_ListNative_SinceFilter`.
