```
  until HasMore == false  →  []NativeID

ReadNative(ctx, id):
  Transport.GetByID → if found: decode → claudeMemRecord(+Raw)
                       if err==ErrNotFound (clean 404): return ErrNotFound
                       if err==ErrUnavailable (missing/5xx/refused):
                         degrade → paginate ListPage, scan items for id
                           found → record ; exhausted → ErrNotFound
                           transport error during scan → ErrUnavailable

ToCanonical(native, idmap):   [pure, no I/O]
  cast to claudeMemRecord (type-assert; mismatch → error)
  if Title=="" && Summary=="" → WARN log rec.Raw  [forensic]
  title := Title; if "" → derive from first N runes of Summary
  createdAt := normalizeTimestamp(CreatedAt)  (err → reject record)
  updatedAt := normalizeTimestamp(UpdatedAt)
  canonicalID,hasMapping := idmap.CanonicalFromNative(NativeID(ID))  // "" → store mints ULID
  return store.CanonicalRecord{
    CanonicalID: canonicalID, Kind: "session_summary",
    Revision: 1 if new else -1 (shared sentinel; see §6 Revision note),
    Title: title, Content: Summary, ContentFormat: "markdown",
    Type: "", TopicKey: "",  CreatedAt: createdAt, UpdatedAt: updatedAt,
    Tags: Tags,
    Origin: {Provider:"claude-mem", ProviderID: ID, Author: authorAttribution(), SessionID: SessionID},
  }

FromCanonical(canonical):     [pure, implemented, NEVER persisted v1]
  → claudeMemRecord{ Title, Summary:Content, Tags, SessionID:Origin.SessionID }

WriteNative(ctx, record):     return "", adapter.ErrUnsupported

Health(ctx):  Transport.Health → nil | wrap adapter.ErrUnavailable
```

Revision (DECISION 2026-05-18, supersedes prior "Revision:1 always"): claude-mem uses the SAME shared cross-adapter sentinel as the engram adapter. New record → `Revision: 1`. Known record (prior IDMap mapping) → `Revision: -1`, a sentinel signalling the PRD-3 orchestrator to replace it with `priorRevision+1` on re-ingest. The adapter stays pure (no store read — IDMap carries only the ID mapping, not the existing revision, the W-03 trap), and PRD-3 handles engram and claude-mem through ONE uniform `revision < 0` branch. Cross-adapter contract consistency is the whole point of the adapter abstraction; this was chosen over the simpler 'always 1' precisely so PRD-3 has a single revision code path.

## 7. Port Resolution (resolveBaseURL)

```go
type supervisorConfig struct {
    Port int `json:"port,omitempty"` // PROVISIONAL: supervisor.json schema unverified
}
func resolveBaseURL(explicit string) string {
    if explicit != "" { return explicit }                 // 1. explicit arg
    if p := readSupervisorPort(); p != 0 {                 // 2. ~/.claude-mem/supervisor.json
        return fmt.Sprintf("http://localhost:%d", p)
    }
    return "http://localhost:37701"                        // 3. hardcoded fallback
}
func readSupervisorPort() int {
    // os.UserHomeDir() + "/.claude-mem/supervisor.json"; ReadFile; json.Unmarshal
    // ANY error (missing file, bad JSON, port==0) → return 0 (caller falls through).
    // NEVER returns an error; NEVER panics; construction never fails on this.
}
```

`supervisorConfig` is intentionally minimal and tolerant: unknown extra keys in supervisor.json are ignored by `encoding/json`; a malformed file degrades to the hardcoded default. Failure mode is silent fallback by design (ADR-3) — a peripheral discovery file must never block adapter construction.

## 8. Error Mapping

| Condition | Mapped to |
|-----------|-----------|
| Connection refused / timeout / context deadline (Health/List/Get) | `adapter.ErrUnavailable` (context.Canceled / DeadlineExceeded propagated raw, same as engram) |
| Non-2xx (except 404 on GetByID) | `adapter.ErrUnavailable` |
| `GET /api/observations/:id` → 404 | `adapter.ErrNotFound` (clean) |
| ReadNative scan exhausted, id absent | `adapter.ErrNotFound` |
| `WriteNative` (any input) | `adapter.ErrUnsupported` |
| `ToCanonical` wrong native type / unparseable timestamp | wrapped descriptive error (not a sentinel) |
| `nil` Transport | `New` still constructs; method calls return `adapter.ErrUnavailable` (List/Read/Health) — no nil-panic |

## 9. Forensic Logging

- **First observation raw JSON**: in `ListNative`, the FIRST decoded item per call logs `log.Printf("claude-mem adapter: first observation raw JSON: %s", rec.Raw)` at INFO. This is the artifact the pre-merge gate inspects to confirm field names.
- **Empty title+summary WARN**: in `ToCanonical`, if `Title=="" && Summary==""`, `log.Printf("claude-mem adapter: WARN observation id=%q has empty title and summary; raw=%s", rec.ID, rec.Raw)` — signals a field-name mismatch (the provisional JSON tags didn't bind).
- **Unparseable timestamp WARN**: in `ListNative`, `log.Printf("claude-mem adapter: WARN skipping obs %q unparseable updated_at %q: %v", ...)` then skip (DEFECT-LN-01 discipline: skip, never store unnormalized).

## 10. Testing Strategy

| Layer | What | Approach |
|-------|------|----------|
| Unit | `ToCanonical` purity, kind=session_summary, title-derivation, provider=claude-mem, REQ-TS-03 byte-comparable | Table-driven, `fakeTransport` canned provisional JSON; assert zero transport calls in pure fns |
| Unit | `ListNative` pagination loop (multi-page, HasMore), strict project filter, since filter, cross-project-leakage negative case | `fakeTransport` returning scripted pages |
| Unit | `ReadNative` per-ID happy path; degrade-to-scan when GetByID returns ErrUnavailable; ErrNotFound on clean 404 and on exhausted scan | `fakeTransport` with toggleable GetByID behavior |
| Unit | Error mapping (refused/timeout/non-2xx/404 → sentinels); `WriteNative`→ErrUnsupported; nil Transport no-panic | `fakeTransport` + `httptest.Server` for real-ish paths |
| Unit | Timestamp tolerance: RFC3339, RFC3339Nano, SQLite layout all normalize equal; garbage → skip/reject | table cases |
| Unit | `WrapIDMap` boundary cast + reverse direction | small fixtures |
| Unit | `resolveBaseURL` precedence; malformed supervisor.json → fallback, no error | `t.TempDir()` fake HOME |
| Integration | Real worker `Health`/`ListNative`/`ReadNative`; **dump first observation raw JSON** | `//go:build integration` / `testing.Short()` skip; **TestPreMergeGate_DumpFirstObservation discharges the HARD pre-merge gate** |

The integration test is the mechanism that satisfies the pre-merge field-mapping gate: it must run against a live claude-mem instance with ≥1 observation, print the raw JSON, and a human (or the test, once shape is known) reconfirms the §5 tags before apply/merge.

## 11. Build Sequence

Single self-contained PR (~350-450 lines, all new files, zero contract/store edits). No chained-PR dependency since nothing else compiles against it yet (PRD-3 wires it). Order within the PR: (1) `transport.go` (Transport iface + httpTransport + resolveBaseURL), (2) `claudemem.go` (record + adapter + copied helpers), (3) `claudemem_test.go`, (4) `integration_test.go`. `sdd-tasks` will sequence; if it exceeds the 400-line budget, split `integration_test.go` into a follow-up PR (feature-branch chain), mirroring PRD-1's PR#2/PR#3 contingency.

## 12. Rollback

Isolated new package, read-only, zero edits to `internal/store` or the `internal/adapter` contract. Rollback = `rm -r internal/adapter/claudemem/`. No data migration, no JSONL/index format change, nothing written to the canonical store until PRD-3 wires it in. Near-zero blast radius.

## 13. Open Questions — RESOLVED

- explore Q2 (per-ID endpoint exists?) → **ADR-5**: try per-ID, degrade to paginate+scan; integration test verifies.
- explore Q3 (timestamp format?) → **ADR-6**: tolerant multi-layout parse (RFC3339Nano/RFC3339/SQLite); unknown → skip+warn / reject.
- explore Q5 (authorAttribution extract vs duplicate) → **ADR-8**: duplicate (contract pkg frozen, out of scope to edit).
- proposal HARD gate (JSON shape unverified) → **§5 PROVISIONAL tags + §9 forensic log + §10 integration test discharges gate**. Still a SPEC-LEVEL blocker for apply/merge — design does not and cannot resolve it without a live instance.

## 14. Constraints Honored

- CON-01: store never imports adapter; IDMap consumed via copied `WrapIDMap` boundary wrapper.
- Contract pkg `internal/adapter` reused VERBATIM — zero churn.
- Faithful engram mirror; all 6 intentional divergences (D1-D6) explicitly documented in §1/§2.

title: sdd/claude-mem-adapter/tasks
content:
# Tasks: claude-mem-adapter (PRD-2)

**Change**: claude-mem-adapter
**Delivery strategy**: ask-on-risk → 4 chained PRs recommended (stacked-to-main)
**Slice layout**: PR#1 (package skeleton + Transport seam + port resolution) → PR#2 (native record struct + ToCanonical/FromCanonical + helpers + unit tests) → PR#3 (ListNative pagination + project/since filter + Health + ReadNative + unit tests) → PR#4 (integration test — pre-merge gate)

---

## Review Workload Forecast

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Estimated changed lines | ~750 total (PR#1 ~130, PR#2 ~200, PR#3 ~230, PR#4 ~90) |
| 400-line budget risk | Low per PR; Medium if PR#2+PR#3 collapsed |
| Chained PRs recommended | Yes (4 PRs) |
| Suggested slice boundaries | PR#1 ~130 lines · PR#2 ~200 lines · PR#3 ~230 lines · PR#4 ~90 lines |
| Decision needed before apply | Yes — confirm 4-PR split or collapse PR#1+PR#2 (~330 lines, within budget) |

**Rationale for 4 slices**: The design (§11) noted ~350-450 lines with a possible contingency split of integration_test.go. Mirroring PRD-1's 3-PR approach but splitting the production code into two PRs (skeleton first, then data+behavior) keeps each review focused and within the ~400-line budget. PR#4 (integration test) is the hard pre-merge gate and MUST land before merge approval regardless of ordering.

---

## PR#1 — Package Skeleton + Transport Seam + Port Resolution (~130 lines) ✅ DONE (commit a8b0d1d)

> Zero application behavior. Just the package boundary, the Transport interface, httpTransport, and resolveBaseURL. Compiles and tests pass at the end of this PR.

### [x] T-01 · transport.go — Transport interface declaration
### [x] T-02 · transport.go — supervisorConfig + readSupervisorPort + resolveBaseURL
### [x] T-03 · transport.go — httpTransport struct + NewHTTPTransport + method implementations
### [x] T-04 · claudemem.go — package file + compile-time interface assertion
### [x] T-05 · claudemem_test.go — PR#1 unit tests (Transport + port resolution)

---

## PR#2 — Native Record + ToCanonical/FromCanonical + Helpers + Unit Tests (~200 lines) ✅ DONE (commit 7d32adb)

> Pure data-layer: all pure functions and all copied helper utilities. No I/O methods implemented yet.

### [x] T-06 · claudemem.go — authorAttribution + providerIDMapWrapper + WrapIDMap (verbatim copies, ADR-7/ADR-8)
### [x] T-07 · claudemem.go — rfc3339NanoFixed + normalizeTimestamp + tolerant multi-layout parse (ADR-6)
### [x] T-08 · claudemem.go — deriveTitle (REQ-CM-12)
### [x] T-09 · claudemem.go — WriteNative (single line ErrUnsupported) + FromCanonical (pure, ADR-10)
### [x] T-10 · claudemem.go — ToCanonical (pure) with full REQ-CM-11 mapping
### [x] T-11 · claudemem_test.go — PR#2 unit tests (27 tests: pure functions, panicTransport, fakeIDMap)

---

## PR#3 — ListNative + Health + ReadNative + Unit Tests (~230 lines) ✅ DONE (commit 55e2c8a)

### [x] T-12 · claudemem.go — Health (delegate to Transport)
### [x] T-13 · claudemem.go — ListNative with full pagination + project filter + since filter
### [x] T-14 · claudemem.go — ReadNative with degrade-to-scan
### [x] T-15 · claudemem_test.go — PR#3 unit tests (I/O methods, 20 new tests via fakeTransport)

---

## PR#4 — Integration Test (Pre-Merge Gate) (~245 lines) ✅ DONE (commit 6a08c1e)

### [x] T-16 · integration_test.go — TestPreMergeGate_Health + TestPreMergeGate_DumpFirstObservation (//go:build integration guarded; t.Skip when unreachable; pins verified shape from obs #82; asserts id is numeric NOT string; asserts 5 known-absent keys absent; logs first item raw JSON)
### [x] T-17 · Pre-merge gate checklist task — RECORDED in apply-progress #80. Gate verdict: FAILED / NOT DISCHARGED (2026-05-19). Chain MUST NOT merge until rework cycle completes.

---

## Cross-Cutting Constraints Checklist

- [x] `internal/store` MUST NOT import `internal/adapter/claudemem` or `internal/adapter` — CON-01.
- [x] `go.mod` MUST NOT gain new entries — CON-02. `net/http`, `encoding/json`, `os` are stdlib.
- [x] `var _ adapter.Adapter = (*ClaudeMemAdapter)(nil)` compile-time assertion present — REQ-CM-01, CON-03.
- [x] All `// PROVISIONAL` json tags in `claudeMemRecord` carry the `// PRE-MERGE GATE` comment — ADR-4.
- [x] `normalizeTimestamp` + `rfc3339NanoFixed` copied verbatim from engram adapter — CON-05.
- [x] `WriteNative` is a single-line return, no I/O — REQ-CM-03.
- [x] `ToCanonical` and `FromCanonical` have no transport calls (proven by panic-on-call fake in T-11) — REQ-CM-10, REQ-CM-15.
- [x] `SchemaVersion` is NOT set by the adapter — store owns it (REQ-CM-11).
- [x] `Kind` is ALWAYS `"session_summary"` — ADR-9, REQ-CM-11.
- [x] `Origin.Provider` is ALWAYS `"claude-mem"` — REQ-CM-02, REQ-CM-11.
- [x] Nil Transport → no panic; methods return `ErrUnavailable` — design §8.
- [x] Integration tests behind `//go:build integration` — CON-04.
- [x] Pre-merge gate T-17 checklist ticked in apply-progress before merge approval — REQ-CM-20. VERDICT: FAILED.

title: PRD-1 fast-forwarded into master; PRD-2 stacks on clean master
content:
**What**: PRD-1 (adapter-contract-engram) was archived but its 4 stacked PRs lived only on feat/adapter-contract-pr4, never merged. Before starting PRD-2 apply, fast-forward merged feat/adapter-contract-pr4 → master (0 behind / 7 ahead → clean linear ff, e4ac4b5). master now has internal/adapter/adapter.go + engram adapter. Created feat/claude-mem-adapter-pr1 off clean master for PRD-2 PR#1.

**Why**: PRD-2 claudemem adapter implements internal/adapter/adapter.go and mirrors the engram adapter — code that only existed on pr4. User chose "merge PRD-1 to master first" so PRD-2 stacks on a clean base (stacked-to-main).

**Where**: master branch; feat/claude-mem-adapter-pr1.

**Learned**: PRD-1's archive-report claimed "integrated into production branch" but that branch was the chain tracker feat/adapter-contract-pr4, NOT master — archive did not perform the master merge. Verified pr4 green (go build/test/vet all pass) before merging. Also: this session's skill-registry refresh tooling dirtied tracked .atl/ files (auto-generated, discarded); an untracked README.md (213 lines, real project doc, not authored by this session) remains untracked and unmerged — flagged, left for user decision.

- [x] T-10 · ToCanonical: full REQ-CM-11 field mapping, IDMap resolution, revision sentinel -1 for known records (aligned with engram adapter, CRITICAL-02 fix); CONTRACT CONFLICT comment now RESOLVED per Decision B (design §6/ADR-12 obs #77 + spec REQ-CM-11/14 obs #76 reconciled)

## Post-review fixes applied to PR#2 (commit 163d496 — amended from dc6b8d5)

- CRITICAL-01: normalizeTimestamp now accumulates lastErr across layout attempts and returns it with %w, making the error chain unwrappable for errors.Is/As. TestNormalizeTimestamp_UnknownFormat_ReturnsError strengthened with errors.Unwrap assertion.
- CRITICAL-02: revision sentinel changed from 2 → -1 for known records, matching engram adapter (internal/adapter/engram/engram.go ~378). TestToCanonical_IDMap_KnownRecord updated to assert revision == -1.
- WARNING-01: Fixed reversed strings.HasPrefix args in TestToCanonical_TitleDeriveFallback. Added TestToCanonical_TitleTruncatesLongSummary testing the >titleMaxRunes path explicitly.
- WARNING-02: Added `var _ Transport = panicTransport{}` compile-time interface guard immediately after the panicTransport type declaration.
- WARNING-03: Added note on WriteNative stub and FromCanonical doc comment: future write paths must re-read CreatedAt/UpdatedAt from provider, not round-trip from FromCanonical. Added TestFromCanonical_OmitsTimestamps asserting both timestamp fields are empty.

## Decision B amendment to PR#2 (2026-05-18, commit 7d32adb)

- RESOLVED 2026-05-18 — Decision B: shared `-1` sentinel, design #77 (ADR-12/§6) + spec #76 (REQ-CM-11/14, Scenarios I/K) reconciled, code amended (CONTRACT CONFLICT comment replaced with RESOLVED DECISION comment), no open conflict.
- claudemem.go: CONTRACT CONFLICT comment block replaced with "DECISION 2026-05-18 (RESOLVED)" comment referencing ADR-12 (obs #77) and REQ-CM-11/14 (obs #76).
- New amended SHA: 7d32adb — build/vet/test confirmed clean (go build/vet/test all green across full repo)

---

## Tasks completed (PR#3)

- [x] T-12 · Health: delegates to Transport.Health; wraps failure as adapter.ErrUnavailable; propagates context.Canceled/DeadlineExceeded raw; nil transport → ErrUnavailable immediately (REQ-CM-04, Scenario F, design §8).
- [x] T-13 · ListNative: full pagination loop (limit=100, offset increments), decodes observationsPage + claudeMemRecord (+Raw), logs first obs raw JSON at INFO (§9/REQ-CM-17), strict ProjectID==project filter (REQ-CM-07/ADR-11), normalizedUpdatedAt>=sinceStr filter (REQ-CM-08), unparseable updated_at → WARN+skip (ADR-6/REQ-CM-13), context cancellation aborts and returns error (REQ-CM-06). Empty-page guard prevents infinite loop on misbehaving server (W-1 fix).
- [x] T-14 · ReadNative: ADR-5 — primary GetByID; clean ErrNotFound → return ErrNotFound; ErrUnavailable → degrade to paginate+scan; found in scan → return record (+Raw); scan exhausted → ErrNotFound; transport error during scan → ErrUnavailable. Empty-page guard in scan loop (W-1 fix).

Prevents infinite loop when a misbehaving server returns HasMore:true with empty Items. Added regression test `TestListNative_EmptyPageWithHasMoreTrue_Terminates` (bounded fake: 1 page with HasMore:true+empty Items, asserts terminates with 0 ids and exactly 1 ListPage call).
- W-4: Strengthened `TestListNative_ContextCancellation_Aborts` — now asserts `errors.Is(err, context.Canceled)` instead of bare `err != nil`. Fake scripted to return `context.Canceled`, deterministic, no goroutine races.
- W-5: Added `getByIDReceivedID string` field to `fakeTransport`; set inside `GetByID(_ ctx, id string)`. `TestReadNative_HappyPath_PerID` now asserts `ft.getByIDReceivedID == "obs-1"`. Compile-time `var _ Transport = (*fakeTransport)(nil)` guard remains valid.

Spec #76 updated with revised REQ-CM-11/14 and Scenarios I/K. Code comment at claudemem.go revision assignment site updated from CONTRACT CONFLICT to RESOLVED DECISION. Decision saved as obs #81. PRD-3 handles -1 from both adapters through a single uniform `revision < 0` branch.

title: Decision B: claude-mem revision uses shared -1 sentinel (engram-consistent)
content:
**What**: claude-mem ToCanonical sets Revision=1 for new records / Revision=-1 sentinel for known records, identical to the engram adapter. PRD-3 orchestrator replaces -1 with priorRevision+1 on re-ingest.

**Why**: spec REQ-CM-11 originally said `existing.Revision+1` is uncomputable at the pure adapter layer — the IDMap interface exposes only the ID mapping (CanonicalFromNative → canonicalID + hasMapping), NOT the existing revision. Attempting to compute it would require a store read, violating REQ-CM-10 purity (the W-03 trap). Chose cross-adapter uniformity over the design's earlier "always 1" so PRD-3 has one single revision code path for ALL adapters via `revision < 0` branch.

**Where**: `internal/adapter/claudemem/claudemem.go` ToCanonical (comment updated); design #77 §6 (revised) + ADR-12 (added); spec #76 REQ-CM-11 Revision row (updated), REQ-CM-14 (rewritten), Scenarios I (updated) and K (updated); apply-progress #80 (CONTRACT CONFLICT closed, RESOLVED recorded).

**Learned**: engram is the reference convention — the adapter contract's value is cross-adapter uniformity. The original design §6 divergence ("always 1") created a hidden 2-vs-(-1) inconsistency that a fresh review (CRITICAL-02) surfaced. The W-03 trap is the definitive reason: IDMap carries ID→CanonicalID only, never the existing revision. Any adapter attempting to compute revision+1 would need a store read and break the pure-function guarantee. The -1 sentinel pattern is the correct abstraction.

title: PRE-MERGE GATE TRIPPED: real claude-mem /api/observations JSON shape contradicts provisional mapping
content:
**What**: Probed the LIVE claude-mem v13 worker (:37701, supervisor.json had NO `port` field — schema is `{processes:{worker:{pid}}}`, graceful fallback to :37701 worked). `GET /api/observations?limit=2` returned the REAL item shape, which contradicts the provisional `claudeMemRecord` (design §5 / spec REQ-CM-11) in nearly every field.

**Why**: PR#4 (T-16/T-17) pre-merge gate. The gate exists precisely to catch this before merge.

**Where**: internal/adapter/claudemem/claudemem.go (claudeMemRecord struct + ToCanonical); spec #76 REQ-CM-07/08/11; design #77 §5/§6/§7/ADR-3/ADR-11.

**Real shape (item)**: `id`(NUMBER int, e.g. 252), `memory_session_id`(uuid), `project`(NAME e.g. "wrapper-mems", not path), `merged_into_project`, `platform_source`, `type`("discovery" — claude-mem HAS a type vocab), `title`, `subtitle`, `narrative`(the body), `text`(null), `facts`(json-string array), `concepts`(json-string), `files_read`, `files_modified`, `prompt_number`, `created_at`(RFC3339 ms string "2026-05-19T16:05:52.876Z"), `created_at_epoch`(int ms). Envelope: `{items,hasMore,offset,limit}` — hasMore camelCase ✓.

**Mismatches vs provisional**: id string→NUMBER (json.Unmarshal HARD BREAK); session_id→memory_session_id; project_id(path)→project(name); summary→narrative; tags→ABSENT (concepts/facts closest, json-string); updated_at→ABSENT (only created_at); type assumed-empty but is populated. Only `title`, `created_at` (as string), `hasMore` survived.

**Learned / architectural implications**:
1. `updated_at` does not exist → spec REQ-CM-08 incremental `since` filter and revision-on-updated_at premise are INVALID. Must rebase on `created_at` (or created_at_epoch) — claude-mem observations appear immutable/append-only (no update field), which actually simplifies revision (no bump-on-update). Architecture-level, needs spec+design revision.
2. `id` is numeric → struct field must be json.Number/int then stringified for NativeID/ProviderID; REQ-CM-11 1:1 mapping needs adjustment.
3. `project` is the project NAME not a filesystem path → ADR-11/REQ-CM-07 strict-equality filter still works but the PRD-2 §3 "project path→name mapping" open question dissolves (claude-mem already uses names). Good news.
4. claude-mem HAS a type vocab ("discovery") → spec REQ-CM-11 "Type empty" is wrong; could map claude-mem `type`→canonical `type`. Decision needed (PRD-2 §6.1 kept streams disjoint under kind=session_summary; revisit).
5. supervisor.json has no `port` → design §7 supervisorConfig{Port} dead code; ADR-3 fallback saved it. Worth a spec/design note.
GATE VERDICT: provisional mapping is substantially wrong; chain MUST NOT merge until claudeMemRecord struct (PR#1) + ToCanonical (PR#2) + spec REQ-CM-07/08/11 + design §5/§6/§7 are reworked against this verified shape. PR#4 integration test should pin this real shape.

title: Session summary: wrapper-mems
content:
## Goal
Run the SDD cycle for PRD-2 (claude-mem read-only adapter) of wrapper-mems in Go. All SDD artifacts in engram (no repo pollution). Config: interactive mode, engram-only, ask-on-risk delivery, stacked-to-main chain, strict_tdd=false (sdd-init #45).

## Instructions

- Surface architecture conflicts, never silently override deliberate design decisions.
- .atl/ skill-registry files are auto-generated noise → discard each session; untracked README.md (213 lines, not authored here) left untouched.
- Conventional commits, NO AI attribution.

## Discoveries
- **PRD-1 was archived but NEVER merged to master** — lived only on feat/adapter-contract-pr4. Fast-forwarded to master (e4ac4b5) before PRD-2 so PRD-2 stacks on a clean base.
- **CONTRACT CONFLICT (resolved, Decision B)**: spec REQ-CM-11 `existing.Revision+1` is uncomputable at the pure adapter (IDMap lacks revision — W-03 trap). design §6 said "Revision:1 always"; engram uses -1 sentinel. User chose B: shared -1 sentinel (engram-consistent), PRD-3 computes priorRevision+1. spec #76 + design #77 + code reconciled; decision saved obs #81.
- **PRE-MERGE GATE TRIPPED (obs #82)**: live claude-mem v13 worker (:37701) `/api/observations` real shape contradicts provisional mapping in nearly every field. `id`=NUMBER (not string → json.Unmarshal breaks), `session_id`→`memory_session_id`, `project_id`(path)→`project`(name), `summary`→`narrative`, `tags`→ABSENT, `updated_at`→ABSENT (only `created_at`), `type` populated (claude-mem HAS a type vocab). Only title/created_at(string)/hasMore survived. supervisor.json has NO `port` field (schema is {processes:{worker:{pid}}}) — ADR-3 fallback to :37701 held.

## Accomplished
- Full SDD planning: explore #74, propose #75, spec #76, design #77 (11 ADRs + ADR-12), tasks #78 (17 tasks, 4-PR split).

- PR#2 `feat/claude-mem-adapter-pr2` @ 7d32adb — ToCanonical/FromCanonical, helpers, 30 tests. Review caught 2 CRITICAL + 3 WARN, fixed. Decision-B reconciliation amended in.
- PR#3 `feat/claude-mem-adapter-pr3` @ 55e2c8a — Health, ListNative (pagination+filters), ReadNative (degrade-to-scan), 20 tests. Review caught 3 WARN (W-1 infinite-loop guard most material), fixed. 58 tests total.
- PR#4 `feat/claude-mem-adapter-pr4` @ 6a08c1e — integration_test.go (//go:build integration), pins verified shape, ran live against worker confirming every mismatch. Gate marked FAILED in #80.
- All go build/vet/test green throughout; CON-01 respected (zero edits to adapter.go / internal/store).

## Next Steps
- **REWORK CYCLE (gate FAILED — chain pr1..pr4 MUST NOT merge)**: against obs #82 verified shape:
  1. Rewrite `claudeMemRecord` struct (id as json.Number/int, memory_session_id, project, narrative, type, created_at, drop tags/updated_at) — touches PR#1 territory.
  2. Rewrite `ToCanonical` mapping (PR#2): narrative→content, memory_session_id→origin.session_id, project(name)→filter, no updated_at → revision simplifies (claude-mem obs appear immutable, no bump-on-update), decide claude-mem `type`→canonical type vs keep empty (PRD-2 §6.1 disjoint-streams revisit).
  3. Revise spec #76: REQ-CM-07 (project is name not path — PRD-2 §3 mapping dissolves), REQ-CM-08 (no updated_at → rebase since-filter on created_at), REQ-CM-11 (field names + type vocab exists).
  4. Revise design #77: §5 native record, §6 data flow, §7 supervisor.json (no port field — note ADR-3 saved it), add ADR for no-updated_at revision model.
  5. Re-run integration test to discharge gate; then sdd-verify + sdd-archive.
- This is a fresh SDD rework, well-scoped, with correct data now pinned in integration_test.go + obs #82.

## Relevant Files
