title: PRD-2 rework decisions: type→Type, since=created_at, narrative→Content, tags=[]
content:
**What**: Contract decisions for claude-mem adapter rework (discharge of pre-merge gate, obs #82). User confirmed 1A+2A+3A+Tags=[]:

- **D1 (type mapping)**: `Kind="session_summary"` SIEMPRE (ADR-9 intacto); `Type = rec.Type` (preservar vocab claude-mem: discovery/feature/bugfix/decision/session_summary/... como sub-clasificación dentro del Kind).
- **D2 (revision sin updated_at)**: since-filter sobre `created_at` (records claude-mem son append-only, no hay updated_at). Revision sentinel = -1 si IDMap tiene mapping, 1 si nuevo (preserva uniformidad cross-adapter con engram, PRD-3 tiene una sola rama `revision<0`).
- **D3 (content mapping)**: `narrative` → `Content` (único). subtitle/facts/concepts/files_* solo en `Raw` para forensics. v1 scope limpio; PRD-3 decide si surface el resto.
- **D4 (tags)**: `Tags: []` siempre. No hay tags nativos en claude-mem; sintetizar de facts/concepts sería ficción.

**Why**: Live worker re-probado 2026-05-20 confirmó shape: id=number, memory_session_id, project (name), platform_source, type (con vocab), title, subtitle, narrative, text, facts/concepts/files_*=stringified-JSON, prompt_number, created_at, created_at_epoch. NO: updated_at, tags, session_id, project_id, summary. Decisiones minimizan riesgo y mantienen ADR-9 + ADR-12 (revision sentinel compartido).

**Where**: internal/adapter/claudemem/claudemem.go (struct + ToCanonical + ListNative since-filter), claudemem_test.go (58 tests pinneados al shape provisional), integration_test.go (gate discharger). Spec #76 y design #77 a actualizar.

**Learned**: claude-mem facts/concepts/files_* son STRINGS JSON-encoded (no arrays Go nativos) — gotcha si futuro PRD-3 los quiere parsear. UpdatedAt en canonical = CreatedAt (records inmutables) — decisión de modelado.

title: claude-mem per-ID endpoint is /api/observations?id=N (query param), NOT /api/observations/:id
content:
**What**: claude-mem v13 worker does NOT expose a path-style per-ID endpoint. `GET /api/observations/273` returns Express's "Cannot GET" 404. The real per-ID lookup is `GET /api/observations?id=273` which returns the standard list envelope `{items:[record], hasMore, offset, limit}` filtered to that id.

**Why**: Discovered during PRD-2 rework gate-discharge testing. ADR-5 originally treated `/api/observations/:id` 404s as ErrNotFound definitive, but those 404s are "route doesn't exist" not "record doesn't exist" — caused ReadNative to fail on IDs that ListNative had just returned. Fix encapsulates the API quirk in transport.GetByID: hits `?id=N`, unwraps the envelope, treats empty items[] as ErrNotFound and 404 as ErrUnavailable (route missing → degrade to scan).

**Where**: `internal/adapter/claudemem/transport.go` GetByID (~lines 156-220 after rework). Unit tests updated: TestHTTPTransport_GetByIDOK uses envelope fixture, TestHTTPTransport_GetByIDNotFound returns empty items, new TestHTTPTransport_GetByIDRouteMissing covers the literal 404 path.

**Learned**: claude-mem's "API" is just the list endpoint with optional filters. There is no per-resource REST surface. Future filters (memory_session_id, type, project) likely follow the same pattern — all on /api/observations with query params. Worth probing before assuming a path-style endpoint exists.

title: sdd/claude-mem-adapter/rework-summary — gate discharged 2026-05-20
content:
# Rework Summary: claude-mem-adapter (PRD-2 gate discharge)

**Branch**: `fix/claude-mem-adapter-mapping` (off master @798d242)
**Gate verdict**: DISCHARGED (was FAILED, obs #82)
**Tests**: unit 58 pass; integration 3/3 pass against live claude-mem v13 worker (:37701)

## Delta vs design #77 / spec #76

### claudeMemRecord (§5) — verified live 2026-05-20
Replaced provisional fields with verified shape:
- `ID int64` (was string) — primary hard-break fix
- `MemorySessionID` (was `SessionID` mapped to `session_id`)
- `Project` (was `ProjectID` mapped to `project_id`) — name, not path
- `Type` populated (was assumed absent)
- `Narrative` (was `Summary` mapped to `summary`)
- Added `Subtitle`, `CreatedAtEpoch`, `PromptNumber` (forensic, decoded but not surfaced)
- **DROPPED**: `UpdatedAt`, `Tags` — do NOT exist in real API

### ToCanonical (§6) — decisions 1A + 2A + 3A + Tags=[] (obs #84)
- `narrative` → `Content` (sole content source — decision 3A)
- `type` → `Type` (claude-mem vocab pass-through — decision 1A); `Kind` stays `"session_summary"` (ADR-9 invariant)
- `created_at` → both `CreatedAt` AND `UpdatedAt` (mirror; records append-only — D7)
- `Tags: []` always (decision D4)
- Title fallback: Title → Subtitle → derived from Narrative
- Revision sentinel unchanged: -1 / 1 (ADR-12 holds)
- ProviderID = strconv int64 → string

### ListNative (§6) — D7 rebase
- Since-filter rebased from `updated_at` to `created_at`
- Strict project equality on `rec.Project` (was `rec.ProjectID`)

### ReadNative + transport.GetByID (§7, ADR-5)
- **New discovery (obs #85)**: per-ID endpoint is `GET /api/observations?id=N` (query param). The path-style `/api/observations/:id` returns Express "Cannot GET" 404.
- transport.GetByID now unwraps the envelope `{items:[...]}`:
  - 1 item → `(body, true, nil)`
  - 0 items → `ErrNotFound` (clean — definitive)
  - 404 (route missing) → `ErrUnavailable` → degrade to scan
  - >1 items → `ErrUnavailable` (impossible per claude-mem semantics)

### Spec deltas (#76)
- REQ-CM-07: filter source is `project` (name), not `project_id`
- REQ-CM-08: since filter is `created_at` (D7) — `updated_at` does not exist
- REQ-CM-11: field-name mapping updated as above
- REQ-CM-14: Revision sentinel unchanged
- D5 marker removed (provisional gate discharged); D7 added (no-updated_at)

## Pre-merge state
- All `go test` green: unit + integration + project-wide
- All `go vet` clean (integration tag included)
- Branch contains 1 logical commit pending; ready for PR to master

## Carry-forward to PRD-3
- Revision sentinel `-1` → priorRevision+1 still owned by orchestrator
- claude-mem `text`, `facts`, `concepts`, `files_read`, `files_modified` (stringified JSON) live in Raw — PRD-3 may surface
- `subtitle`, `created_at_epoch`, `prompt_number` likewise available via Raw

title: Session summary: wrapper-mems
content:
## Goal
Discharge pre-merge gate of PRD-2 (claude-mem adapter) — the integration test pinneado en obs #82 reportaba FAILED porque el provisional `claudeMemRecord` no matcheaba el shape JSON real del worker live. Master tenía el adapter mergeado pero con mapping roto (deuda técnica explícita).

## Decisions
- **1A**: claude-mem `type` (discovery/feature/bugfix/decision/...) → canonical `Type` (pass-through). `Kind` permanece "session_summary" (ADR-9 intacto).
- **2A**: Since-filter rebased on `created_at` (claude-mem es append-only, no hay `updated_at`); revision sentinel -1/1 sin cambios (ADR-12).
- **3A**: `narrative` → `Content` (única fuente). Subtitle/facts/concepts/files_* sólo en Raw (forensic).
- **D4 Tags=[]**: claude-mem no tiene tags nativos; sintetizar sería ficción.

## Discoveries
- **Worker shape verificado live 2026-05-20** (obs #85 ya existente del previo): id=int64, memory_session_id, project (name), type vocab, narrative, created_at, created_at_epoch. NO updated_at, NO tags, NO session_id, NO project_id, NO summary. facts/concepts/files_* son strings JSON-encoded.
- **per-ID endpoint NO es path-style** (NEW, obs #85 nuevo): `GET /api/observations/:id` → Express "Cannot GET" 404. Real: `GET /api/observations?id=N` retorna envelope `{items:[record],...}`. transport.GetByID ahora unwrappea: 1 item → body, 0 items → ErrNotFound, 404 → ErrUnavailable (route missing → degrade to scan).

## Accomplished
- Rewrote `claudeMemRecord` struct (int64 id, MemorySessionID/Project/Type/Narrative, drop UpdatedAt/Tags/etc.)
- Rewrote `ToCanonical` (decisiones 1A+2A+3A+Tags=[], title fallback Title→Subtitle→Narrative)
- Rewrote `ListNative` (since-filter en created_at, project name equality)
- Updated `FromCanonical` (preserve Type, drop fields no longer in struct)
- Fixed `transport.GetByID` (real `?id=N` endpoint with envelope unwrap)
- Rewrote unit tests (~60 tests, all green) including new TestToCanonical_TypePassThrough, _Tags_AlwaysEmpty, _TitleDeriveFromSubtitle, TestHTTPTransport_GetByIDRouteMissing
- Replaced integration_test.go with positive gate-discharge tests (HealthOK + ShapeContract + AdapterEndToEnd) — 3/3 green contra worker live (probe project="wrapper-mems" id=273)
- Saved 3 obs: rework-decisions #84, per-ID endpoint discovery #85, rework-summary #86
- Commit `7860638` on fix/claude-mem-adapter-mapping → fast-forward merged to master (master HEAD = 7860638)

## Next Steps
- **PRD-3 (orchestrator)** ya es abordable — el contrato claude-mem está válido contra el adapter real. Tiene que consumir la sentinel revision=-1 → priorRevision+1, federación de adapters (engram + claude-mem), y eventualmente decidir si surface subtitle/facts/concepts de Raw.
- Spec #76 / design #77 NO fueron editados en engram (overwrite cost vs. resumen acumulativo en obs #86); si PRD-3 los necesita expandir, leer #86 primero.
- No remote git configurado — los merges siguen siendo locales fast-forward.

## Relevant Files
- internal/adapter/claudemem/claudemem.go — struct, ToCanonical, ListNative, ReadNative, FromCanonical
- internal/adapter/claudemem/claudemem_test.go — ~60 unit tests
- internal/adapter/claudemem/transport.go — GetByID fix (?id=N envelope)
- internal/adapter/claudemem/integration_test.go — gate discharge proof
- engram: obs #82 (gate trip), #84 (rework decisions), #85 (per-ID endpoint), #86 (rework summary)

title: sdd/sync-engine/explore
content:
## Exploration: sync-engine (PRD-3) ### Current State The project has a fully-built storage and adapter layer (PRDs 0-2) but ZERO orchestration glue. `cmd/` contains only `cmd/debug-store/main.go`, a throwaway smoke-test tool that directly calls `store.Open` — there is no `cmd/wrapper-mems/` entrypoint, no cobra/bubbletea wiring, no `init`, `sync`, `status`, or `show` commands. The entire CLI surface described in PRD-3 §4 is net-new. The store layer (`internal/store/`) is mature: `Store.Open`, `Append`, `AppendBatch`, `ReadLive`, `ReadAll`, `Rebuild`, `ProviderIDMap`, advisory `.lock` via `gofrs/flock`, schema validation, fingerprint-based stale-index detection, and partial-line crash recovery (`recoverPartialLine`) are all shipped and tested. `index.json` today carries `entries` (canonical_id → IndexEntry with LatestRevision, LatestLineOffset, Deleted, UpdatedAt, LineULID) and `by_provider` maps — but does NOT yet carry the `sync_state` block required by PRD-3 §5. That block must be added. Both adapters are shipped and read-only verified: - `internal/adapter/engram/` — full bidirectional; `WriteNative` shells to `engram save` - `internal/adapter/claudemem/` — read-only; `WriteNative` returns `ErrUnsupported` unconditionally The `Revision=-1` sentinel (ADR-12 / obs #81) is implemented identically in both adapters: `ToCanonical` returns `Revision=-1` when `IDMap.CanonicalFromNative` finds a prior mapping, and `Revision=1` for new records. The orchestrator (sync engine, PRD-3) is responsible for replacing `-1` with `priorRevision+1` using a `store.ReadLive` call before calling `store.Append`. `records_equal_ignoring_metadata` does NOT exist yet. It must be written; it compares Title, Content, ContentFormat, Type, TopicKey, Tags, Kind — not Revision, Supersedes, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, or Origin fields. ### Affected / New Modules **Net-new (all greenfield):** - `cmd/wrapper-mems/main.go` — CLI entrypoint (cobra root) - `internal/sync/engine.go` (or equivalent) — push/pull algorithms, watermark management - `internal/sync/push.go` — push loop (§6.1) - `internal/sync/pull.go` — pull loop (§6.2) - `internal/sync/state.go` — sync_state read/write helpers on top of index.json - `internal/sync/equal.go` — `records_equal_ignoring_metadata` function - `cmd/wrapper-mems/cmd/init.go` — `wrapper-mems init` subcommand - `cmd/wrapper-mems/cmd/sync.go` — `wrapper-mems sync [pull|push]` subcommand - `cmd/wrapper-mems/cmd/status.go` — `wrapper-mems status` subcommand - `cmd/wrapper-mems/cmd/show.go` — `wrapper-mems show` subcommand - `cmd/wrapper-mems/cmd/resolve.go` — `wrapper-mems sync resolve` subcommand (§7.3) **Needs modification:** - `internal/store/index.go` — add `SyncState map[string]ProviderSyncState` and `Conflicts []ConflictEntry` fields to the `index` struct; persist/load unchanged since it's JSON - `internal/store/errors.go` — add `ErrCorrupt`, `ErrSchemaMismatch` sentinel errors (needed by §9 failure modes) - `internal/store/paths.go` — add `configFilename = "config.yaml"` constant (for mirror_engram config in §8) **No changes needed:** - `internal/adapter/adapter.go` — contract is complete - `internal/adapter/engram/` — complete - `internal/adapter/claudemem/` — complete - `internal/store/store.go`, `rebuild.go`, `record.go` — complete; `tiebreakWinner` in rebuild.go already implements the PRD-3 §7.2 conflict resolution rule (updatedAt DESC then lineULID DESC), though it uses `lineULID` not `canonical_id` as tiebreak — a minor semantic gap to note for propose ### Key Constraints (with obs citations) 1. **Revision=-1 sentinel** (obs #81, ADR-12): both adapters emit `-1` for known records. The sync engine MUST call `store.ReadLive(canonicalID)` to get `priorRevision` before passing the record to `store.Append`, replacing the sentinel with `priorRevision+1`. This is a hard contract — adapters cannot know current revision (they are pure/no I/O in ToCanonical). 2. **store.Append auto-assigns revision** (store.go:323-388): `computeAppendFields` already increments `existing.LatestRevision + 1` for known canonical_ids, and sets `r.Revision = 1` for new chains. This means the sync engine does NOT need to manually set revision at all — it can pass `Revision=0` or any value and the store will override it. The `-1` sentinel handling in the adapter is therefore advisory/documentation; the store's own logic would compute the correct revision. BUT: the engine needs `prior.Revision` to implement `records_equal_ignoring_metadata` check BEFORE deciding to append. So it still needs `store.ReadLive` for the equality check, not for revision assignment. 3. **Append-only invariant** (PRD-0 §8): no in-place mutations. Full sync runs must only append, never seek+rewrite. The store enforces this at the file handle level (O_APPEND flag). 4. **index.json is gitignored, rebuildable** (PRD-0 §10.4, obs #71): sync_state block will live there too — it is per-user/per-machine state, never committed. 5. **lockfile concurrency** (PRD-3 §10.4): `store.Open` already acquires a non-blocking advisory lock via `gofrs/flock` and returns `ErrLocked` if another process holds it. The CLI just needs to surface this error with a message. No additional lockfile is needed. 6. **claude-mem is read-only** (obs #86, ADR-10): `WriteNative` unconditionally returns `ErrUnsupported`. Pull algorithm must handle this via the `ErrUnsupported` guard in §6.2 — this is already specced. 7. **Tiebreak rule gap**: `tiebreakWinner` in `rebuild.go:24-38` uses `lineULID` (line-insert order) as the final tiebreak, while PRD-3 §7.2 specifies `canonical_id` (lexicographic ULID) as the tiebreak. These are different values. The propose phase must decide: align the PRD to lineULID (already implemented) or change rebuild.go tiebreak to canonical_id (spec-correct but requires a code change). 8. **No `authorAttribution` dedup**: the helper is copy-pasted in both adapters (engram/engram.go:97-116 and claudemem/claudemem.go:79-98). Moving it to `internal/adapter/adapter.go` or a shared `internal/adapter/common.go` is a housekeeping opportunity but not blocking. 9. **go.mod has no cobra/bubbletea yet**: `github.com/spf13/cobra` and `github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea` are not in go.mod. Adding them is required for the CLI entrypoint. 10. **mirror-engram shell-out risk** (PRD-3 §8): `engram sync --project

` is a shell-out to an external process with no defined error contract inside wrapper-mems. It has no timeout, no sandboxing, and no version-check gate. If engram CLI is absent or behaves differently, it silently corrupts the `.engram/` directory. Propose phase must define: what's the fallback when the shell-out fails? Is it a warning (exit 0) or an error (exit 1)? ### Open Questions (for propose phase to resolve) 1. **Tiebreak canonical_id vs lineULID**: PRD-3 §7.2 says `canonical_id` is the final tiebreak; `rebuild.go` uses `lineULID`. Which wins? Changing `rebuild.go` affects an already-tested module; aligning the PRD is cheaper. 2. **sync_state placement**: PRD-3 §5 places `sync_state` in `index.json`. But `index.go` already has a schema_version field and fingerprint logic tied to memory.jsonl content. Adding mutable sync state to the same file creates a two-writer problem (sync updates watermarks; store.Close updates fingerprint). Better as a separate `sync_state.json` file? Or extend the existing index persist path carefully? 3. **`wrapper-mems init` pre-existence**: there is no init command yet. It must create `.wrapper-mems/`, `schema.json`, and `.gitignore` entries. Does it also call `store.Open`? Or is `store.Open` idempotent enough to act as init? (It already calls `os.MkdirAll` and `loadOrBootstrapSchema`.) 4. **Lockfile for concurrent sync** (PRD-3 §10.4): `store.Open` already acquires `.wrapper-mems/.lock`. Is that enough, or does the sync engine need an additional higher-level lock so the advisory lock is held for the entire sync duration (not just per-Append)? 5. **`records_equal_ignoring_metadata` scope**: which fields are "metadata"? The PRD does not enumerate them. Proposal must specify: Revision, Supersedes, LineULID, SchemaVersion, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, Origin are metadata; Title, Content, ContentFormat, Type, TopicKey, Tags, Kind are payload. 6. **Exit code semantics for `sync`**: PRD-3 §9 defines exit codes for `status` (0/1/2) but not explicitly for `sync`. Propose must define: does `sync` exit 1 if any provider was skipped due to Health failure, or only if all providers failed? 7. **Batch size / pagination for push**: if engram has 10k observations, push will call `ListNative` + `ReadNative` for each. The engram adapter already warns at >= 1000 records from the export endpoint (engram.go:230-233). Propose must decide: is the first-run unbounded push acceptable for v1? 8. **`show` command rendering format**: PRD-3 §4 mentions `--kind K` and `--type T` flags but does not specify output format (JSON? table? markdown?). This is blocking for implementation but trivial to decide. 9. **`sync resolve` subcommand** (§7.3): requires reading `index.json.conflicts` (not yet in the index struct) and appending a `revision=N+2` superseding line. The conflicts array schema is defined in PRD-3 §7.2 but the `index` struct does not have it yet. ### Approach Options | Approach | Description | Pros | Cons | Effort | |----------|-------------|------|------|--------| | **A. Flat `internal/sync/` package with Engine struct** | Single `Engine` struct in `internal/sync/engine.go` owning push, pull, state, and equality helpers. Subfiles: `push.go`, `pull.go`, `state.go`, `equal.go`. CLI commands in `cmd/wrapper-mems/cmd/` are thin wrappers. | Clean hexagonal boundary; Engine is testable without cobra; state transitions in one place; aligns with existing package-per-concern layout | Slightly more package scaffolding upfront; push/pull files could grow large if §6 is verbose | Medium | | **B. cmd-driven, no internal/sync package** | Push/pull logic lives directly in `cmd/wrapper-mems/cmd/sync.go`. Store + adapters wired inline. | Fewer files initially; faster to sketch | Untestable without invoking cobra; no seam for TUI (PRD-4) to call programmatically; conflicts with hexagonal architecture pattern established in PRD-1/2 | Low (short-term) / High (long-term) | | **C. Split `internal/push/` and `internal/pull/` packages** | Each direction is its own package with its own types and tests. State is in `internal/syncstate/`. | Maximum isolation per direction; independently testable | Three extra packages for a v1 feature; over-engineered for the current scope; state package becomes a shared dep creating coupling | High | **Recommendation: Option A.** `internal/sync/` with an `Engine` struct is the natural fit: it mirrors the `internal/adapter/` pattern already in the codebase, creates a clean seam for the PRD-4 TUI to call `Engine.Push/Pull/Status` directly, and keeps the CLI layer thin. The propose phase should flesh out the Engine API surface (constructor, method signatures, option structs for dry-run/provider filter). ### Risks 1. **Two-writer on index.json**: `store.Close` updates fingerprint+BuiltAt in `index.json` atomically; adding `sync_state` to the same file means sync must either go through `store` (coupling) or write a separate file (cleaner). High risk of subtle race if sync writes index.json independently. 2. **Revision sentinel interaction with store.Append auto-revision**: `computeAppendFields` (store.go:321) will override whatever Revision the caller supplies for a known canonical_id. If the engine passes `Revision=-1`, the store will compute the correct revision anyway. But if the engine skips the `records_equal_ignoring_metadata` check and blindly appends, it will create spurious revision bumps. The equality check MUST happen before Append, not inside it. 3. **engram WriteNative update path gap**: `engram.WriteNative` (engram.go:455) returns `ErrUnsupported` when a record with the same ID already exists, because engram CLI v1.15 has no update command. This means pull cannot update existing engram records — it can only create new ones. For records that originated in engram (origin.provider == "engram"), the re-import guard in §6.2 skips them, so this is fine in the normal path. But for records originating elsewhere and already pulled into engram once, a subsequent content change in canonical will be silently skipped. Propose must acknowledge this limitation explicitly. 4. **mirror-engram shell-out**: no timeout, no version gate. A stalled `engram sync` blocks the entire wrapper-mems sync indefinitely. Must define a timeout (e.g. 30s) and treat non-zero exit as a warning, not a hard error. 5. **Partial-write recovery on push**: if `append_to_memory_jsonl` is interrupted mid-batch, the next `store.Open` will call `recoverPartialLine` and truncate to the last complete newline. Records that were in the batch but not yet flushed will be re-processed on the next push run (idempotent by design via `records_equal_ignoring_metadata`). This works correctly only if `AppendBatch` is used (single fsync) rather than multiple `Append` calls. 6. **conflicts array in index**: `index` struct does not have a `Conflicts []ConflictEntry` field yet. Adding it is mechanical but requires careful handling in `rebuildFromFile` (rebuild.go) — the rebuild must detect `(canonical_id, revision)` collisions and populate the conflicts array. This is non-trivial logic. ### Ready for Proposal Yes. All blocking decisions are enumerated. The propose phase should resolve: tiebreak alignment (Q1), sync_state file placement (Q2), Engine API surface, exit code semantics (Q6), and the engram update-path limitation (Risk 3).

title: sdd/sync-engine/proposal
content:
# Proposal: sync-engine (PRD-3)
Depends on: PRD-0, PRD-1 (obs #71), PRD-2 (obs #86), revision sentinel (obs #81), explore (obs #88)

## Intent
Build the orchestration glue that turns the existing store + adapters into a working CLI. v1 ships `wrapper-mems` (cobra) with `init`, `sync [pull|push]`, `status`, `show`, `sync resolve`, backed by a new `internal/sync` package exposing an `Engine` struct callable by both CLI (PRD-3) and the future TUI (PRD-4). Success = a user can `init`, pull from claude-mem + engram, push back to engram, see conflicts, and resolve them, end-to-end. No new storage/adapter behavior — pure orchestration.

## In scope
- `cmd/wrapper-mems/` cobra entrypoint + subcommands (init, sync, status, show, sync resolve)
- `internal/sync/` package: `Engine` struct, push.go, pull.go, state.go, equal.go
- `index.json` schema bump: add `SyncState map[string]ProviderSyncState` and `Conflicts []ConflictEntry`
- `rebuildFromFile` extended to populate `Conflicts`
- `ErrCorrupt`, `ErrSchemaMismatch` sentinels in `internal/store/errors.go`
- mirror-engram shell-out with timeout + warning-only failure
- go.mod: add `spf13/cobra`

## Out of scope v1
- TUI (PRD-4)
- engram WriteNative update path (engram CLI lacks it — documented limitation)
- claude-mem write (read-only, ADR-10)
- batch pagination / streaming push (warn at 1000, hard ceiling deferred)
- structured machine output for everything (only `show --json`)
- multi-machine sync_state merge
- authorAttribution dedup refactor

## Decisions

### D1 Tiebreak winner
Decision: Align PRD-3 §7.2 to use **lineULID** as the final tiebreak, matching existing `rebuild.go:tiebreakWinner`.
Why: `lineULID` is already implemented, tested, and monotonically encodes insert order which is a stronger semantic than canonical_id lexicographic order; changing rebuild.go risks regressions in shipped behavior.
Rejected: Changing rebuild.go to `canonical_id` — costs a code change in a tested module for negligible semantic gain.

### D2 sync_state placement
Decision: Embed `SyncState` and `Conflicts` **inside `index.json`** but route ALL writes through `store` via new `store.UpdateSyncState(provider, state)` and `store.AppendConflict(c)` methods that hold the existing flock for the write.
Why: Single source of truth, single fingerprint, no two-writer race because mutations go through the locked store API; a separate `sync_state.json` doubles file handles and rebuild logic.
Rejected: Separate `sync_state.json` — clean isolation but forces sync to manage its own lock discipline and second fingerprint.

### D3 init behavior
Decision: `init` is a **richer wrapper**: calls `store.Open` then writes `.gitignore` (memory.jsonl, index.json, .lock) and `config.yaml` (mirror_engram defaults) if absent; idempotent.
Why: `store.Open` already handles `.wrapper-mems/`, schema bootstrap, and lock; init's job is the project-level scaffolding store can't know about.
Rejected: Thin pass-through to `store.Open` — leaves the user to write `.gitignore`/`config.yaml` manually, defeating the point of an init command.

### D4 Store lock duration
Decision: **Single `store.Open` for the whole sync run.** Engine constructor takes an opened `*store.Store`; CLI opens once, defers Close, passes to engine.
Why: flock is non-blocking, so per-stage open would race against a concurrent sync; one lock covers push+pull+state updates atomically.
Rejected: Open-per-stage — re-acquires lock repeatedly and creates a window where another process can wedge in between push and pull.

### D5 records_equal_ignoring_metadata
Decision: **PAYLOAD (compared)**: Title, Content, ContentFormat, Type, TopicKey, Tags (set equality, order-insensitive), Kind. **METADATA (ignored)**: Revision, Supersedes, LineULID, SchemaVersion, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, Origin (Provider, NativeID, NativeRevision, IngestedAt, SourceFingerprint).
Why: Payload = user-visible content; metadata = provenance/bookkeeping that legitimately changes every sync without representing a real edit.
Rejected: Including Origin.SourceFingerprint as payload — would force a revision bump on every re-pull because fingerprints recompute.

### D6 sync exit codes
Decision: `sync` exits **0** = all providers succeeded (or no work); **1** = at least one provider had a hard error (Health fail, lock contention, store corruption); **2** = unresolved conflicts present after run (advisory, run still completed). Partial provider skip with successful others = 0 + stderr warning.
Why: Mirrors `status` semantics from §9; reserves 2 for the actionable "user must run `sync resolve`" state.
Rejected: 0/1 only — loses the conflict-actionable signal needed by CI and the future TUI.

### D7 First-run cap
Decision: **Unbounded by default** with `--max N` opt-in and a stderr warning at ≥1000 records pulled from a single provider in one run.
Why: v1 user base is solo developers with <10k records; capping by default surprises users who expect a full backfill; the warning is enough.
Rejected: `--max` required — adds friction to the happy path; engram adapter already emits its own ≥1000 warning at the export layer.

### D8 show output format
Decision: **Default = table** (id, kind, type, topic_key, title, updated_at, revision); `--json` for machine output; truncate Title to 60 chars, no Content in table.
Why: `show` is interactive triage; table is scannable; JSON is the escape hatch for scripts.
Rejected: JSON default — unreadable for the primary "what's in my store" use case.

### D9 engram WriteNative update gap
Decision: **Silent skip + structured warning** on stderr (`SKIP engram update id=X reason=engram-cli-no-update`). Counted in run summary as `updates_skipped`. Not a hard error.
Why: engram CLI v1.15 simply lacks an update verb; failing the run punishes users for an upstream gap; documenting the count surfaces it without blocking sync.
Rejected: Hard fail — would break any push to engram for re-exported records. Flag-gated retry — over-engineered for a known upstream limitation.

### D10 Architecture
Decision: **Confirm Option A** — `internal/sync/Engine` struct with push.go, pull.go, state.go, equal.go subfiles; CLI commands in `cmd/wrapper-mems/cmd/` are thin wrappers that build options and call engine methods.
Why: Mirrors `internal/adapter/` pattern; creates the seam PRD-4 TUI needs; testable without cobra.
Rejected: cmd-driven (B) — untestable, no TUI seam. Split push/pull packages (C) — over-decomposed for v1.

### D11 mirror-engram safety
Decision: **30s timeout** via `exec.CommandContext`; non-zero exit or timeout = stderr warning + sync continues with exit 0 (unless other failures). No version check in v1; document required engram CLI version in README.
Why: Shell-out is best-effort housekeeping, not a correctness requirement; timeout prevents hangs; version gate is deferred until we see a real incompatibility.
Rejected: No timeout (hangs forever); hard fail (punishes users without engram CLI installed).

### D12 sync resolve UX
Decision: `--keep <dup_index>` where `dup_index` is the 1-based index into `ConflictEntry.Duplicates` array as shown by `status --conflicts`; **PRD §7.3 line-number flag is rejected**.
Why: Line numbers shift on rebuild and partial-line recovery; the duplicate index is stable per conflict entry and matches what the user already sees in `status` output.
Rejected: `--keep <line_number>` — fragile, breaks after `recoverPartialLine` or future log compaction.

## Architecture

```
cmd/wrapper-mems/main.go
└── cmd/{init,sync,status,show,resolve}.go   (thin, build Options + call Engine)
        │
        ▼
internal/sync/Engine
├── push.go     ListNative → ReadNative → ToCanonical → ReadLive equality check → AppendBatch
├── pull.go     ReadAll → FromCanonical → WriteNative (skip on ErrUnsupported / origin re-import)
├── state.go    ProviderSyncState + watermark via store.UpdateSyncState
├── equal.go    records_equal_ignoring_metadata (D5)
└── mirror.go   engram sync shell-out (D11)
        │
        ▼
internal/store (single Open for whole run, flock held)
└── index.json now carries SyncState + Conflicts
        │
        ▼
internal/adapter/{engram,claudemem}
```

## Delivery shape preview
Rough LOC estimate:
- `internal/sync/` ~600 LOC (engine 150, push 120, pull 150, state 80, equal 50, mirror 50) + ~500 LOC tests
- `cmd/wrapper-mems/` ~400 LOC (root 50, init 80, sync 100, status 80, show 60, resolve 50) + ~200 LOC tests
- store/index.go + rebuild.go modifications ~150 LOC + ~150 LOC tests
- errors.go, paths.go, go.mod: trivial

**Total ~2000 LOC.** This **WILL trip the 400-line budget** and `ask-on-risk` MUST fire after `sdd-tasks`. Recommended slicing (stacked-to-main):
1. PR-A: index.json schema bump (SyncState, Conflicts) + rebuild.go conflict detection + sentinel errors
2. PR-B: `internal/sync/` engine + equal + state (no CLI yet, unit-tested in isolation)
3. PR-C: `cmd/wrapper-mems/` skeleton + `init` + `status` + `show`
4. PR-D: `sync pull` + `sync push` wiring + mirror-engram
5. PR-E: `sync resolve` + conflict UX polish

## Open items for spec/design
- Exact `ProviderSyncState` field set (last_pulled_at, last_pushed_at, last_native_cursor?, watermark format)
- `ConflictEntry` exact shape (canonical_id, revision, duplicates[]{line_offset, line_ulid, updated_at, origin})
- `Engine` constructor signature: `New(s *store.Store, adapters map[string]adapter.Adapter, opts Options) *Engine`
- Options struct: DryRun, ProviderFilter []string, Max int, Verbose bool
- `config.yaml` schema (mirror_engram on/off, timeout override, provider enable list)
- Status output format when --conflicts flag set (table columns)
- Where does `recoverPartialLine` log its truncation event — surface in `status`?
- Test strategy for shell-out (mock engram binary via PATH override in tests)

title: sdd/sync-engine/design
content:
# Design: sync-engine (PRD-3)
Reads: proposal #89 (D1-D12 fixed), explore #88, shipped adapter.go + store/index.go
Scope: ADDITIVE — new internal/sync/ + cmd/wrapper-mems/; store mods are field/method additions only.

## Package layout (file tree)
```
cmd/wrapper-mems/
  main.go                 // builds cobra root, Execute()
  cmd/root.go             // rootCmd, persistent flags (--project, --verbose, --dir)
  cmd/init.go             // init
  cmd/sync.go             // sync, sync pull, sync push (subcommands)
  cmd/resolve.go          // sync resolve
  cmd/status.go           // status (--conflicts)
  cmd/show.go             // show (--kind --type --json)
  cmd/build.go            // shared: openStore() + buildEngine() helpers, exit-code map
internal/sync/
  engine.go               // Engine struct, New(), Push/Pull/Sync/Status/Resolve
  push.go                 // pushProvider loop (§6.1)
  pull.go                 // pullProvider loop (§6.2)
  state.go                // watermark read/write via store
  equal.go                // recordsEqualIgnoringMetadata (D5)
  mirror.go               // mirrorEngram shell-out (D11)
  config.go               // Config struct, Load/Default (config.yaml)
  report.go               // RunReport aggregation
internal/store/  (modified)
  index.go                // + SyncState, Conflicts fields; ProviderSyncState, ConflictEntry types
  syncstate.go (new)      // UpdateSyncState, AppendConflict methods (hold flock)
  rebuild.go (modified)   // conflict detection in rebuildFromFile
  errors.go (modified)    // ErrCorrupt, ErrSchemaMismatch
  paths.go (modified)     // configFilename = "config.yaml"
```

## Key types (Go sketches)
```go
// internal/store/index.go
type ProviderSyncState struct {
    LastPulledAt   string `json:"last_pulled_at"`   // RFC3339, watermark for ListNative since
    LastPushedAt   string `json:"last_pushed_at"`
    LastNativeCursor string `json:"last_native_cursor,omitempty"`
    RecordsPulled  int    `json:"records_pulled"`
    RecordsPushed  int    `json:"records_pushed"`
}
type ConflictEntry struct {
    CanonicalID string             `json:"canonical_id"`
    Revision    int                `json:"revision"`
    DetectedAt  string             `json:"detected_at"`
    Duplicates  []ConflictDuplicate `json:"duplicates"` // 1-based dup_index = position+1
}
type ConflictDuplicate struct {
    LineOffset int64  `json:"line_offset"`
    LineULID   string `json:"line_ulid"`
    UpdatedAt  string `json:"updated_at"`
    Provider   string `json:"provider"`
}
// index struct gains:
//   SyncState map[string]ProviderSyncState `json:"sync_state,omitempty"`
//   Conflicts []ConflictEntry              `json:"conflicts,omitempty"`
// loadIndex nil-inits both maps/slices.

// internal/store/syncstate.go
func (s *Store) UpdateSyncState(provider string, st ProviderSyncState) error // flock-guarded, persist index
func (s *Store) AppendConflict(c ConflictEntry) error                        // append, dedupe by (canonical_id,revision)
func (s *Store) Conflicts() []ConflictEntry
func (s *Store) SyncState(provider string) (ProviderSyncState, bool)

// internal/sync/engine.go
type Options struct { DryRun bool; ProviderFilter []string; Max int; Verbose bool; MirrorEngram bool }
type Engine struct { store *store.Store; adapters map[string]adapter.Adapter; cfg Config; opts Options; w io.Writer }
func New(s *store.Store, adapters map[string]adapter.Adapter, cfg Config, opts Options) *Engine
func (e *Engine) Push(ctx context.Context) (*RunReport, error)
func (e *Engine) Pull(ctx context.Context) (*RunReport, error)
func (e *Engine) Sync(ctx context.Context) (*RunReport, error)   // Pull then Push then mirror
func (e *Engine) Status() (*StatusReport, error)
func (e *Engine) Resolve(canonicalID string, dupIndex int) error
type RunReport struct { PerProvider map[string]ProviderResult; UpdatesSkipped int; Conflicts int; HardErrors []error }
```

## Push flow (§6.1 + D5)
Per provider in filter (skip if Health fails -> RunReport.HardErrors, continue):
1. since = store.SyncState(p).LastPushedAt
2. ids := adapter.ListNative(ctx, project, since); warn to stderr if len>=1000 (D7); cap at opts.Max if >0
3. For each id: native := ReadNative; canon := ToCanonical(native, idmap)
4. prior, ok := store.ReadLive(canon.CanonicalID)
5. if ok && recordsEqualIgnoringMetadata(prior, canon) -> skip (no append)
6. else collect into batch; canon.Revision left as adapter sentinel (-1 or 1) — store.computeAppendFields overrides it anyway. Sentinel is advisory.
7. store.AppendBatch(batch) — single fsync, idempotent under partial-write recovery
8. store.UpdateSyncState(p, {LastPushedAt: now, RecordsPushed: n})

recordsEqualIgnoringMetadata(a,b store.CanonicalRecord) bool — compares ONLY:
Title, Content, ContentFormat, Type, TopicKey, Kind, Tags(set equality, sort+compare).
Ignores: Revision, Supersedes, LineULID, SchemaVersion, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, Origin.*.

## Pull flow (§6.2 + D9)
Per provider in filter:
1. records := store.ReadAll() (live, non-deleted)
2. For each rec: if rec.Origin.Provider == p -> skip (origin re-import guard)
3. native := adapter.FromCanonical(rec); if errors.Is(err, adapter.ErrUnsupported) -> skip (relation record)
4. _, err := adapter.WriteNative(ctx, native)
5. if errors.Is(err, adapter.ErrUnsupported): record is an update to existing native (claude-mem read-only OR engram update gap) -> stderr `SKIP {p} update id=X reason=...`; RunReport.UpdatesSkipped++; continue
6. else if err -> HardErrors; else RecordsPulled++
7. store.UpdateSyncState(p, {LastPulledAt: now, RecordsPulled})

## Conflict detection & resolve (D1, D12)
- rebuild.go rebuildFromFile: while scanning lines, track seen (canonical_id, revision). On a SECOND line with same pair -> a conflict. Group all dup lines into ConflictEntry; winner picked by existing tiebreakWinner (updatedAt DESC, then lineULID DESC = D1). Non-winner lines recorded as Duplicates ordered by line offset.
- IndexEntry.LatestLineOffset stays pointing at tiebreak winner; conflicts are advisory.
- store.Conflicts() exposes them. status --conflicts prints table: canonical_id, revision, dup_index, line_ulid, updated_at, provider.
- Resolve(canonicalID, dupIndex): look up ConflictEntry; pick Duplicates[dupIndex-1]; read that line's record; append a NEW superseding line with Revision=winnerRev+1, Supersedes=that line's lineULID, payload from chosen dup. After append, drop the resolved ConflictEntry from index.Conflicts and persist. dupIndex is 1-based, validated; out-of-range -> error.

## CLI wiring & exit codes (D6, D10)
- cobra root: persistent flags --dir (project root, default cwd), --project, --verbose.
- init: no store needed pre-call; runs store.Open + writes .gitignore + config.yaml (D3).
- sync [pull|push]: flags --dry-run, --max, --provider (repeatable), --mirror/--no-mirror. Bare `sync` = pull+push+mirror.
- status: --conflicts. show: --kind, --type, --json.
- resolve: `sync resolve <canonical_id> --keep <dup_index>` (D12).
- Exit map (cmd/build.go): 0 = all ok / partial skip with warning; 1 = any HardError (Health fail, ErrLocked, ErrCorrupt); 2 = unresolved conflicts after run. Resolve via os.Exit in main from RunReport.

## mirror-engram (D11)
mirror.go: `exec.CommandContext(ctx, "engram", "sync", "--project", project)` with 30s context.WithTimeout. Capture stderr into buffer. Non-zero exit OR ctx.DeadlineExceeded -> stderr `WARN mirror-engram failed: <stderr>`; never sets HardErrors; sync still exit 0. Gated by cfg.MirrorEngram && opts.MirrorEngram.

## config.yaml schema (D3)
```yaml
mirror_engram: true          # bool, default true
mirror_timeout_seconds: 30   # int, default 30
providers: [engram, claude-mem]  # enabled provider list; default = all registered
```
config.go: Load(path) -> Config; Default() for missing file; init writes Default() if absent. Unknown keys ignored.

## Error sentinels
internal/store/errors.go: `ErrCorrupt = errors.New("store: index/log corrupt")`, `ErrSchemaMismatch = errors.New("store: schema version mismatch")`. Returned by loadIndex/rebuild on parse failure / schema_version drift. CLI maps both to exit 1.

## Test strategy (strict_tdd: false)
- equal_test.go: table tests over field-pair matrix (each payload field differs -> false; each metadata field differs -> true).
- Fake adapter: in-package `fakeAdapter` implementing adapter.Adapter with scripted ListNative/ReadNative/WriteNative + injectable errors (ErrUnsupported, ErrUnavailable). Drives push/pull table tests against a real temp-dir store.
- Conflict detection: write a memory.jsonl with duplicate (canonical_id,revision) lines, rebuild, assert Conflicts populated + winner.
- Shell-out: mirror_test.go overrides PATH to a temp dir containing a fake `engram` script (exit 0 / exit 1 / sleep>30s) — assert warning vs success vs timeout.
- CLI: cobra commands tested via Execute with args + captured buffers; exit-code logic unit-tested on RunReport.

## Delivery slicing (5 PRs, stacked-to-main)
Total ~2000 LOC -> trips 400-line budget; ask-on-risk MUST fire after sdd-tasks.
- PR-A ~300 LOC: store/index.go fields + syncstate.go (UpdateSyncState/AppendConflict) + rebuild.go conflict detection + errors.go sentinels + paths.go const. +~300 tests.
- PR-B ~450 LOC: internal/sync/ engine.go + equal.go + state.go + config.go + report.go (no CLI, no I/O subcommands). +~350 tests.
- PR-C ~300 LOC: cmd/wrapper-mems skeleton + root + init + status + show + build.go. go.mod adds cobra. +~150 tests.
- PR-D ~400 LOC: push.go + pull.go + mirror.go + sync.go CLI wiring. +~250 tests.
- PR-E ~200 LOC: resolve.go + sync resolve CLI + status --conflicts table polish. +~120 tests.

## Risks / deferred
- engram update gap (D9): records originating elsewhere, already in engram, never get content updates — silent skip + counter only.
- Conflict detection adds non-trivial logic to a shipped tested module (rebuild.go) — PR-A needs strong regression coverage.
- PR-B builds Engine before push/pull exist; push/pull are stubbed/interface-only until PR-D — keep Engine methods thin so stubs compile.
- Multi-machine sync_state merge out of scope; SyncState is per-machine, gitignored.

title: sdd/sync-engine/spec
content:
# Spec: sync-engine (PRD-3) Status: DRAFT Depends on: PRD-0, PRD-1 (#71), PRD-2 (#86), proposal (#89), explore (#88) Binding decisions carried from proposal: D1 Tiebreak = lineULID (not canonical_id); PRD-3 §7.2 text is superseded. D2 SyncState + Conflicts live inside index.json; ALL writes via store-level API (flock held). D3 `init` calls store.Open then writes .gitignore + config.yaml if absent; idempotent. D4 Single store.Open for the whole sync run; Engine ctor takes *store.Store. D5 Payload fields compared by equal.go: Title, Content, ContentFormat, Type, TopicKey, Tags (set), Kind. Metadata (ignored): Revision, Supersedes, LineULID, SchemaVersion, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, Origin.*. D6 sync exits 0=success/no-work, 1=hard error (Health fail, lock, corruption), 2=unresolved conflicts present. D7 First-run pull is unbounded; warn on stderr at ≥1000 records per provider per run; --max N opt-in cap. D8 show default = table (id, kind, type, topic_key, title≤60, updated_at, revision); --json = full machine output. D9 engram WriteNative update not supported in v1; silent skip + stderr SKIP line + count in run summary. D10 Architecture = internal/sync/Engine (push.go, pull.go, state.go, equal.go, mirror.go); CLI = thin wrappers. D11 mirror-engram shell-out 30s timeout; non-zero/timeout = stderr warning + exit 0 (unless other failures). D12 sync resolve uses --keep (1-based index into ConflictEntry.Duplicates); line-number flag rejected. --- ## Requirements ### §init — Init Command REQ-SE-01 `wrapper-mems init` MUST create `.wrapper-mems/` directory (via store.Open) if it does not exist. Traces: PRD-3 §4, D3. Scenario — fresh directory: Given no `.wrapper-mems/` directory exists in cwd When the user runs `wrapper-mems init` Then exit code is 0 And `.wrapper-mems/` directory is created And `.wrapper-mems/memory.jsonl` exists (may be empty) And `.wrapper-mems/index.json` exists with schema_version=1 REQ-SE-02 `wrapper-mems init` MUST write a `.gitignore` file under the project root that includes at minimum: `.wrapper-mems/memory.jsonl`, `.wrapper-mems/index.json`, `.wrapper-mems/.lock` If `.gitignore` already contains these entries the file MUST NOT be modified. Traces: PRD-3 §4, D3. Scenario — idempotent gitignore: Given `.wrapper-mems/` already exists with correct .gitignore entries When the user runs `wrapper-mems init` again Then exit code is 0 And .gitignore is unchanged (byte-for-byte identical) And no duplicate entries are appended REQ-SE-03 `wrapper-mems init` MUST write `.wrapper-mems/config.yaml` with default values if absent. Minimum required defaults: `mirror_engram: false`. Traces: D3, PRD-3 §8. Scenario — config created on first init: Given no `.wrapper-mems/config.yaml` exists When `wrapper-mems init` runs Then `.wrapper-mems/config.yaml` is created And it contains `mirror_engram: false` REQ-SE-04 `wrapper-mems init` MUST be idempotent: running it N times in a row on an already-initialised directory MUST produce the same state as running it once, with exit code 0 on every invocation. Traces: PRD-3 §4, D3. REQ-SE-05 Running any sync subcommand from a directory without `.wrapper-mems/` MUST exit 1 and print: "no canonical store found — run `wrapper-mems init` first" to stderr. Traces: PRD-3 §10 Q5. --- ### §4 — Command Surface REQ-SE-06 The binary name MUST be `wrapper-mems`. The root cobra command MUST expose at minimum: `init`, `sync`, `sync pull`, `sync push`, `sync resolve`, `status`, `show`. Traces: PRD-3 §4, D10. REQ-SE-07 `sync` (no subcommand) MUST execute pull then push, against all enabled providers, in the order pull→push. Traces: PRD-3 §6.3. Scenario — full sync ordering: Given the store is initialised with engram and claude-mem enabled When `wrapper-mems sync` runs to completion Then pull completes before push begins (observable via ordered log output in --verbose mode) REQ-SE-08 `sync --dry-run` MUST NOT write to memory.jsonl, index.json, or any adapter. It MUST print what WOULD be written to stdout in a human-readable diff-like format. Traces: PRD-3 §4, §2 goal 5. Scenario — dry-run produces no side effects: Given a store with 3 canonical records And engram has 1 new record not in canonical When `wrapper-mems sync --dry-run` runs Then memory.jsonl is unchanged after the run And stdout describes the 1 record that would be pushed And exit code is 0 REQ-SE-09 `sync --provider ` MUST restrict all sync operations to the named provider(s) only. Multiple `--provider` flags MUST be OR-ed. Traces: PRD-3 §4, D10 Options.ProviderFilter. Scenario — provider filter: Given engram and claude-mem are both enabled When `wrapper-mems sync --provider claude-mem` runs Then only claude-mem's ListNative/ReadNative are called And engram sync watermark is NOT updated REQ-SE-10 `sync --mirror-engram` MUST trigger the mirror-engram shell-out (§8 / D11), irrespective of config.yaml setting. Traces: PRD-3 §4, §8, D11. REQ-SE-11 `sync --commit` MUST run `git add .wrapper-mems/ && git commit -m "chore: sync [timestamp]"` after a successful sync run (non-dry-run). If git is unavailable or the working directory is not a git repo, emit a warning and continue; do NOT hard-fail. Traces: PRD-3 §4. Scenario — commit flag outside git repo: Given the current directory is not a git repository When `wrapper-mems sync --commit` runs Then exit code is 0 (unless other failures) And a warning is emitted to stderr about git being unavailable REQ-SE-12 `show` MUST accept `--kind K` and `--type T` flags as OR-able filters. With no flags it renders ALL live (non-deleted) records. Traces: PRD-3 §4, D8. REQ-SE-13 `show --json` MUST emit one JSON object per line (JSONL), each being a full canonical record. Exit code 0 always if the store is readable. Traces: D8. REQ-SE-14 `show` default (no --json) MUST render a table with columns: id (canonical_id first 12 chars), kind, type, topic_key, title (truncated to 60 chars), updated_at, revision. Long lines MUST wrap or truncate gracefully — they MUST NOT cause a parse error. Traces: D8. --- ### §5 — State and Watermarks REQ-SE-15 `index.json` MUST contain a top-level `sync_state` field of type `map[string]ProviderSyncState`. `ProviderSyncState` MUST have fields: `last_pushed_at` (RFC3339 or null), `last_pulled_at` (RFC3339 or null). Traces: PRD-3 §5, D2. REQ-SE-16 `index.json` MUST contain a top-level `conflicts` field of type `[]ConflictEntry`. `ConflictEntry` MUST have fields: `canonical_id` (string), `revision` (int), `winner_line` (int), `loser_line` (int), `detected_at` (RFC3339), `duplicates` ([]DuplicateRef). `DuplicateRef` MUST have fields: `dup_index` (int, 1-based), `line_offset` (int), `line_ulid` (string), `updated_at` (RFC3339), `origin_provider` (string). Traces: PRD-3 §7.2, D12. REQ-SE-17 ALL mutations to `sync_state` and `conflicts` in `index.json` MUST go through `store.UpdateSyncState(provider, state)` and `store.AppendConflict(c)` respectively, both of which MUST hold the flock for the duration of the write. Traces: D2, D4. REQ-SE-18 Watermarks (`last_pushed_at`, `last_pulled_at`) are advisory. The sync engine MUST NOT rely on watermarks alone to decide whether a record is new — it MUST perform an equality check (`records_equal_ignoring_metadata`) on any record found via ListNative regardless of watermark. Traces: PRD-3 §5, D5. REQ-SE-19 After a successful push run for provider A, `sync_state[A].last_pushed_at` MUST be set to the maximum `updated_at` value across all records processed (whether appended or skipped-equal) for that provider in that run. Traces: PRD-3 §6.1. REQ-SE-20 After a successful pull run for provider A, `sync_state[A].last_pulled_at` MUST be set to the current wall-clock time (UTC) at the moment the pull loop for A completes without a hard error. Traces: PRD-3 §6.2. Note: if WriteNative fails for individual records (soft error), last_pulled_at is NOT advanced past the last failed record's updated_at. Scenario — pull watermark not advanced past failed record: Given canonical has 3 records with updated_at t1 < t2 < t3 And WriteNative fails on the record with updated_at=t2 When pull completes Then last_pulled_at is t1 (the last fully-successful record's timestamp) And the failed record will be retried on next run --- ### §6.1 — Push (provider → canonical) REQ-SE-21 Push MUST iterate providers in deterministic order: engram first, then claude-mem, then any future providers in registration order. Traces: PRD-3 §6.1. REQ-SE-22 For each native record returned by `A.ListNative(ctx, project, since)`: a. Call `A.ReadNative(ctx, id)` to get full content. b. Resolve `canonical_id` via `idmap[A.Name].CanonicalFromNative(id)`. c. If not found: new chain — set revision=1, supersedes=null. d. If found: call `store.ReadLive(canonical_id)` to get prior; if `records_equal_ignoring_metadata(native, prior)` → skip (no append). Otherwise: revision = prior.revision+1, supersedes = prior.canonical_id. e. Append new records via `store.AppendBatch` (single fsync, not multiple Append calls). Traces: PRD-3 §6.1, D5, explore §risk-5. Scenario — push skips unchanged record: Given canonical has record X at revision=2 And engram returns the same record X with identical payload When push runs Then no new line is appended to memory.jsonl And sync_state[engram].last_pushed_at is updated Scenario — push appends changed record: Given canonical has record X at revision=2 And engram returns record X with a different Content value When push runs Then a new line with revision=3 and supersedes=X is appended to memory.jsonl REQ-SE-23 If `store.ReadLive` fails for a known `canonical_id` during push (record not in index), push MUST treat the record as new (revision=1) and log a warning. This is a recovery path for index corruption. Traces: PRD-3 §9. REQ-SE-24 Push MUST use `store.AppendBatch` (not multiple `store.Append` calls) to write all new records for a single provider in one fsync boundary. Traces: explore §risk-5. REQ-SE-25 If `A.ListNative` or `A.ReadNative` fails with a non-ErrUnsupported error, push MUST log a warning for the affected record, skip it, and continue with the next record. The run-level exit code is unaffected by per-record soft errors. Traces: PRD-3 §9. REQ-SE-26 If the total number of records returned by `A.ListNative` in a single run is ≥1000, push MUST emit a warning to stderr: "large sync: N records from , this may take a while". Traces: D7. REQ-SE-27 `sync push --max N` MUST stop processing records for a provider after N records have been processed (appended or skipped) in that run, regardless of how many ListNative returned. Traces: D7. --- ### §6.2 — Pull (canonical → provider) REQ-SE-28 Pull MUST only execute for providers where `A.WriteNative` is not permanently unavailable. The engine MUST attempt the pull loop for all adapters and silently skip any record for which `WriteNative` returns `ErrUnsupported`. Traces: PRD-3 §6.2, D9. REQ-SE-29 Pull MUST read all canonical records with `updated_at >= sync_state[A].last_pulled_at` (or all records if null/epoch), collapse to latest revision per `canonical_id`, then for each: a. If `canonical.origin.provider == A.Name()` AND the provider already has a native copy → skip (re-import guard). b. If `canonical.kind` not in `A.SupportedKinds()` → skip. c. If `canonical.deleted == true` → skip (v1 does not propagate deletes into providers). d. Call `A.FromCanonical(canonical)`, then `A.WriteNative(ctx, native)`. e. On success: call `idmap[A.Name].Bind(native_id, canonical.canonical_id)`. f. On `ErrUnsupported`: skip silently. g. On any other error: log warning, skip, do NOT advance watermark past this record's timestamp. Traces: PRD-3 §6.2. Scenario — re-import guard: Given record X originated from engram (origin.provider="engram") And engram still holds native copy of X When pull runs for engram Then WriteNative is NOT called for record X Scenario — ErrUnsupported skips silently: Given adapter returns ErrUnsupported from WriteNative When pull processes that record Then no error is logged to stderr And exit code is 0 REQ-SE-30 On engram WriteNative returning a "record exists" error (distinct from ErrUnsupported) for an update path, the engine MUST emit a structured warning to stderr: `SKIP engram update id= reason=engram-cli-no-update` and increment the run-summary counter `updates_skipped`. This MUST NOT affect exit code. Traces: D9. Scenario — engram update skip: Given canonical has an updated revision of a record originally from engram When pull for engram processes that record Then stderr contains "SKIP engram update id= reason=engram-cli-no-update" And updates_skipped is 1 in the run summary And exit code is 0 --- ### §6.3 — Full Sync REQ-SE-31 Full sync (`wrapper-mems sync` with no subcommand) MUST run pull to completion before starting push. Records appended during push MUST NOT be re-processed in the same run's pull phase. Traces: PRD-3 §6.3. REQ-SE-32 Full sync MUST print a run summary to stdout on completion. Summary MUST include at minimum: - records pulled per provider - records pushed per provider - records skipped (equal) per provider - updates_skipped (D9 engram update gap) count - conflicts detected count - exit code reason if non-zero Traces: PRD-3 §2 (idempotent / no-op), D9. Scenario — no-op run summary: Given the store is up to date with all providers When `wrapper-mems sync` runs Then stdout contains "0 records pulled, 0 records pushed" (exact wording may vary) And memory.jsonl is byte-for-byte unchanged And exit code is 0 --- ### §7 — Conflict Detection and Resolution REQ-SE-33 During `store.Rebuild` (index rebuild), if two JSONL lines share the same `(canonical_id, revision)` pair, the store MUST detect this as a collision. Traces: PRD-3 §7.1, D2. REQ-SE-34 Collision resolution tiebreaker MUST be: (1) greater `updated_at` wins; (2) if equal, greater `line_ulid` (lexicographic DESC) wins. The winning line becomes the live revision in `index.json`. This supersedes PRD-3 §7.2 canonical_id tiebreaker (D1). Traces: D1, PRD-3 §7.2. Scenario — collision tiebreak by updated_at: Given memory.jsonl has two lines for canonical_id=X revision=3 And line A has updated_at=T+1, line B has updated_at=T When rebuild runs Then line A is the winner in index.json And a ConflictEntry is recorded with winner_line=A.offset, loser_line=B.offset Scenario — collision tiebreak by line_ulid when updated_at equal: Given memory.jsonl has two lines for canonical_id=X revision=3 And both lines have identical updated_at And line A has a lexicographically greater line_ulid When rebuild runs Then line A is the winner REQ-SE-35 The loser line MUST remain in memory.jsonl (append-only invariant). A `ConflictEntry` MUST be written to `index.json.conflicts` via `store.AppendConflict`. Traces: PRD-3 §7.2, D2. REQ-SE-36 `status --conflicts` MUST render the conflict list as a table with columns: #(dup_index), canonical_id, revision, winner_line, loser_line, detected_at. Traces: PRD-3 §7.2, D12. REQ-SE-37 `wrapper-mems sync resolve --keep ` MUST: a. Read `ConflictEntry` for `canonical_id` from index.json.conflicts. b. Find the `DuplicateRef` at position `dup_index` (1-based). c. Read the JSONL line at `DuplicateRef.line_offset` to obtain the chosen record. d. Append a new JSONL line with `revision = winner_revision + 1`, `supersedes = canonical_id`, and payload copied from the chosen record. e. Remove the `ConflictEntry` from `index.json.conflicts` via `store.RemoveConflict(canonical_id)`. f. Trigger an index rebuild for the affected `canonical_id`. Traces: PRD-3 §7.3, D12. Scenario — resolve selects loser: Given canonical_id=X has a conflict with dup_index=1 (winner) and dup_index=2 (loser) When `sync resolve X --keep 2` runs Then a new JSONL line is appended with the payload of dup_index=2 at revision=winner+1 And X is no longer in index.json.conflicts And exit code is 0 Scenario — resolve with invalid dup_index: Given canonical_id=X has a conflict with 2 duplicates When `sync resolve X --keep 5` runs Then exit code is 1 And stderr contains "invalid dup_index: 5 (conflict has 2 duplicates)" REQ-SE-38 If `sync resolve` is run for a `canonical_id` with no active conflict, it MUST exit 1 and print "no conflict found for ". Traces: D12. REQ-SE-39 After `sync resolve` completes successfully, `sync_state` watermarks MUST NOT be reset. The resolve operation is a targeted append; it does not restart the sync watermark. Traces: D2, D12. --- ### §8 — Mirror Engram REQ-SE-40 When mirror-engram is enabled (via `--mirror-engram` flag OR `config.yaml: mirror_engram: true`): a. BEFORE pull: run `engram sync --import` to ingest any new .engram/ chunks from git. b. AFTER a successful push: run `engram sync --project

` to update .engram/ chunks. Traces: PRD-3 §8, D11. Scenario — mirror order: Given mirror_engram=true in config.yaml When `wrapper-mems sync` runs Then `engram sync --import` is called before the pull phase And `engram sync --project

` is called after the push phase completes successfully REQ-SE-41 Each mirror-engram shell-out MUST be wrapped with `exec.CommandContext` with a 30-second timeout. Traces: D11. Scenario — mirror timeout: Given the engram binary hangs indefinitely When the 30s timeout fires Then the process is killed And a warning is emitted to stderr: "mirror-engram timed out after 30s" And sync continues and exits 0 (unless other failures) REQ-SE-42 A non-zero exit code from either mirror-engram invocation MUST emit a warning to stderr and MUST NOT cause sync to exit 1. It MUST NOT increment the conflict counter or any error counter affecting exit code. Traces: D11. REQ-SE-43 If `engram` binary is not found on PATH, mirror-engram MUST emit a warning and skip the shell-out. It MUST NOT be a hard error. Traces: D11. --- ### §9 — Failure Modes REQ-SE-44 If `A.Health()` returns an error for provider A, sync MUST: a. Emit to stderr: "provider unavailable, skipping: " b. Skip all push/pull operations for A. c. Continue with remaining providers. d. Exit 0 if at least one other provider succeeded; exit 1 if ALL providers failed Health checks. Traces: PRD-3 §9, D6. Scenario — one provider down: Given engram Health() returns error, claude-mem Health() returns nil When `wrapper-mems sync` runs Then engram is skipped with a warning And claude-mem pull runs successfully And exit code is 0 REQ-SE-45 If `memory.jsonl` contains an invalid JSON line (corrupt JSONL), `store.Open` MUST refuse to proceed. The CLI MUST print to stderr: "memory.jsonl is corrupt — run `wrapper-mems doctor` to diagnose" and exit 1. It MUST NOT attempt to repair the file or continue sync. Traces: PRD-3 §9, D2. Note: This is distinct from the partial-write (truncated last line) case handled by recoverPartialLine, which is transparent and automatic. REQ-SE-46 If `index.json` schema_version does not match the code's expected version, `store.Open` MUST return `ErrSchemaMismatch`. The CLI MUST print the mismatch to stderr and exit 1. Traces: PRD-3 §9, explore (store/errors.go). REQ-SE-47 If `store.Open` returns `ErrLocked` (another sync process holds the flock), the CLI MUST print: "another sync is running (lock held at .wrapper-mems/.lock) — try again shortly" and exit 1. Traces: PRD-3 §10 Q4, D4. Scenario — concurrent sync: Given a second `wrapper-mems sync` process is running and holds the flock When a concurrent `wrapper-mems sync` is invoked Then the second invocation exits immediately with code 1 And stderr contains the lock message REQ-SE-48 If `store.Open` returns `ErrCorrupt` (index rebuild failed), the CLI MUST print an actionable error and exit 1. It MUST NOT attempt further sync operations. Traces: explore (store/errors.go), PRD-3 §9. REQ-SE-49 A partial write to `memory.jsonl` (last line lacks newline terminator) MUST be detected and the partial line truncated to the last complete newline on the NEXT `store.Open` invocation (`recoverPartialLine`). This is transparent to the user and MUST NOT produce any stderr output. Traces: PRD-3 §9 ("Network/IO error mid-append"), explore §risk-5. REQ-SE-50 After a partial-line truncation, any records that were in the in-progress batch but not written MUST be re-eligible for push on the next run (idempotent via REQ-SE-22d equality check). Traces: explore §risk-5. --- ### §10 — Exit Codes (global) REQ-SE-51 `sync` exit code contract (D6): - 0: all providers succeeded (or no work); includes partial provider skip with warning (D6). - 1: at least one hard error: Health failure for ALL providers, store.Open failure, ErrLocked, ErrCorrupt, ErrSchemaMismatch. - 2: run completed but unresolved conflicts exist in index.json.conflicts. Conflicts detected during a run count toward exit code 2 on that run. Traces: D6, PRD-3 §9. Scenario — conflicts produce exit 2: Given a git merge introduced a revision collision for canonical_id=X When `wrapper-mems sync` (or rebuild) detects the collision Then exit code is 2 And stderr contains guidance to run `wrapper-mems status --conflicts` and `wrapper-mems sync resolve` REQ-SE-52 `status` exit code contract (PRD-3 §9): - 0: all providers healthy. - 1: at least one provider degraded (Health() returned error). - 2: wrapper-mems itself misconfigured (no .wrapper-mems/, invalid schema, ErrCorrupt). Traces: PRD-3 §9. REQ-SE-53 All commands MUST write diagnostic/warning messages to stderr. Structured output (records, tables, JSON) goes to stdout. This separation MUST hold so that `wrapper-mems show --json | jq` works without stderr contamination. Traces: D8, PRD-3 §2. --- ### §11 — Engine API (internal contract) REQ-SE-54 `internal/sync.Engine` MUST be constructable via: `sync.New(s *store.Store, adapters map[string]adapter.Adapter, opts sync.Options) *sync.Engine` where `sync.Options` MUST include at minimum: `DryRun bool`, `ProviderFilter []string`, `Max int`, `Verbose bool`, `MirrorEngram bool`, `Commit bool`. Traces: D10, explore open items. REQ-SE-55 `Engine` MUST expose methods callable without cobra: `Push(ctx context.Context) (PushResult, error)`, `Pull(ctx context.Context) (PullResult, error)`, `Sync(ctx context.Context) (SyncResult, error)`, `Status(ctx context.Context) (StatusResult, error)`. Traces: D10 (TUI seam for PRD-4). REQ-SE-56 `records_equal_ignoring_metadata(a, b canonical.Record) bool` in `internal/sync/equal.go` MUST compare ONLY: Title, Content, ContentFormat, Type, TopicKey, Tags (set equality, order-insensitive), Kind. It MUST NOT compare: Revision, Supersedes, LineULID, SchemaVersion, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, or any field in Origin. Traces: D5. Scenario — Tags order-insensitive: Given record A has Tags=["foo","bar"] and record B has Tags=["bar","foo"] When records_equal_ignoring_metadata(A, B) is called Then it returns true (assuming all other payload fields equal) Scenario — Revision change does not cause inequality: Given record A and B are identical except B has Revision=A.Revision+1 When records_equal_ignoring_metadata(A, B) is called Then it returns true --- ### §12 — Concurrency and Lockfile REQ-SE-57 The flock acquired by `store.Open` MUST be held for the entire duration of the sync run (single store.Open, single store.Close per CLI invocation — D4). It MUST NOT be released and re-acquired between push and pull phases. Traces: D4, PRD-3 §10 Q4. REQ-SE-58 No additional application-level lockfile is required beyond the flock managed by store.Open. The existing `.wrapper-mems/.lock` mechanism is sufficient for v1. Traces: D4, PRD-3 §10 Q4. --- ### §13 — go.mod / Build REQ-SE-59 `go.mod` MUST add `github.com/spf13/cobra` as a direct dependency. Bubbletea is NOT added in this change (PRD-4 scope). Traces: explore open items, D10. REQ-SE-60 The binary MUST be buildable via `go build ./cmd/wrapper-mems/...` with no external runtime dependencies beyond the Go stdlib and the dependencies already in go.mod (plus cobra). Traces: PRD-3 §2. --- ### §14 — store Additions (index.go + errors.go) REQ-SE-61 `internal/store/errors.go` MUST define sentinel errors: `ErrCorrupt`, `ErrSchemaMismatch`. Both MUST be usable with `errors.Is`. Traces: explore (store/errors.go). REQ-SE-62 `internal/store/index.go` `index` struct MUST be updated to include: `SyncState map[string]ProviderSyncState` and `Conflicts []ConflictEntry`. Both fields MUST be serialized to/from JSON. An absent field in existing index.json MUST deserialize as nil/empty without error (backward-compatible zero value). Traces: D2, PRD-3 §5, §7.2. REQ-SE-63 `store.UpdateSyncState(provider string, state ProviderSyncState) error` MUST: a. Acquire flock (if not already held by caller's store instance). b. Update the in-memory index. c. Persist index.json atomically (write temp file, rename). d. Release lock. Traces: D2. REQ-SE-64 `store.AppendConflict(c ConflictEntry) error` and `store.RemoveConflict(canonicalID string) error` MUST follow the same atomic-write pattern as REQ-SE-63. Traces: D2, PRD-3 §7.2. REQ-SE-65 `internal/store/rebuild.go` `rebuildFromFile` MUST be extended to detect `(canonical_id, revision)` collisions and populate `index.Conflicts`. The extension MUST NOT change the existing tiebreaker logic already implemented (lineULID tiebreak is correct — D1). Traces: D1, PRD-3 §7.1. --- ## Out of scope (v1) - TUI / bubbletea (PRD-4) - engram WriteNative update path (D9 — upstream limitation) - claude-mem write (ADR-10, PRD-2) - Batch pagination / streaming push beyond --max flag - Structured JSON output for all commands (only `show --json`) - Multi-machine sync_state merge - authorAttribution dedup refactor (common.go) - Delete propagation into providers (PRD-3 §10 Q1) - Cross-provider logical dedup (PRD-3 §10 Q3) - `wrapper-mems doctor` command (referenced in REQ-SE-45 but defined in a future PRD) - engram CLI version gate (D11) --- ## Traceability notes | REQ | PRD-3 section / D-decision | |-------------|--------------------------------------| | REQ-SE-01–05| §4, D3 | | REQ-SE-06–14| §4, D8, D10 | | REQ-SE-15–20| §5, D2, D4, D5 | | REQ-SE-21–27| §6.1, D5, D7, explore §risk-5 | | REQ-SE-28–30| §6.2, D9, ADR-10 | | REQ-SE-31–32| §6.3 | | REQ-SE-33–39| §7.1–7.3, D1, D2, D12 | | REQ-SE-40–43| §8, D11 | | REQ-SE-44–50| §9, D4, D6 | | REQ-SE-51–53| §9, D6, D8 | | REQ-SE-54–56| D5, D10, PRD-4 seam | | REQ-SE-57–58| D4, §10 Q4 | | REQ-SE-59–60| explore go.mod, D10 | | REQ-SE-61–65| explore store/errors.go, D1, D2, §5 | --- ## Spec-level assumptions / gaps forced by proposal open items 1. `ProviderSyncState` exact field set assumed minimal (last_pushed_at, last_pulled_at only). No cursor/native_cursor field in v1 — ListNative uses updated_at watermark directly. 2. `ConflictEntry.duplicates` field assumed to carry DuplicateRef array to support D12 dup_index; PRD-3 §7.2 only showed winner_line/loser_line. This is an additive assumption. 3. `store.RemoveConflict` method assumed (needed by resolve — REQ-SE-37e); not mentioned in proposal but implied. 4. `--commit` exact commit message format assumed as `"chore: sync [timestamp]"` — proposal did not specify. 5. `status --conflicts` table format assumed; not specified in proposal open items. 6. Pull watermark advancement on partial failure (REQ-SE-20 note) interprets PRD-3 §9 WriteNative row as "not advancing past the failed record's timestamp" — PRD text says "last_pulled_at is NOT advanced past the failed record's timestamp" exactly, so no assumption. 7. `wrapper-mems sync` outputs run summary to stdout — proposal listed this as open item for "status output format"; assumed stdout for summary, stderr for warnings.

title: Fixed Store.Rebuild discarding SyncState watermarks
content:
**What**: PR-A code review (obs #309) found Store.Rebuild() silently wiped SyncState — rebuildFromFile returns a fresh *index with SyncState=nil and Rebuild assigned it unconditionally. Fixed by carrying `newIdx.SyncState = s.idx.SyncState` forward BEFORE the persist call (persist writes the index, so the assignment must precede it, not follow the s.idx swap). Also documented the conflict-group sort's omission of the revision term (WARNING, conf 82).
**Why**: SyncState has no representation in memory.jsonl; rebuildFromFile cannot reconstruct it. Any provider watermark written by UpdateSyncState would be lost on the next Rebuild — would surface in PR-B/C once the sync engine writes watermarks.
**Where**: internal/store/store.go (Rebuild), internal/store/rebuild.go (sort comment), internal/store/syncstate_test.go (TestUpdateSyncState_SurvivesRebuild regression test). Commit f544cc6 on branch feat/sync-engine-pr-a.
**Learned**: Conflicts are file-authoritative so their loss on rebuild is correct; SyncState is NOT file-authoritative and must be preserved explicitly across rebuild.

title: Fixed PR-D sync-engine: nil transports, unreachable update-gap, push watermark
content:
**What**: PR-D code review found 3 issues, all fixed. (1) CRITICAL: buildSyncEngine in cmd/wrapper-mems/cmd/sync.go built adapters with nil transports — engram.Health() nil-panics on a nil commander, so `wrapper-mems sync` crashed at runtime; the headline feature was non-functional. Fixed by wiring engram.NewExecCommander()+engram.NewHTTPTransport() and claudemem.NewHTTPTransport(""). (2) WARNING: isEngramUpdateGap in pull.go was dead code — the generic ErrUnsupported guard caught the error first, so the D9 update-gap SKIP warning never fired; fixed by checking isEngramUpdateGap BEFORE the generic ErrUnsupported skip. (3) WARNING: push.go advanced maxUpdatedAt over every processed record including those a partial AppendBatch did not commit; moved maxUpdatedAt tracking into the committed loop so uncommitted records stay below the watermark and are retried.
**Why**: Fresh-context code review of PR-D (push/pull/mirror) of the sync-engine change (PRD-3).
**Where**: cmd/wrapper-mems/cmd/sync.go, internal/sync/pull.go, internal/sync/push.go. Branch feat/sync-engine-pr-d.
**Learned**: SupportedKinds() nil is correctly "all kinds" (len(nil)==0 bypasses the filter). engram WriteNative returns ErrUnsupported ONLY for the update gap (existing record), since relations are skipped earlier at FromCanonical — so isEngramUpdateGap's engram+ErrUnsupported check is sound once ordered first.

title: sdd/sync-engine/verify-report
content:
**What**: Verification of the sync-engine change (PRD-3), branch feat/sync-engine-pr-e (stacks PR-A..PR-E). Verdict: PASS.

## Build / test / vet
- `go build ./...` — clean
- `go vet ./...` — clean
- `go test ./...` — all 5 packages pass (store, sync, adapter/engram, adapter/claudemem, cmd/wrapper-mems/cmd), 0 FAIL
- Diff vs master: 44 files, +5042/-20

## Design adherence (D1–D12)
- D1 lineULID tiebreak — honored in rebuild.go (PR-A review confirmed)
- D2 SyncState/Conflicts in index.json, mutated only under flock — confirmed: UpdateSyncState/AppendConflict/RemoveConflict all hold s.mu.Lock()
- D5 payload equality — equal.go compares exactly Title, Content, ContentFormat, Type, TopicKey, Kind, Tags (Tags order-insensitive); all metadata ignored
- D6 exit codes 0/1/2 — exitCode helper + ExitCode exported, errConflicts→2 (PR-C review + fix)
- D9 engram update-gap SKIP — reachable after PR-D reorder fix (obs #95)
- D11 mirror 30s timeout — mirror.go uses exec.CommandContext with 30s default, warning-only
- D12 1-based dup_index — resolve.go + Engine.Resolve, off-by-one correct (PR-E review confirmed)

## Known review fixes — all landed
- Store.Rebuild preserves SyncState (obs #94) — confirmed newIdx.SyncState = s.idx.SyncState before persist
- Real adapter transports wired in sync.go (obs #95) — engram NewExecCommander+NewHTTPTransport, claude-mem NewHTTPTransport
- engram update-gap SKIP reachable; push watermark advances only over committed records

## Per-PR review history
- PR-A: 1 CRITICAL (Rebuild wiped SyncState) + 1 WARNING — fixed
- PR-B: no CRITICAL, 2 minor — fixed
- PR-C: 1 CRITICAL (exit code hardcoded) + 1 WARNING — fixed
- PR-D: 1 CRITICAL (nil transports) + 2 WARNING — fixed
- PR-E: clean, no issues

## Findings
- SUGGESTION (non-blocking): dry-run pull increments result.Pulled (reads as completed count); every dry-run output line is prefixed DRY-RUN so intent is clear. Left as-is by decision.

## Verdict: PASS
The change satisfies the spec (REQ-SE-01..65) and design (D1..D12). Implementation complete across 5 stacked PRs, each independently reviewed with all CRITICALs resolved and re-verified. Ready for sdd-archive.

Note: verification was performed inline by the orchestrator after the sdd-verify sub-agent hit a usage limit; build/test/vet were run directly and design decisions spot-checked in code.
