content: The SDD tasks phase for claude-mem-adapter (PRD-2) completed with a 4-PR stacked-to-main delivery strategy confirmed low risk per PR while maintaining strategic slice boundaries. PR#1 (~130 lines) establishes the Transport interface seam, httpTransport implementation with resolveBaseURL three-tier port resolution, and package skeleton with compile-time adapter conformance assertion. PR#2 (~200 lines) introduces the claudeMemRecord struct with all 8 fields marked PROVISIONAL and tagged with // PRE-MERGE GATE comments, copies authorAttribution and WrapIDMap helpers verbatim from engram adapter, implements tolerant multi-layout timestamp parsing (RFC3339Nano/RFC3339/SQLite), and delivers WriteNative (one-liner), FromCanonical (pure), ToCanonical (full field mapping with title derivation, timestamp normalization, IDMap lookup, revision bumping, forensic warn), plus unit tests with panic-on-call fakeTransport to prove purity. PR#3 (~230 lines, largest single slice) implements Health (Transport-delegated), ListNative (full pagination with 100-record pages, strict project_path equality filter, since timestamp filter, raw JSON forensic logging on first observation — line T-13 uses provisional project_id field), ReadNative (ADR-5 resilient 3-step: per-ID endpoint first, degrade to paginate+scan on ErrUnavailable, return ErrNotFound if exhausted), plus comprehensive unit tests covering all I/O paths and context cancellation. PR#4 (~90 lines) gates the entire implementation via T-16 integration test TestPreMergeGate_DumpFirstObservation and T-17 non-code checklist: the test MUST run against real claude-mem with ≥1 observation, log raw JSON, and a human must verify all PROVISIONAL field names match PRD-2 §5 before merge approval — this is a spec-level HARD gate (REQ-CM-20) that cannot be waived. Design decisions reflected include: T-10 sets Revision=1 always (read-only adapter avoids W-03 revision write-back trap), T-14 implements degrade-to-scan only on ErrUnavailable (not clean 404), T-13 filters strictly by project_path equality in-memory post-pagination. Critical risks documented: provisional field names unverified (mitigated by T-16/T-17 forensic gate), hasMore pagination field name unverified (ListNative infinite-loops if mismatch), per-ID endpoint may not exist (mitigated by ADR-5 degrade-to-scan). Optional collapse available: PR#1+PR#2 can merge as single ~330-line PR if preferred; PR#3 remains separate as it's the largest interdependent slice.

title: Claude-mem Adapter Tasks — 4-PR Delivery Plan with Pre-Merge Gate
content: The claude-mem adapter task plan (observation #78) commits to a 4-PR delivery strategy totaling ~750 lines, strategically split to keep each PR within the 400-line review budget while maintaining logical coherence. PR#1 establishes the package skeleton and Transport seam (interface, httpTransport implementation, resolveBaseURL three-tier port resolution, basic compile assertions and tests). PR#2 introduces all pure data-layer functions: the claudeMemRecord struct with all 8 fields marked PROVISIONAL and tagged with // PRE-MERGE GATE comments, reused helpers (authorAttribution, WrapIDMap, normalizeTimestamp, tolerant timestamp parser supporting RFC3339Nano/RFC3339/SQLite layouts), and complete implementations of WriteNative (single-line ErrUnsupported), FromCanonical (pure mapping), and ToCanonical (full REQ-CM-11 field mapping with title derivation, timestamp normalization, IDMap lookup, revision bumping, forensic warn on empty title+summary). PR#3 implements all I/O methods: Health (Transport-delegated), ListNative (paginated GET /api/observations with 100-record pages, strict project_path equality filter, since timestamp filter, raw JSON forensic logging on first observation), and ReadNative (resilient 3-step: try GET /api/observations/:id, degrade to paginate+scan if endpoint missing/errors, return ErrNotFound if exhausted — ADR-5 design for robustness against unverified endpoints). PR#4 gates the entire implementation behind a hard pre-merge checklist: integration test TestPreMergeGate_DumpFirstObservation must run against a real claude-mem instance with ≥1 observation and log raw JSON; a human reviews the logged JSON against PRD-2 §5 and reconfirms or corrects all PROVISIONAL field names (id, project_id, session_id, title, summary, tags, created_at, updated_at, hasMore); the test verifies timestamp format is parseable and normalizable to Z-suffix RFC3339Nano; and apply-progress artifact tracks 7 checklist items before merge approval. This pre-merge gate is a spec-level requirement (REQ-CM-20) — the provisional JSON shape cannot be verified without querying a live instance. Tolerant timestamp parsing (ADR-6) defends against DEFECT-LN-01's silent corruption of REQ-TS-03: three layout attempts (RFC3339Nano, RFC3339, SQLite 2006-01-02 15:04:05) before error, ensuring multi-source compatibility. All 17 tasks carry specific acceptance criteria (verifiable when); unit tests use fakeTransport that panics on any call to prove purity of ToCanonical/FromCanonical; integration tests skip cleanly under -short flag and behind //go:build integration or testing.Short().

title: Claude-mem Adapter (v1) Design Document Retrieved
content: The claude-mem adapter design (PRD-2, observation #77) establishes an HTTP-only read-only adapter mirroring the engram adapter structure but with critical divergences. The Transport interface seam handles all HTTP I/O; no Commander exists (claude-mem has no usable CLI enumeration). ReadNative uses resilient degradation: attempts GET /api/observations/:id for fast path (ADR-5), but if the endpoint is missing or errors with non-2xx, automatically degrades to full pagination+scan, ensuring robustness against unverified API endpoints. Timestamp parsing is defensively multi-format (ADR-6): tolerant of RFC3339Nano, RFC3339, and SQLite datetime format, each normalized to UTC RFC3339Nano with Z suffix to guarantee lexicographic byte-comparability (protecting REQ-TS-03, which corrupted DEFECT-LN-01). Port resolution (ADR-3) is three-tier with graceful fallback: explicit baseURL argument wins, then supervisor.json port reading, finally hardcoded 37701 — construction never fails on missing config file. To preserve the frozen adapter contract package (CON-01), two utilities are intentionally duplicated into claudemem: providerIDMapWrapper boundary cast and authorAttribution helper (ADR-7, ADR-8). ListNative paginates GET /api/observations with strict project_path equality filter and since timestamp filter (both post-pagination, in-memory). Revision is always set to 1 (simpler than engram's -1 sentinel) — the orchestrator layer (PRD-3) owns revision bumping on re-ingest. The integration test TestPreMergeGate_DumpFirstObservation logs the first observation's raw JSON to discharge the pre-merge gate, confirming field names match §5 before merge approval.

title: Engram Adapter Implementation — 3-PR Task Breakdown with 400-Line Budget Split
content: The engram adapter task breakdown (observation #63) establishes a 3-PR delivery strategy designed to manage the estimated 680-line implementation within the 400-line-per-PR review budget. PR#1 (store layer, ~210 lines) adds the ByProvider index field to track native-to-canonical ID mappings, implements the ProviderIDMap accessor with lazy reverse-map building, and creates the frozen adapter contract package (NativeID, CanonicalID, IDMap interface, Adapter interface with 7 methods, three sentinel errors). PR#2 (adapter implementation + unit tests, ~320 lines) implements EngramAdapter with Commander interface (for CLI: engram version, engram search, engram save) and Transport interface (for HTTP: GET /observations/:id fallback when CLI can't resolve). The pure methods ToCanonical/FromCanonical handle field mapping and timestamp normalization via one-pass time.Parse → Format(RFC3339Nano) to guarantee byte-comparable UTC timestamps (critical for REQ-TS-03 tiebreaker correctness, learned from DEFECT-LN-01). ReadNative uses resilient 3-step resolution: CLI search → HTTP fallback (daemon down recovery) → ErrNotFound; WriteNative checks for existing record before deciding update vs. create path. All 12 unit tests use fakes (no real binary required) covering idempotence, context cancellation, author attribution, timestamp normalization, scope filtering, relation handling. PR#3 (integration tests, ~150 lines) runs against real engram binary on PATH, skips cleanly under testing.Short() flag, and validates Health() and ListNative() against actual project state. Critical regression protection: S-09 rebuild test must run unconditionally (no testing.Short() guard) to catch IDMap repopulation bugs. Hard dependencies enforce order: PR#1 must land before PR#2 compiles; PR#2 must land before PR#3 compiles. Cross-cutting constraints preserve: store never imports adapter (CON-01 via ProviderIDMap returning concrete providerIDMap that structurally satisfies IDMap), adapter contract untouched (zero churn), relation handling stubbed with TODO comments pending v1.1.

title: Claude-mem Adapter (v1) Specification PRD-2 Retrieved
content: The claude-mem adapter specification (PRD-2, observation #76) establishes the read-only v1 implementation for wrapper-mems to consume observations from claude-mem over HTTP. The adapter must implement all 7 Adapter interface methods, with WriteNative explicitly unsupported for v1. Core features: full pagination via GET /api/observations with 100-record pages, strict project-path filtering for cross-project isolation, timestamp filtering using RFC3339Nano lexicographic comparison, and native-to-canonical record conversion using provisional field mappings (id→ProviderID, session_id→SessionID, title→Title, summary→Content, tags→Tags, created_at/updated_at→timestamps, all others mapped or defaulted). The specification includes a hard pre-merge gate (REQ-CM-20): implementation must NOT pass apply/merge until a real claude-mem instance with ≥1 observation has been queried, raw JSON logged and compared against §5, and all field names in REQ-CM-11 reconfirmed. This ensures production correctness before release. Timestamp normalization is critical (REQ-TS-03): all times must use rfc3339NanoFixed to ensure lexicographic ordering equals chronological ordering, preventing tiebreaker corruption in store/rebuild.go. IDMap integration tracks canonical identity; new records mint ULIDs on Append with Revision=1, known records increment Revision unconditionally even on backward updated_at (with warning log). Port resolution is three-tier: explicit baseURL wins, then supervisor.json port, then fallback 37701.

title: SDD design phase completed for claude-mem adapter (PRD-2)
content: The design phase completes PRD-2's specification-and-design lifecycle. The sub-agent digested the proposal, exploration, PRD-1 precedent, and formal spec, then made 11 explicit architecture decisions covering: package isolation (ADR-1), single Transport seam instead of dual Commander+Transport (ADR-2, Divergence D1), port discovery strategy (ADR-3), permissive struct parsing (ADR-4), ReadNative per-ID-with-degrade strategy (ADR-5, resolving explore Q2), tolerant timestamp parsing (ADR-6, resolving explore Q3 and applying DEFECT-LN-01 lesson), boundary wrapper duplication (ADR-7), authorAttribution duplication (ADR-8, resolving explore Q5), kind always session_summary (ADR-9, Divergence D4), write-unsupported (ADR-10, Divergence D2), and strict project filtering (ADR-11). The HARD pre-merge JSON-shape gate remains unresolved at design time — the design specifies the discovery mechanism (forensic INFO log of first observation raw JSON + empty-title/summary WARN) and the integration test that discharges the gate (TestPreMergeGate_DumpFirstObservation), but field mapping verification must happen against a live instance during implementation/review. Five risks are explicit: three JSON-shape/endpoint/format unknowns that degrade gracefully (skip+warn or fallback to scan), one intentional tech-debt code duplication, and one supervisor.json schema assumption that fails silently. The next phase (sdd-tasks) will decompose the spec + design into implementation tasks.

title: Claude-mem adapter design documented (PRD-2)
content: The design phase resolves all open questions from exploration (Q2: per-ID endpoint existence, Q3: timestamp format, Q5: authorAttribution strategy) and operationalizes the spec's pre-merge gate via integration testing. The core architecture is a faithful structural mirror of the engram adapter (same contract, same pure/I/O separation, same factory pattern) with three significant simplifications: (1) HTTP-only transport (no CLI path needed), (2) read-only implementation (WriteNative is one line), (3) always kind=session_summary (no type switching). The design emphasizes resilience against unverified endpoints and formats: GetByID with scan fallback survives missing-endpoint scenarios; tolerant multi-layout timestamp parsing handles format unknowns; port resolution gracefully degrades on supervisor.json errors. The boundary pattern (ADR-7, ADR-8) explicitly copies ~45 lines from engram rather than editing the frozen contract pkg, preserving CON-01 and keeping the contract pkg usable by future adapters without edit-churn. The pre-merge gate (TestPreMergeGate_DumpFirstObservation) is an enforceable integration test that logs the raw JSON bytes of the first observation decoded — this artifact is inspected during review to reconfirm the provisional field names in the claudeMemRecord struct against reality, blocking apply/merge if field names don't match the live API shape.

title: SDD spec phase completed for claude-mem adapter (PRD-2)
content: The SDD spec phase completes the requirements phase for PRD-2. The sub-agent read the proposal (obs #75), exploration (obs #74), and PRD-2 source document, then drafted 20 formal requirements grouped by interface method (Name, Health, ListNative, ReadNative, ToCanonical, FromCanonical, WriteNative) plus cross-cutting concerns (timestamp normalization, cross-project isolation, port resolution, forensic logging). Each requirement is sized for testability — e.g., REQ-CM-11 maps all 11 fields from ClaudeMemRecord to CanonicalRecord with explicit rules (title derive fallback, kind always session_summary, origin.provider always claude-mem). The 14 scenarios cover both happy paths (full pagination, project filter, IDMap reuse, revision bump) and error states (worker down, backward timestamps, empty fields). The critical pre-merge gate (REQ-CM-20) enforces that the JSON field names in §6 must be reconfirmed against a live instance before code can be merged—this blocks the silent failures that DEFECT-LN-01 created. The spec marks all 5 assumptions as TO-CONFIRM and documents them as explicit risks (field names, hasMore field, timestamp format, endpoint existence, project_path semantics) that design phase must resolve.

title: Claude-mem Adapter Specification (PRD-2) drafted
content: The specification document formalizes the design decisions from PRD-2 proposal (obs #75) into enforceable implementation requirements. The adapter must implement all 7 methods of the Adapter interface from PRD-1, operating in read-only mode for v1 (WriteNative always returns ErrUnsupported). The critical blocker is the unverified JSON shape of /api/observations responses — the spec makes this a hard pre-merge gate, requiring live-instance verification before code can be accepted. All timestamps must be normalized to RFC3339Nano UTC with Z suffix to maintain byte-comparable ordering needed by store.rebuild.go's tiebreakWinner function. The adapter reuses the providerIDMapWrapper boundary pattern from engram to keep internal/store free of adapter dependencies. Scenarios cover happy path conversion, pagination with project filtering, cross-project isolation, timestamp handling, revision tracking with backward-timestamp warnings, and port discovery.

title: PRD-2 Proposal: claude-mem Adapter (read-only v1)
content:
PRD-2 proposes implementing the claude-mem adapter as a read-only HTTP client feeding the canonical memory store. Unlike the engram adapter (which has a CLI primary path + HTTP fallback), claude-mem has no public write surface and no CLI enumeration, so the adapter is HTTP-only. The design reuses the entire PRD-1 contract (Adapter interface, ID aliases, sentinels, IDMap pattern) to ensure multi-provider federation is uniform.

Key architectural decisions: (1) Read-only in v1 because bidirectional HTTP API is unverified; (2) Kind always "session_summary" to keep it disjoint from atomic "observation" records per PRD-0 decision; (3) Strict project_path filtering because one claude-mem worker holds observations for all developer projects; (4) Permissive JSON parsing with forensic Raw capture to handle unverified API shape; (5) Hard blocker gate before merge: field names must be reconfirmed against a live instance.

The proposal documents three design-phase open questions (resolved via exploration findings): (a) if GET /api/observations/:id missing, ListNative calls Paginate+Scan fallback; (b) timestamp format must be probed and normalized per DEFECT-LN-01 pattern to protect REQ-TS-03 lex-tiebreak invariant; (c) authorAttribution logic either extracted to shared helper or duplicated (implementation choice).

Risks are low-to-medium: JSON shape is the primary blocker (mitigated by permissive + raw + forensic log + pre-merge gate), cross-project leakage is defensible (strict filter + WARNING log), worker unavailability is graceful (PRD-3 orchestrator degrades). Rollback is near-zero blast radius: delete internal/adapter/claudemem/ tree.

title: SDD Exploration phase completed for PRD-2 claude-mem adapter; artifact saved; next phase is proposal
content:
The sub-agent sdd-explore (Sonnet model) completed the exploration phase for PRD-2 claude-mem adapter, investigating the codebase, documenting reusable patterns from PRD-1, outlining requirements, and identifying risks. Execution took 2 minutes 6 seconds, consuming 48K tokens across 16 tool operations (primarily reads and engram searches).

**Artifact Status**: Exploration artifact saved to engram as `sdd/claude-mem-adapter/explore` (obs #74). This artifact documents current state, reusable patterns, affected files, approach options, risks, and recommendations for the proposal phase.

**Reusable from PRD-1**: Four direct-copy patterns identified: (1) `providerIDMapWrapper` boundary adapter for casting between plain-string store methods and named-type adapter interface, (2) `authorAttribution()` for origin.author resolution, (3) `normalizeTimestamp()` + `rfc3339NanoFixed` for byte-comparable timestamps, (4) Transport interface injection pattern for HTTP abstraction. No new dependencies needed (stdlib net/http and encoding/json sufficient).

**Structure Plan**: Four new files in `internal/adapter/claudemem/`: (1) `claudemem.go` with ClaudeMemRecord, ClaudeMemAdapter, all 7 interface methods, (2) `transport.go` with Transport interface and httpTransport, (3) `claudemem_test.go` with unit tests using fake transport, (4) `integration_test.go` for live worker testing (build-tag gated). Zero changes to existing code.

**Approach Options**: Three decisions documented:
- **A1 (Port discovery)**: Recommendation is `httpTransport` accepts `baseURL string` at construction; caller resolves from config.yaml or supervisor.json helper, falling back to `http://localhost:37701`. Defers config infrastructure to PRD-3 while keeping adapter testable.
- **A2 (JSON shape handling)**: Recommendation is permissive struct with `Raw json.RawMessage` field (exact PRD-2 §5 pattern). Enables forensic logging if field mapping assumptions prove wrong.
- **A3 (Project mapping)**: Single `project_path string` for v1 (simple, matches PRD). Can promote to list in v1.1 if monorepo support needed.

**Hard Pre-Merge Blocker**: The `/api/observations` item JSON field names (`id`, `project_id`, `session_id`, `title`, `summary`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) are assumptions based on claude-mem's documented model but unconfirmed against live API responses. The PRD notes test installation had zero observations, preventing real-world shape validation. Both `ToCanonical` field mapping and `ListNative` project-identity filtering depend on these field names being correct. Pre-merge gate: implementation cannot ship until shape is confirmed against a real claude-mem instance with at least one captured observation.

**Secondary Risks**: (1) `ReadNative` per-ID endpoint (`GET /api/observations/:id`) unconfirmed; fallback is full list scan if missing. (2) Timestamp format in `/api/observations` unknown; may repeat DEFECT-LN-01 (SQLite datetime vs RFC3339 confusion). (3) No config infrastructure yet; `base_url` and `project_path` config fields need a home (deferred to PRD-3). (4) Multi-project leakage if `project_id` filter is incorrect or missing.

**Next Phase**: Recommended transition is to `sdd-propose`. The proposal can fully specify the adapter design assuming PRD-2 §5 field names are correct, but must mark those names as provisional and call out the JSON shape confirmation as an explicit pre-implementation checkpoint.

**Skill Resolution**: none (explore is investigation-only; no project skills required for codebase reading).

title: SDD Exploration Phase for PRD-2 (claude-mem adapter) completed: architecture documented, risks identified, approach options presented
content:
User completed the exploration phase for PRD-2 (claude-mem adapter) and saved a comprehensive 400+ line exploration document to engram. The artifact synthesizes discoveries from codebase investigation and presents structured risk analysis and approach options.

**Reuse from PRD-1**: Four patterns can be copied verbatim from engram adapter: (1) `providerIDMapWrapper` boundary adapter for casting between plain-string `store.providerIDMap` and named-type `adapter.IDMap`, (2) `authorAttribution()` for `origin.author` field resolution (env var fallback to USER@hostname), (3) `normalizeTimestamp()` + `rfc3339NanoFixed` constant for `REQ-TS-03` byte-comparable invariant, (4) `Transport` interface pattern for HTTP abstraction and testability. No changes to existing code; pure reuse.

**Planned Structure**: New package `internal/adapter/claudemem/` with four files: (1) `claudemem.go` — `ClaudeMemRecord`, `ClaudeMemAdapter`, all 7 interface methods, (2) `transport.go` — `Transport` interface + `httpTransport` impl, (3) `claudemem_test.go` — unit tests with fake transport, (4) `integration_test.go` — integration tests against live worker (build-tag gated, requires TO-CONFIRM shape).

**Critical Blocker**: JSON shape of `GET /api/observations` items is unverified. PRD-2 §5 defines expected minimal fields (`id`, `project_id`, `session_id`, `title`, `summary`, `tags`, `created_at`, `updated_at`) but these are provisional. The actual response may use different field names (e.g., `observation_id` instead of `id`, `updated` instead of `updated_at`). This blocks both `ToCanonical` field mapping and `ListNative` filtering logic. Mitigation approach: define `ClaudeMemRecord` with permissive parsing (`json:",omitempty"` on optional fields) plus `Raw json.RawMessage` field for logging. Adapter must fail-open on field mismatches and log raw JSON of first observation encountered. **Pre-merge gate**: shape must be confirmed against live instance before implementation ships.

**Secondary Risks**: (1) Per-ID fetch endpoint (`GET /api/observations/:id`) unverified — PRD only confirms list endpoint; if missing, `ReadNative` must either re-paginate full list (expensive) or skip per-ID reads in v1. (2) Timestamp format unknown — could be RFC3339, Unix epoch, SQLite datetime; must be normalized like engram's export timestamps. (3) Project identity mapping not yet configured — no config infrastructure exists; adapter needs a way to match claude-mem `project_id` to wrapper-mems project scope.

**Approach Options Presented**: 
- **A1 (Port discovery)**: Hardcode 37701 (low effort, fragile) vs read supervisor.json at startup (low-medium effort, robust) vs config.yaml resolution (medium effort, requires config infrastructure). Recommendation: config-driven baseURL as primary, supervisor.json helper as resolver, caller passes resolved URL to httpTransport.
- **A2 (JSON parsing)**: Strict struct (simple, silent failures) vs permissive struct with RawMessage (tolerates unknowns, forensic logging). Recommendation: permissive + Raw field per PRD spec.
- **A3 (Project mapping)**: Single project_path string (v1 simple) vs list (supports monorepos, overkill for v1) vs dynamic /api/projects discovery (adds API shape risk). Recommendation: single string for v1.

**Questions for Proposal Phase**: (1) confirmed JSON shape, (2) per-ID endpoint existence, (3) timestamp format, (4) config infrastructure scope (PRD-2 or deferred to PRD-3), (5) helper function extraction (shared `internal/adapter` helper vs per-adapter duplication).

**Ready-for-Proposal Gate**: Yes, but with explicit pre-implementation checkpoint: the TO-CONFIRM JSON shape must be resolved before merging any code. The proposal can specify full design assuming PRD §5 shape is correct, but field names must be marked provisional and validated against live instance during explore phase conclusion.

title: Project structure: 28 Go files, no config infrastructure, lean dependencies (stdlib net/http sufficient)
content:
User enumerated codebase structure and checked for existing configuration infrastructure. Discoveries inform PRD-2 implementation strategy.

**Project Structure**: 28 Go files organized as three logical groups: (1) 21 store files for canonical JSONL storage, (2) 6 engram adapter files, (3) 1 CLI debug tool. No intermediate packages (e.g., orchestrator, config) yet — these are PRD-3 and beyond.

**Configuration Infrastructure**: Zero *.yaml files found. PRD-2 mentions .wrapper-mems/config.yaml for provider base URLs and timeouts, but this is not yet implemented. First PRD to need config will be responsible for creating the infrastructure.

**Dependencies**: Intentionally lean. Direct dependencies focus on core operations: xxhash (fingerprinting for index validation), gofrs/flock (advisory lock for single-writer enforcement), oklog/ulid (canonical ID generation), testify (assert/require in tests). No external HTTP client — stdlib net/http is used directly (engram's httpTransport pattern).

**Patterns**: Commander abstraction (exec.go) demonstrates how to abstract subprocess interactions for testability. Same pattern can be applied to HTTP: define a Transport interface, inject an httpTransport (stdlib *http.Client), inject a fake in tests. This is exactly what engram does.

**Schema Evolution**: decodeLine's two-pass decode (schema_version check before full unmarshal) ensures forward compatibility. Records with schema_version > StoreSupportedVersion are silently skipped during rebuild. This allows older wrappers to safely ignore lines written by newer versions.

**Implications for PRD-2**: (1) Use stdlib net/http (no new dependencies), (2) follow Commander/Transport abstraction pattern for testability, (3) config infrastructure will be created by the PRD that introduces config (PRD-3 or dedicated PRD-X), (4) ensure claude-mem adapter implements the exact same CanonicalRecord schema (kind, origin, timestamps, etc.) so records round-trip cleanly.

title: Codebase exploration: Adapter interface, engram reference impl, store ID mapping, and HTTP transport patterns
content:
User conducted systematic exploration of the codebase to understand architecture and reference implementations before starting PRD-2 (claude-mem adapter). Examined 8 key files spanning adapter contract, engram reference implementation, store infrastructure, and transport patterns.

Core discoveries:

**Adapter Contract** (adapter.go): Seven-method interface with purity contract separating I/O-free conversions (ToCanonical, FromCanonical) from I/O methods (Health, ListNative, ReadNative, WriteNative). Three sentinel errors (ErrUnsupported, ErrNotFound, ErrUnavailable) wrap all error returns. IDMap provides read-only bidirectional ID mapping for deduplication — satisfiable by store.providerIDMap without circular imports.

**Engram Reference Implementation** (engram.go, 513 lines): Uses HTTP Transport abstraction (two endpoints) instead of relying solely on CLI. DEFECT-LN-01 resolved by ListNative using GET /export for deterministic enumeration (engram CLI v1.15 rejects empty queries). Implements read strategy: CLI search first, HTTP fallback. Handles two timestamp formats (RFC3339Nano from CLI, SQLite datetime from export) with unified rfc3339NanoFixed normalization for byte-comparable string comparison (W-02 invariant). Personal-scope and relation records defensively filtered. ToCanonical returns kind=observation; ReadNative supports optional fallback to HTTP.

**Store Infrastructure** (store.go, index.go): Append-only JSONL with in-memory index and advisory lock. computeAppendFields handles all store-managed fields (canonical_id, line_ulid, revision, supersedes). ProviderIDMap() returns read-only snapshot of per-provider native→canonical ID mapping (used for deduplication and idempotence checks). index.ByProvider added in PRD-1 to track all provider_native_id→canonical_id bindings.

**Boundary Adaptation** (W-01 pattern in engram.go): providerIDMapWrapper casts between plain-string methods (store.providerIDMap) and named types (adapter.IDMap), keeping internal/store free of internal/adapter imports. Critical for dependency isolation.

**Transport Patterns** (transport.go): HTTP abstraction with GetByID (single record) and Export (full dump) injected at construction; defaults to http://127.0.0.1:7437. exportObservation uses SQLite datetime format (space-separated, no T/Z); parseExportTimestamp normalizes to rfc3339NanoFixed.

For PRD-2, this demonstrates: (1) HTTP is the transport pattern for read-heavy providers, (2) timestamp normalization is critical for determinism, (3) boundary adapters isolate dependencies, (4) defensive filtering of out-of-scope records is required, (5) Transport abstraction enables testing without daemons.

title: Skill registry available with 11 indexed skills for wrapper-mems delegated workflows
content:
User accessed the skill registry to understand available delegation points for PRD-2 SDD workflow. The registry file `.atl/skill-registry.md` is an index maintained by gentle-ai and maps 11 user skills to exact SKILL.md paths for delegating subagent work.

The sdd-explore skill contract was loaded to understand how the exploration phase will be orchestrated. Key insight: sdd-explore is a sub-agent-only skill (orchestrator gate prevents inline execution). It requires the calling orchestrator to delegate via platform primitives (task, sub-agent invocation). The skill defines a 6-step exploration workflow: (1) load skills, (2) understand request, (3) investigate codebase, (4) analyze options, (5) persist artifact, (6) return structured analysis.

Artifact persistence follows conditional logic: engram mode stores to sdd/{change-name}/explore with type=architecture; openspec and hybrid modes add filesystem artifacts per openspec-convention.md; none mode returns analysis only without persistence.

For PRD-2, the registry provides access to go-testing (for Bubbletea teatest patterns), issue-creation (for logging blocking items), and chained-pr (for review-slice delivery strategy).

title: PRD-2 SDD kickoff: claude-mem read-only adapter, confirmed configuration and hard blocker
content:
User initiated PRD-2 (claude-mem adapter) work by confirming SDD configuration matching PRD-0/PRD-1 established patterns. The PRD document (prds/PRD-2-claude-mem-adapter.md) is in draft status and defines a read-only v1 adapter that imports claude-mem observations via HTTP into the canonical JSONL store.

Key architectural decision: claude-mem is read-only because the CLI lacks write/enumeration commands and HTTP write surface is unverified. HTTP endpoint `/api/observations` provides paginated access (unlike engram's FTS5 CLI). The adapter maps claude-mem observations to canonical kind=session_summary (not observation) to keep the two streams visibly disjoint — claude-mem summaries are compressed narratives of entire sessions, not atomic facts like engram observations.

A blocking dependency exists: the JSON schema of `/api/observations` items is documented as "expected minimal viable shape" in §5 but unverified in practice (test installation had zero observations). Before implementation can be merged, the adapter must be run against a real claude-mem worker with captured sessions, the first observation's raw JSON must be logged, and the field mapping in §6 must be confirmed against that reality.

Deferred to v1.1: bidirectional sync (writing canonical observations back into claude-mem). Pure FromCanonical function will be implemented in v1 anyway to validate round-trip losslessness and avoid rework when bidirectional lands.

SDD artifacts (spec, design, tasks, verification reports) will be stored in engram-only per established pattern. Delivery strategy is ask-on-risk (escalate architectural conflicts to user for decision).

title: W-03: ToCanonical backward updated_at warning impossible to implement at adapter layer
content: The spec requirement to log a warning when updated_at moves backward cannot be satisfied at the adapter layer without architectural redesign. The adapter's ToCanonical method must be pure (REQ-AC-04) to remain testable without provider calls. To detect backward timestamps, the adapter would need either (1) the existing record's timestamp passed in the IDMap, (2) a separate read of the store before calling ToCanonical, or (3) passage of prior revision data. None of these are part of the current contract. The orchestrator (PRD-3) layer owns the overall versioning and could detect this by comparing incoming updated_at to the store's existing record, but PRD-1 adapter contract does not include this responsibility. The implementation correctly implements the sentinel (revision=-1 for existing mapping) and tests verify no error is returned (S-20 passing), but the warning requirement is unmet. This is documented as a spec gap requiring resolution at PRD-3 design time (orchestrator responsibility) rather than a PRD-1 defect.

title: Adapter contract + engram adapter implementation complete with DEFECT-LN-01 resolved
content: The adapter-contract-engram change (PRD-1) ships a stable provider interface enabling multiple adapters to feed the canonical store without coupling store to any specific provider. The work spans 4 stacked feature branches implementing 16 tasks across store IDMap extension, adapter contract definition, engram reference adapter, and integration tests. DEFECT-LN-01 (empty search query rejection by engram CLI v1.15) was diagnosed through real API probing and resolved by switching ListNative to deterministic GET /export enumeration with client-side filtering. All timestamps normalized to RFC3339Nano UTC with 9-digit nanosecond format to maintain byte-comparable lex-ordering invariant required by conflict resolution in rebuild (tiebreakWinner at rebuild.go:32). Adapter layer is pure (ToCanonical/FromCanonical do no I/O); only ListNative/ReadNative/WriteNative/Health touch provider. IDMap is stored in index.json as ByProvider[provider][nativeID]=canonicalID; rebuild automatically repopulates on stale/corrupt index, preventing silent wipeout during recovery. Engram adapter wraps CLI (primary) + HTTP fallback (secondary ReadNative path) with pluggable Commander/Transport seams for test injection. Author attribution sourced from WRAPPER_MEMS_AUTHOR env or USER@hostname. Relations and tags deferred to v1.1 with TODO comments marking no-op branches. Objective verification passed (go build/vet/test all green, integration tests against real daemon with 33 observations retrieved for wrapper-mems project, personal-scope filtering confirmed, timestamp boundary edge case verified).

title: DEFECT-LN-01: ListNative enumeration fixed via HTTP GET /export deterministic path
content: DEFECT-LN-01 surfaced during PR#3 integration testing when ListNative called \\\"engram search \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\" and received rejection. Root cause analysis through real engram API (v1.15.12 at localhost:7437) revealed FTS-only search constraint — both CLI and HTTP endpoints require non-empty query. The sole deterministic enumeration path is GET /export returning full memory dump. Design decision made to reuse Transport seam with new Export operation, fetch the dump, and filter client-side by project + scope + updated_at. This trades transient cross-project data in process memory for deterministic enumeration without redesigning engram upstream. PR#4 implemented full reimplementation of ListNative: Transport.Export returns exportResponse with flat observations array; each observation timestamp in SQLite format (\\\"2006-01-02 15:04:05\\\", UTC) is parsed via parseExportTimestamp and normalized to rfc3339NanoFixed using time.Parse + time.Format. The W-02 boundary regression is confirmed fixed: previous truncation of nanoseconds at zero (time.RFC3339Nano → \\\"...Z\\\") would exclude boundary records from incremental syncs due to lex-ordering; fixed code always formats 9 digits including trailing zeros. Personal-scope filtering applied defensively at line 249 even though export scope filtering should prevent them — REQ-AC-07 compliance verified. Cross-project filtering present; warning logged if export returns >= 1000 records (pagination unsupported in v1). All integration tests now active against real daemon.

title: PRD-1 adapter-contract-engram implementation complete across 4 stacked PRs
content: PRD-1 adapter-contract-engram has completed full implementation and objective verification. The work spans 4 chained feature branches introducing store-provider-agnostic adapter contracts with HTTP fallback transport, RFC3339 timestamp handling fixes, and ListNative export capabilities. All 16 implementation tasks closed with inline verification showing clean builds, passing test suites, and successful integration against live daemon. One critical defect (DEFECT-LN-01) surfaced during integration testing and resolved via Transport.Export migration. Remaining work is final fresh-context specification verification across all 31 requirements and 21 scenarios to validate no scope bleed between implementation slices, then archive to engram memory store with cumulative verify report.

title: ListNative Export-Based Implementation Verified with Comprehensive Integration Tests
content: The engram adapter's ListNative method successfully uses the Export endpoint to retrieve records from the engram daemon, replacing an earlier CLI-based search approach (DEFECT-LN-01 fix). Integration tests comprehensively validate the implementation across multiple scenarios: personal scope filtering ensures user-scoped records are correctly excluded, boundary conditions verify the since-filter with whole-second precision, cross-project filtering confirms data isolation, and daemon unavailability is properly handled with ErrUnavailable. The ToCanonical conversion consistently maps all engram records to the "markdown" content format, which is the standard for engram-sourced entries.

title: Architectural Constraint CON-01 Verified: Store Layer Isolation
content: The codebase maintains a critical architectural constraint (CON-01) ensuring the store layer remains independent from the adapter layer, preventing circular dependencies and maintaining clean separation of concerns. The feat/adapter-contract-pr4 branch passes all verification checks: successful compilation, static analysis, and test suite. The engram adapter implementation includes iterative refinements on the ListNative operation, replacing a previous implementation with an Export-based approach and adding comprehensive integration tests against a real engram binary. The branch stack shows deliberate, incremental development with test updates following each implementation change.

title: TODO comments and S-20 backward-timestamp detection confirmed: pragmatic constraints documented
content: All deferred features and pragmatic constraints are properly documented. Relations (kind=relation records) are skipped with explicit TODO(v1.1) marker at both entry/exit points (ToCanonical/FromCanonical), signaling that v1.1 will handle engram relation objects as a separate feature. Tags are deferred with separate TODO comments indicating the gap is in upstream engram CLI, not the adapter. The S-20 backward-timestamp detection limitation is explicitly acknowledged: the IDMap interface carries ID mappings only; detecting whether a timestamp moved backward would require reading the existing canonical record, which violates the purity contract (REQ-AC-04, REQ-AC-05). The -1 revision sentinel is the pragmatic resolution: it signals "existing mapping found" without requiring a store read. The design comment clearly explains why this approach was chosen over alternatives. Test skip behavior confirms CON-04 compliance: integration tests (which require engram binary on PATH) cleanly skip under -short mode, preserving CI ability to run fast unit test feedback without daemon dependencies.

title: Verification complete: commit hygiene, scope bleed, rawRecord Origin field, ContentFormat consistency all confirmed
content: Final verification confirms all housekeeping details are correct. Commits follow conventional semantic versioning format without AI attribution — each commit is traceable and human-authored with clear intent. PR#4 scope is tight: only the four engram adapter files touched (transport, engram, and their tests), no unintended changes to store or contract. The rebuild mechanism for ByProvider is complete: rawRecord struct exposes Origin with Provider and ProviderID fields; rebuild.go accesses these during the JSONL scan to populate the ByProvider mapping, ensuring that stale-index recovery (S-09) repopulates the mapping correctly. ContentFormat consistency is enforced at three levels: search-result converter, HTTP unmarshalling, and ToCanonical hardcoding, guaranteeing that all records reaching the canonical store have ContentFormat="markdown" regardless of input source. No further verification needed — the feature is complete, tested, and ready for handoff.

title: W-02 Timestamp Boundary Bug: rfc3339NanoFixed enforces lex-comparable whole-second alignment
content: The W-02 bug is a subtle timestamp format inconsistency that breaks the REQ-TS-03 byte-comparable invariant used for incremental syncs. When ListNative filters by updated_at >= since, it must ensure both operands are in identical format. Export returns SQLite datetime format ("2006-01-02 15:04:05"); the adapter normalizes to rfc3339NanoFixed. However, the prior buggy code formatted sinceStr using time.RFC3339Nano, which truncates trailing zero nanoseconds (e.g., "2026-05-16T10:00:00Z" for a whole-second time). The normalized export timestamp includes full nanoseconds ("2026-05-16T10:00:00.000000000Z"). Lexicographically, "...00Z" sorts before "...00.000000000Z" (because '.' < 'Z' in ASCII is false; actually '.' = 46 < 'Z' = 90), so the boundary record is excluded from results (false positive for >= check). The fix changes sinceStr formatting to use rfc3339NanoFixed, ensuring all timestamps are byte-comparable with 9-digit nanoseconds. Test TestListNative_SinceBoundary_WholeSecond_Included verifies the fix: boundary record is included, before-boundary is excluded. This is a regression guard for incremental sync correctness.

title: Final verification: 100+ tests pass; build/vet/CON-01 clean; adapter-contract-engram complete
content: The final verification confirms that the adapter-contract-engram feature is fully implemented, tested, and verified across all 4 PR branches. Build and vet are clean with no errors or warnings. The store package has no dependency on the adapter package (CON-01 constraint satisfied). The full test suite passes with 100+ tests: 75 store tests covering all core lifecycle scenarios (bootstrap, append, tombstone, rebuild, conflict resolution, partial line recovery, schema versioning, index persistence, ByProvider IDMap survival), 25+ adapter engram tests covering purity contracts, scope filtering, HTTP error mapping, idempotence, timestamp normalization, author attribution, guards for personal-scope and relation records, revision tracking with -1 sentinel, and the W-01 boundary wrapper. Integration tests (5 tests) exercise the real engram binary and HTTP daemon with proper skip guards: skipIfNoBinary for binary-dependent tests, skipIfDaemonDown for HTTP-dependent ListNative tests. All tests use uncached run (count=1) to ensure no stale results. No test failures, no blocking issues. The feature is ready for archive and handoff to PRD-3 orchestrator (PRD-3 will handle revision minting, orchestration, and higher-level context).

title: DEFECT-LN-01 Resolution: ListNative reimplemented over GET /export deterministic enumeration
content: DEFECT-LN-01 was discovered during PR#3 integration testing: ListNative's empty search query ("") was rejected by engram CLI v1.15 with exit status 1. Investigation revealed no project-scoped enumeration endpoint exists in the HTTP API either (GET /search requires non-empty query). The only deterministic enumeration path is GET /export, which returns all observations across all projects. Decision: ListNative calls Transport.Export instead of CLI, filters client-side by project + scope + updated_at. Export timestamps use SQLite datetime format ("2006-01-02 15:04:05" UTC, no T/Z separator). Adapter normalizes via parseExportTimestamp helper to rfc3339NanoFixed for byte-comparable >= since string comparison (REQ-TS-03 invariant). Tests updated: fakeTransport.Export added with buildExportBody helper; unit test TestListNative_SinceBoundary_WholeSecond_Included verifies whole-second boundary inclusion (W-02 regression guard). Integration tests rewritten with skipIfDaemonDown probe (GET /health, 2s timeout); S-18 ListNative tests now PASS against real daemon, confirming 33 project-scope observations in wrapper-mems project. The tradeoff (export dumps entire user memory, filters in-process) is explicitly accepted as the only v1 path forward until engram adds scoped enumeration upstream.

title: Adapter contract + Store IDMap extension + Engram adapter implementation (PR#1-4 complete)
content: The adapter-contract-engram feature is fully implemented across 4 stacked PRs. PR#1 extends the store with ByProvider mapping (provider → native → canonical ID) and persists it in index.json with backward-compatible omitempty tag; the adapter contract defines NativeID/CanonicalID/IDMap/Adapter types and 3 sentinel errors. PR#2 implements the engram adapter: Commander encapsulates exec.CommandContext for testing; Transport abstracts HTTP to :7437; ToCanonical and FromCanonical are provably pure (unit tests with panicCommander/panicTransport confirm no I/O); personal-scope filtering in ListNative (REQ-AC-07) and defensive double-filter; revision tracking uses -1 sentinel because adapter cannot read store (purity constraint). The WrapIDMap boundary wrapper (W-01 resolution) adapts store's plain-string providerIDMap to the adapter's named-type IDMap interface without importing internal/adapter in store (CON-01 intact). PR#3 adds integration tests with testing.Short() guards. PR#4 resolves DEFECT-LN-01 (ListNative empty-query rejection) by reimplementing over GET /export with client-side project/scope/since filtering; tests updated to use SQLite datetime format with rfc3339NanoFixed normalization for lex-comparable since boundaries (W-02 regression guard: whole-second timestamps now align). All 4 PRs pass full test suite (75 store tests + 20 adapter unit tests + 5 integration tests). The design is binding-decision-compliant: string RFC3339Nano timestamps throughout, HTTP fallback for ReadNative only (not ListNative after PR#4), relations deferred to v1.1 with TODO comments, tags round-trip deferred with TODO comments.

title: Export endpoint schema verified against real engram v1 daemon: observations flat array, SQLite datetime format, all-projects enumeration
content: The investigation into engram v1.15.12 HTTP API discovered that GET /export is the sole deterministic enumeration endpoint. The endpoint returns a full dump of all user observations across all projects in a flat array at the top level (not nested under sessions as initially assumed). Timestamps are in SQLite datetime format ("2006-01-02 15:04:05" UTC), differing from the RFC3339Nano format used by CLI search output. This discovery drove the DEFECT-LN-01 resolution: ListNative must call Transport.Export and filter client-side because no project-scoped enumeration exists. The unscoped export is the tradeoff accepted for deterministic behavior in v1, pending upstream engram enhancements.

title: ListNative reimplemented: GET /export with client-side filtering fixes DEFECT-LN-01
content: ListNative was completely reimplemented to fix DEFECT-LN-01 by switching from the CLI `engram search ""` path (which rejected empty queries) to the HTTP GET /export endpoint. The new implementation fetches the full export dump containing all observations across all projects, then filters client-side by: 1) exact project match, 2) rejection of personal-scope records, 3) byte-comparable updated_at >= since timestamp filtering. A critical addition is timestamp normalization: export timestamps use SQLite datetime format ("2006-01-02 15:04:05" with no timezone), requiring conversion to rfc3339NanoFixed format to match canonical records and maintain the W-02 invariant (whole-second boundaries must be byte-comparable). The implementation now uses sync_id (confirmed empirically from API testing) as the stable NativeID, not the integer id field. Transport is now mandatory for ListNative, raising ErrUnsupported if nil.

title: httpTransport.Export method implemented: GET /export with raw JSON response
content: The httpTransport.Export method implements the HTTP side of the DEFECT-LN-01 fix by fetching the full export dump from GET /export and returning it as raw JSON bytes. ListNative will parse this export and filter by project + scope + updated_at to enumerate observations. The implementation follows the same error-handling pattern as GetByID: context deadline/cancellation propagates, connection errors and non-2xx responses wrap to ErrUnavailable. The method includes comprehensive documentation of the /export response structure (derived from empirical API testing) including all observation fields and the specific timestamp format used by the API.

title: Transport interface extended with Export method for DEFECT-LN-01 fix
content: The user began implementation of the DEFECT-LN-01 fix by extending the Transport interface with an Export method. This adds the HTTP GET /export endpoint to the transport seam, enabling ListNative to fetch the full observation export dump and perform client-side filtering by project + scope + updated_at. This resolves the blocking issue where ListNative called `engram search ""` (empty query), which the CLI rejects with "search query is required". By using the unscoped /export endpoint instead, ListNative becomes deterministic and functional. The Transport seam now abstracts two HTTP operations: GetByID for ReadNative fallback, and Export for ListNative enumeration.

title: engram /export timestamps use YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format (no timezone, not RFC3339)
content: Empirical testing confirms that engram /export API returns timestamps in a simple YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS format with no timezone annotation and no nanosecond precision. This differs materially from the RFC3339Nano format required by the canonical memory schema (REQ-TS-01). The adapter's normalizeTimestamp function must handle this format explicitly — `time.Parse(time.RFC3339, ts)` will fail on these timestamps. Additionally, topic_key field is confirmed present in the /export output (contrary to some prior uncertainty), enabling proper topic classification during import. The id field is an integer sequence (used for some internal indexing), while sync_id is the stable string identifier that should be mapped to CanonicalRecord.Origin.ProviderID. No personal-scope observations appear in /export — the user-level export naturally filters to project scope.

title: engram /export API detailed structure: 67 observations with sync_id, revision_count, duplicate_count fields
content: Empirical testing of the real engram HTTP /export endpoint (running on :7437) revealed the complete observation record structure. The observations are served in a top-level array with 67 records present. Each record includes standard fields (title, content, type, project, scope) plus metadata: revision_count and duplicate_count (used for deduplication tracking), last_seen_at (timestamp of most recent encounter), and a sync_id field that appears to be the canonical engram identifier. The timestamps in the /export response use a different format (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS without timezone) compared to the RFC3339Nano format used in canonical memory records, indicating a normalization step is required in the adapter during import.

title: engram /export API structure verified: observations at root level, not nested under sessions
content: The user empirically tested the real engram HTTP /export endpoint by curling http://127.0.0.1:7437/export and inspecting the JSON structure. The test revealed that the observations are not nested under session objects as the adapter design (obs #61) had assumed. Instead, the API returns a flat top-level "observations" array alongside a separate "sessions" array. Sessions contain only metadata (id, project, directory, started_at) without nested observations. This structural difference has implications for the ListNative implementation that was designed to parse sessions and extract observations from within them. The discovery confirms that empirical API testing is necessary before finalizing implementation — assumptions from the PRD can be incorrect without real-world verification.

title: ListNative adapter uses HTTP GET /export with client-side filtering
content: Empirical probing of the real engram v1.15.12 HTTP API (daemon on :7437) revealed that the FTS5 `/search` endpoint cannot enumerate project observations — it requires a non-empty query and broad tokens are non-deterministic. No project-scoped list endpoint exists over HTTP. The only deterministic enumeration path is the unscoped `/export` endpoint, which returns a full JSON dump of all sessions and observations across all projects. ListNative will use this endpoint via the existing HTTP Transport seam, parse the response in-process, and filter by project + scope + updated_at. This approach reuses an already-approved transport seam and maps daemon-down conditions to the existing ErrUnavailable error. The tradeoff — loading all project memories to enumerate one project — is accepted as the only viable path in engram v1.15, with the caveat that it will be revisited if engram adds a scoped list endpoint upstream. This decision was user-confirmed and supersedes prior design assumptions that rested on false premises about API capabilities.

title: engram HTTP API has /observations POST endpoint and auxiliary routes (stats, context, export, conflicts)
content: Comprehensive HTTP endpoint probing reveals the engram daemon exposes a broader API surface than initially assumed. The /observations endpoint, which returned 405 with GET/POST in earlier tests, is actually restricted to POST only (confirmed by OPTIONS response). This suggests it may accept JSON request bodies for operations like list/filter. The API also provides auxiliary endpoints: /stats for aggregate counts, /context for session summaries, /export for bulk data export (the approach rejected in DEFECT-LN-01 due to cross-project concerns), /timeline for per-observation history, and /conflicts for pending sync conflicts. The daemon reports version 0.1.0, which is distinct from the CLI v1.15.12, indicating the HTTP and CLI may have diverged in features or behavior. The /projects endpoint is not implemented. This expanded API surface suggests the POST /observations endpoint is the intended route for ListNative enumeration, not the /search endpoint.

title: engram HTTP /search cannot enumerate without a query term; FTS5 syntax constraints apply
content: Testing confirms that the engram HTTP API /search endpoint has the same enumeration limitation as the CLI: there is no query pattern that returns all observations in a project without a search term. Empty strings, wildcards, and whitespace all fail with errors or return null. The underlying search uses SQLite FTS5 (full-text search), which has strict syntax requirements and rejects incomplete or special-character-only queries. Legitimate queries (e.g., q=a) succeed and respect filtering (project parameter) and pagination (limit parameter). This means the HTTP transport cannot work around the original DEFECT-LN-01 by using a different query strategy — the enumeration problem exists at both the CLI and HTTP API layers. The ListNative implementation will need to document this limitation or find a different architectural path (e.g., requiring a search term, using a different engram endpoint if available, or accepting incomplete enumeration).

title: engram HTTP API endpoint shape: /search is the query interface, /observations returns 405
content: HTTP API probing against running engram daemon (v1.15.12 on :7437) reveals the actual shape of the HTTP transport interface. The /search endpoint is the primary query interface, accepting a required 'q' parameter and optional 'project' parameter for filtering. Results are returned as JSON arrays of observation objects containing id, sync_id, session_id, type, title, and content fields. The /observations endpoint exists but returns Method Not Allowed (405) for all HTTP methods, suggesting it is not yet implemented or is intended for a different access pattern. The /health endpoint is available for daemon status checks. The /api, /memories, and /memories/* paths are not implemented in this version. This confirms that ListNative enumeration must use the /search endpoint with an empty 'q' parameter if the CLI limitation is worked around at the HTTP layer, or must iterate through filtered results if server-side "list all" behavior is not supported.

title: DEFECT-LN-01: ListNative adapter moves from engram CLI search to HTTP transport
content: PR#3 integration testing against the real engram binary (v1.15.12) revealed DEFECT-LN-01: the `engram search` CLI command rejects empty queries, and there is no CLI approach that reliably lists all project-scoped observations. This contradicts assumptions in PRD-1 §5.1 and design #61 that assumed `engram search "" --project P` would work. The decision moves `ListNative` from the CLI `search` codepath to the engram HTTP transport layer (via `engram serve` listening on :7437), reusing the Transport seam interface already approved for v1. The HTTP transport is the only path that deterministically lists all project observations. CLI `search` is retained for ReadNative by-ID lookups, which work correctly with concrete queries. This change updates the Transport interface with a list/search operation, and impacts files in internal/adapter/engram (transport.go, engram.go, test variants), as well as amending design #61 and PRD-1 §5.1. The discovery that unit-test fakes returned canned data for any query explains why the empty-query limitation was hidden until real-binary integration testing.

title: Engram CLI v1.15 has no all-records list mode; search is the only enumeration mechanism and requires non-empty query
content: Analysis of engram CLI v1.15 help output confirms that the API does not support fetching all observations for a project in a single deterministic call. The `search` command is the primary enumeration mechanism, but it requires a non-empty query string. There is no documented wildcard pattern, no list-all flag, and no project-filtered export. This is a fundamental gap between the adapter specification (which assumes ListNative can fetch all observations) and the actual CLI design. The fix is not a one-line change to ListNative but requires either (a) negotiating with engram maintainers to add an all-records mode in a future version, (b) changing the ListNative contract to accept a search term parameter, or (c) redesigning the PRD-3 orchestrator to not rely on full enumeration. DEFECT-LN-01 is correctly categorized as a spec-to-implementation mismatch, not a bug in the code.

title: SDD apply phase complete for adapter-contract-engram (PRD-1); all 12 tasks across PR#1/PR#2/PR#3 finalized
content: The SDD apply phase for adapter-contract-engram is complete. All implementation work is done across three stacked PRs. PR#1 established the store IDMap extension and adapter contract foundation. PR#2 implemented the engram reference adapter with proper separation of concerns (Commander interface for CLI, Transport interface for HTTP fallback). PR#3 added integration tests against the real engram binary with proper guardrails (testing.Short() and exec.LookPath). A critical defect (DEFECT-LN-01) was discovered during integration testing: the specification calls for `engram search ""` to fetch all observations, but engram CLI v1.15 rejects empty queries. The implementation is correct per the (incorrect) specification; the fix belongs in production code, not test code, and is intentionally isolated from this PR slice to maintain scope discipline. All tests pass or skip gracefully; the suite is green. Next step is the verify phase to formally document the discovery and assess readiness for archive.

title: DEFECT-LN-01 formalized and tracked: ListNative incompatible with engram CLI v1.15 empty-query limitation
content: DEFECT-LN-01 is now formally documented and tracked in the project memory system. The defect represents a fundamental incompatibility between the specification (which calls for `engram search ""` to fetch all observations) and the actual engram CLI v1.15 behavior (which rejects empty queries). Integration testing revealed that even single-character queries like "a" only return partial results (20 observations in wrapper-mems project), not a complete list. A proper resolution requires either (a) asking engram maintainers to implement a list-all mode or wildcard query, or (b) redesigning ListNative to use paginated search if that's available, or (c) changing the PRD-3 orchestrator approach to not rely on fetching all observations in a single call. The defect is not a critical blocker for PR#1/PR#2/PR#3 completion (those are done), but it is a blocker for PRD-3 implementation. Kept separate from the chained-PR work to maintain scope discipline and allow each PR to be independently stable.

title: PR#3 committed with all integration tests; chained-PR work complete for adapter-contract-engram (PRD-1)
content: The chained-PR work for adapter-contract-engram (PRD-1) is now complete. All 12 tasks across three stacked branches (PR#1: store IDMap extension + adapter contract; PR#2: engram adapter implementation + unit tests; PR#3: integration tests against real engram binary) have been implemented, tested, and committed. PR#1 extends the store with ByProvider tracking and defines the stable adapter interface contract. PR#2 implements the engram reference adapter with CLI-primary and HTTP-fallback strategies, 20 unit tests, and fixes for W-02 timestamp formatting. PR#3 adds integration tests with real-binary execution, guarded properly per CON-04. DEFECT-LN-01 (ListNative empty-query incompatibility with engram v1.15) is documented and tracked for a separate follow-up fix, not bundled into the current PR scope. The test suite is green: all unit tests pass, integration tests for Health are verified, and ListNative tests gracefully skip with documented reason. CON-01 (store does not import adapter) is verified. Work is ready for review or archival.

title: PR#3 integration tests complete and passing; Health verified, ListNative gracefully deferred via DEFECT-LN-01
content: PR#3 integration tests are now complete and the test suite is green. Health() functionality is fully verified: engram version command succeeds (Health PASS), and context cancellation is properly handled. ListNative tests gracefully skip when the real engram binary rejects the empty search query, with clear documentation of DEFECT-LN-01. The error wrapping shows the adapter correctly propagates engram CLI failures as adapter.ErrUnavailable. This approach maintains test discipline: the tests are correct per specification and will pass once DEFECT-LN-01 is fixed in the production code (engram.go ListNative method). No test failures; suite is stable and ready for archival with DEFECT-LN-01 tracked as a known limitation.

title: Integration tests refactored to gracefully handle ListNative empty-query defect (DEFECT-LN-01)
content: The integration tests are now resilient to the ListNative empty-query defect. Rather than fail when ListNative encounters the known issue (engram CLI v1.15 rejecting ""), tests skip with a clear message referencing DEFECT-LN-01. This approach keeps the test suite passing while the production code defect is tracked separately for resolution. The tests are written correctly per the specification; they will pass without modification once the production code is fixed to use a non-empty wildcard query or alternative approach. This follows the chained-PR discipline: each PR slice must be independently stable, and cross-cutting defects belong in a separate issue, not bundled into the current slice.

title: Engram CLI search requires non-empty, FTS-compatible query; no all-records mode available in v1.15
content: The specification's approach of using an empty search string is fundamentally incompatible with engram's CLI design. Engram v1.15 uses SQLite FTS5 for search, which requires a valid, non-empty, syntactically-correct query. There is no "list all" mode or all-records query pattern. Single-character or common-word queries (like "a") return results but are arbitrary and not guaranteed to match all observations (they only match records containing those characters/words). Using `engram search "a"` returns 20 observations for wrapper-mems, but this is likely incomplete if the total observation count exceeds 20 or if some observations don't contain the letter "a". This is a fundamental blocker: ListNative cannot be implemented with the current engram CLI v1.15 in a way that reliably fetches all observations. A workaround must be negotiated (e.g., asking engram maintainers for an all-records mode, or redesigning the sync strategy in PRD-3 to NOT require fetching all observations in one call).

title: Engram CLI search requires non-empty query; ListNative spec incompatible with engram v1.15 behavior
content: The specification was written or reviewed without testing the actual engram CLI v1.15 behavior. The empty-string search query documented in REQ-ENG-11 does not work; engram CLI requires a valid non-empty search pattern. A working alternative must be identified (wildcard `"*"`, space `" "`, or a different flag like `--all`) and ListNative must be updated accordingly. This is a blocking issue for PR#3 integration testing. The unit tests pass because they use a fakeCommander; integration tests will fail immediately when executing the real engram search command.

title: Engram CLI rejects empty search query; ListNative implementation incompatible with engram v1.15
content: While unit tests pass because they use a fakeCommander that returns canned JSON responses, the ListNative implementation will fail at runtime when called against a real engram binary. The specification calls for `engram search ""` with an empty search string, but the actual engram CLI v1.15.12 rejects this with "search query is required". This is a spec-to-implementation mismatch that must be resolved before PR#3 integration tests can run successfully. The fix likely involves using a wildcard query (e.g., `"*"`) or checking engram CLI v1.15 documentation for the correct all-records search syntax. This blocks PR#3 completion.

title: PR#3 integration tests created and begin real engram binary testing
content: PR#3 begins integration testing by separating the real-binary tests from PR#2's unit tests. All integration tests follow the CON-04 constraint by calling skipIfNoBinary() at the top, which enforces testing.Short() skipping and requires engram on PATH. The realAdapter() helper constructs an EngramAdapter using production components (NewExecCommander and NewHTTPTransport), exercising the full exec boundary. Tests validate Health() probes the engram daemon, ListNative() returns real observations from the wrapper-mems project, and personal-scope filtering is defensive (spot-checking the first returned ID via ReadNative to prove it is a project-scope record). Since filtering is validated with a far-future timestamp, expecting zero results. The engram binary is confirmed present at v1.15.12, unblocking real integration testing.

title: Adapter contract + store IDMap extension (PR#1) complete and tested
content: PR#1 establishes the foundation for adapter integration by extending the store's index structure to track provider-to-canonical ID mappings and defining the stable adapter contract. The `ByProvider` field uses omitempty to preserve backward compatibility with existing index.json files created before this change. The rebuild repopulation logic is regression-critical: it ensures that any stale-index recovery (triggered by fingerprint mismatch or index corruption) produces a complete ByProvider mapping fresh from the JSONL, with no merge of stale state. The adapter contract package provides stable interfaces for all future adapters, with a dependency arrow pointing only from adapter implementations toward store, not the reverse (CON-01).

title: Engram adapter (PR#2) complete with 20 unit tests and real daemon fallback
content: PR#2 implements the first concrete adapter for engram observations. The adapter bridges engram's CLI and optional HTTP daemon to the canonical store schema. ToCanonical maps engram fields to canonical records with a revision=-1 sentinel (orchestrator PRD-3 owns final revision increment) and author attribution. Personal-scope filtering is enforced at ListNative entry point (not delegated to caller). The HTTP transport is optional and used only as a fallback for ReadNative when CLI cannot locate a record by ID; connection errors wrap to ErrUnavailable to distinguish provider unavailability from actual not-found. WriteNative is idempotent on provider_id by pre-checking; v1 limitation is documented (returns ErrUnsupported if update would be needed, since engram CLI v1.15 has no update command). All timestamp fields use RFC3339Nano (string), ensuring byte-comparable ordering required by rebuild.go's tiebreakWinner logic.

title: Fixed W-02: ListNative since-boundary records silently dropped due to RFC3339Nano truncation
content: The adapter's ListNative method formats the caller's `since` parameter (time.Time) to a string for comparing against normalized record timestamps. The Go stdlib's `time.RFC3339Nano` constant has built-in zero-omission (e.g., `2026-05-16T10:00:00Z` for a whole-second time), while engram records are normalized to always include 9 digits of nanoseconds (`.000000000`). This mismatch caused lexicographic filtering to drop records at exact-second boundaries. The project already uses a fixed 9-digit format constant (`rfc3339NanoFixed`) elsewhere to protect the rebuild.go tiebreakWinner invariant; the same constant must be used here for consistency. This is a correctness defect caught during verify phase before merge.

title: Revision=-1 sentinel for prior-mapping case in ToCanonical (pragmatic response to spec-interface mismatch)
content: The specification's scenario S-19 assumes ToCanonical can produce the final incremented revision (4) based only on a native record and an IDMap interface. However, the IDMap interface was defined without revision metadata, and reading the store to fetch current revision would break the purity contract required by REQ-AC-04. This is a spec inconsistency: either (a) the IDMap interface must carry revision, or (b) the orchestrator (PRD-3) owns revision increment, or (c) ToCanonical must be allowed to read the store (breaking purity). The implementation chose (b): use a sentinel (-1) to signal the orchestrator that a prior mapping was found, and let PRD-3 handle the final increment. This preserves purity, is explicit and detectable, and does not corrupt revision chains. The design is sound; the spec requires updating to reflect the actual interface contract.

title: W-02 Fix Documented in Memory: Architecture Decision Record Saved (obs-37f992f77980591d)
content: The W-02 bugfix has been formally documented in memory as an architecture decision record. The record explains the root cause (time.RFC3339Nano truncation breaking byte-comparability), the impact (silent data loss in incremental syncs at exact-second boundaries), the fix (consistent use of rfc3339NanoFixed), and the prevention (regression test). Critically, it documents the learned lesson: all timestamp string comparisons in the codebase must use the fixed 9-digit format because the truncation behavior is incompatible with lexicographic ordering. This protects future maintenance from re-introducing the same class of bug in other code paths.

title: W-02 Fix Committed to feat/adapter-contract-pr2: Commit 138d917
content: The W-02 bug fix has been formally committed to the PR#2 branch with a comprehensive commit message. The message documents the root cause (RFC3339Nano truncation of trailing zeros), the impact (boundary records silently dropped from incremental syncs due to lexicographic string comparison), the solution (use rfc3339NanoFixed constant), and the regression prevention (new test). The commit includes both the code fix (sinceStr format change + detailed inline comment) and the regression test that exercises the boundary case. This completes the W-02 warning resolution as identified during verification.

title: W-02 Fix and Regression Test Verified: Build Clean, All Tests Pass
content: The W-02 fix and regression test are verified complete and working. The sinceStr format change from time.RFC3339Nano to rfc3339NanoFixed, combined with a detailed comment explaining the bug, has been deployed. The new regression test TestListNative_SinceBoundary_WholeSecond_Included specifically exercises the boundary case (whole-second since parameter matched against 9-digit nanosecond records) and verifies the fix prevents the bug. All 96 tests in the combined suite pass. The build and static analysis (go vet) are clean. No regressions were introduced. The W-02 warning is now resolved with both a code fix and regression coverage.

title: W-02 Regression Test Added: TestListNative_SinceBoundary_WholeSecond_Included
content: The W-02 bug has been addressed with both a code fix and a regression test. The new test exercises the exact scenario that would expose the bug: a whole-second `since` parameter (e.g., from an incremental sync's last-seen timestamp) matched against records with 9-digit nanosecond precision. The test verifies that records updated exactly at the since boundary are correctly included (>= semantics), not dropped. The test includes a detailed comment documenting the W-02 bug: time.RFC3339Nano truncates trailing zeros, causing boundary timestamp mismatches in string comparison. This regression test prevents future changes from reintroducing the bug and serves as documentation for why rfc3339NanoFixed must always be used for timestamp comparisons in this codebase.

title: W-02 Fix Confirmed In Place; rfc3339NanoFixed Now Used In ListNative SinceStr
content: The W-02 bug fix is confirmed deployed in the live code. The ListNative sinceStr formatting now uses rfc3339NanoFixed, which ensures all timestamp comparisons operate on consistently-formatted strings (always 9-digit nanoseconds). The existing test TestListNative_PersonalScopeFiltered does not exercise the boundary case that would expose the bug (it uses a zero time.Time as since), but the fix is correct and comprehensive. The comment added to the code explains the subtle lexicographic ordering issue clearly for future maintainers. The fix is minimal (one-line change) and addresses exactly the issue identified during verification.

title: Fixed W-02: ListNative SinceStr Format Bug with Comprehensive Comment
content: The W-02 warning identified during verification—that ListNative incorrectly filters records at exact since boundaries—has been fixed in the live code. The change replaces the Go standard time.RFC3339Nano format (which truncates trailing zero nanoseconds) with the custom rfc3339NanoFixed constant (which preserves 9-digit nanosecond precision). This ensures the since-filter comparison operates on consistently-formatted timestamps. The fix includes a comprehensive comment documenting the bug: when a caller passes a whole-second since time (e.g., the last-seen timestamp from an incremental sync), RFC3339Nano formats it as "2026-05-16T10:00:00Z" (no nanoseconds), but engram returns normalized "2026-05-16T10:00:00.000000000Z". The string comparison "...00.000000000Z" < "...00Z" evaluates true (because dot sorts before Z), causing records at the exact boundary to be wrongly excluded from the result. The fix is now deployed and the comment warns future maintainers of this subtle but critical behavior.

title: Independent Adversarial Verification of PR#2 Confirms: PASS WITH WARNINGS
content: An independent adversarial sdd-verify agent conducted a fresh-context review of PR#2 without relying on the primary session's observations. The agent performed independent code inspection, ran all tests themselves, verified CON-01 dependency isolation, adjudicated the revision=-1 sentinel design, and traced timestamp normalization behavior. The findings align precisely with the primary session's independent discovery work: PASS WITH WARNINGS with 4 WARNINGs (W-01 resolved, W-02 sinceStr format, W-03 backward log, W-04 spec drift). The subagent's key adjudication: revision=-1 is an acceptable, documented sentinel response to the fact that IDMap carries no revision metadata; the spec scenario S-19 was written with an unstated assumption incompatible with the IDMap interface contract (REQ-AC-08). This is classified as a SPEC GAP, not a code defect—no PR#2 fix needed; orchestrator (PRD-3) owns revision assignment. The sinceStr bug (W-02) is the only actionable fix (single-line change) and can be deferred to follow-up or PR#3. The independent verification confirms the findings are real and the assessment is sound.

title: PR#1 and PR#2 Verification Complete: PASS WITH WARNINGS
content: The combined PR#1 and PR#2 verification is complete with a PASS WITH WARNINGS verdict. All 95 unit tests pass (20 engram adapter + 75 store), the build system reports no errors (clean go build and go vet), and dependency isolation is verified (CON-01 satisfied). W-01 interface type mismatch from PR#1 is resolved in PR#2 by the WrapIDMap boundary wrapper.
